## PAGE 00291: (2026x) Creating parts

- page_id: `254422351`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254422351/2026x+Creating+parts
- version_number: 5
- version_date: `2025-12-03T08:53:48.226+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Structure > (2026x) Parts
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A part can represent any level of abstraction, different kinds of system components, or a physical component. A system is modeled as a composite part, and its part usages may themselves have further composite structure. A part definition defines a class of part objects, and a part usage is a usage of part or item definitions. Parts may have attributes, ports, and other elements, as well as perform actions or exhibit states.

##### Creating part usage and definition elements

###### Creating part usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor

To create part usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the keyword:
  1. *part def*for a part definition.
  2. *part* for a part usage.
2. Specify the element name.
3. (Optional) Specify the part's features in the element body.
4. Click the Synchronize button.

partdefFlashlightParts; 
partflashlightSwitch:FlashlightParts;

###### Creating part usage and definition elements via the Create Element command

To create part usage and definition elements via the Create Element command

1. In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click **Create Element**.
2. In the Create Element dialog, select one of the following:
  1. part def to create a part definition element.
  2. part to create a part usage element.
3. Name the element.
4. (Optional) Specify the part's features.

###### Creating part usage and definition elements via the view palette

To create part usage and definition elements via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Items/Ports/Attributes group, click one of the following:
  1. The **part** def button to create a part definition element.
  2. The **part** button to create a part usage element. If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to either the **part**or the **part def**button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.
2. Click in the view pane where you want to create the part symbol.
3. (Optional) Specify the part's features.

###### Creating part usage elements via the symbol compartment in a view

For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Compartments' space=''] page.

To create part usage elements via the symbol compartment in a view

- Do one of the following:
  - Select an element symbol and click the Create Element button on it. In the dropdown menu, click the part element.
  - Select an element symbol and on the right side of the parts compartment, click the Create part button. The new element is created and displayed in the parts compartment.

###### Creating part usage elements via the Specification panel

To create part usage elements via the Specification panel

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification panel' space=''].
2. Do one of the following:
  1. In the Properties section bar, click the **Create Element** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The shortcut menu appears. Click the **part**element in the shortcut menu.
  2. In the **parts**compartment section's bar, click the **Create part**[IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The element is created in the **parts** compartment.

###### Auto-creating part definition elements via the Extract Definition command

To auto-create part definition elements via the Extract Definition command

- See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Extract Definition and Usage' space='']page.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254423031 space=SYSML2P version=6 -->
## PAGE 00292: (2026x) Creating requirements

- page_id: `254423031`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254423031/2026x+Creating+requirements
- version_number: 6
- version_date: `2025-12-17T15:16:52.314+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Requirements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A requirement is a kind of constraint that specifies stakeholder-imposed constraints that a design solution must satisfy to be a valid solution. There are both requirement definition and requirement usage elements, where a requirement usage is a usage of a requirement definition in some context. The context for multiple requirements can be provided by a package, a part, or another requirement.

- The informal text of a requirement is given by [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Annotations' space=''] comments in the body of a requirement definition or usage.
- If a requirement definition or usage is declared with a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Element names' space=''] , this is also considered its requirement ID .

##### Creating requirement usage and definition elements

###### Creating requirement usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor

To create requirement usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the keyword:
  1. *requirement def*for a requirement definition.
  2. *requirement* for a requirement usage.
2. Specify the element name.
3. (Optional) [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Subjects, actors, and stakeholders for requirements' space=''] the requirement in the element body.
4. Click the Synchronize button.

requirementdefMassRequirement{ 
subjectvehicle:Vehicle; 
doc/* The actual mass shall be less than or equal to the required mass. */ 
attributemassRequired:MassValue; 
}

requirement<'1'>fullVehicleMassLimit:MassRequirement{ 
subjectvehicle:Vehicle; 
attribute:>>massRequired=2000[kilogram]; 
}

###### Creating requirement usage and definition elements via the Create Element command

To create requirement usage and definition elements via the Create Element command

1. In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click **Create Element**.
2. In the Create Element dialog, select one of the following:
  1. requirement def to create a requirement definition element.
  2. requirement to create a requirement usage element.
3. Name the element.
4. (Optional) [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Subjects, actors, and stakeholders for requirements' space=''] .

###### Creating requirement usage definition elements via the view palette

To create requirement usage and definition elements via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Requirements/Constraints group, click one of the following:
  1. The **requirement** def button to create a requirement definition element.
  2. The **requirement** button to create a requirement usage element. If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to either the **requirement**or the **requirement def**button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.
2. Click in the view pane where you want to create the requirement symbol.
3. (Optional) [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Subjects, actors, and stakeholders for requirements' space=''] .

###### Auto-creating requirement definition elements via the Extract Definition command

To auto-create requirement definition elements via the Extract Definition command

- See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Extract Definition and Usage' space='']page.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254422613 space=SYSML2P version=5 -->
## PAGE 00293: (2026x) Creating states

- page_id: `254422613`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254422613/2026x+Creating+states
- version_number: 5
- version_date: `2025-12-03T10:34:13.637+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Behavior > (2026x) States
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

State definitions and usages are used to describe the system’s state-based behavior, where the execution of any particular state is triggered by events. A state usage applies a state definition in various contexts, enabling the reuse of state definitions across different parts of the system.

##### Creating state usage and definition elements

###### Creating state usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor

To create state usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the keyword:
  1. *state def*for a state definition.
  2. *state* for a state usage.
2. Specify the element name .
3. (Optional) Specify the state's features in the element body.
4. Click the Synchronize button.

statedefVehicleStates{ 
inoperatingVehicle:Vehicle; 
incontroller:VehicleController; 
} 
statevehicleStates:VehicleStates;

###### Creating state usage and definition elements via the Create Element command

To create state usage and definition elements via the Create Element command

1. In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click **Create Element**.
2. In the Create Element submenu, select one of the following:
  1. state def to create a state definition element.
  2. state to create a state usage element.
3. Name the element.
4. (Optional) Specify the state's features.

###### Creating state usage and definition elements via the view palette

To create state usage and definition elements via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the States group, click one of the following:
  1. The **state** def button to create a state definition element.
  2. The **state** button to create a state usage element. If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to either the **state**or the **state def**button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.
2. Click in the view pane where you want to create the state symbol.
3. (Optional) Specify the state's features.

###### Auto-creating state definition elements via the Extract Definition command

To auto-create state definition elements via the Extract Definition command

- See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Extract Definition and Usage' space='']page.

###### Auto-creating state usage elements via the Extract Usage command

To auto-create state usage elements via the Extract Usage command

- See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Extract Definition and Usage' space='']page.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

definition elements</h3><h4 class="auto-cursor-target">Creating state usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To create state usage and definition elements via the Textual name</span>.</li><li>(Optional) Specify the state's features in the element body. </li><li>Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong>button.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="30044d15-f229-4626-9e04-2b2739874b7d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">state</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">VehicleStates</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">in</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">operatingVehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Vehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">in</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">controller</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">VehicleController</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">state</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">vehicleStates</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">VehicleStates</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Creating state usage and definition elements via the Create Element command</h4><p>To create state usage and definition elements via the Create Element command</p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click <strong>Create Element</strong>.</p></li><li>In the Create Element submenu, select one of the following:<ol><li><strong>state def</strong> to create a state definition element.</li><li><strong>state </strong>to create a state usage element.</li></ol></li><li>Name the element.</li><li>(Optional) Specify the state's features.</li></ol><h4 class="auto-cursor-target">Creating state usage and definition elements via the view palette</h4><p>To create state usage and definition elements via the view palette</p><hr /><ol><li>In the view palette, under the States group, click one of the following:<ol><li>The<span> <strong>state </strong></span><strong>def </strong>button to create a state definition element.</li><li>The<span> <strong>state</strong></span> button to create a state usage element.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="769c3a3a-7b27-471d-b151-165748c95ab6"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to either the <strong>state </strong>or the <strong>state def </strong>button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></li><li>Click in the view pane where you want to create the state symbol.</li><li>(Optional) Specify the state's features.</li></ol><h4>Auto-creating state definition elements via the Extract Definition command</h4><p><span>To auto-create state definition elements via the Extract Definition command</span></p><hr /><ul><li><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Extract Definition and Usage" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Extract Definition and Usage]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">page.</span></span></li></ul><h4>Auto-c<span>reating state usage elements via the Extract Usage command</span></h4><p><span>To auto-create state usage elements via the Extract Usage command</span></p><hr /><ul><li><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Extract Definition and Usage" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Extract Definition and Usage]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">page.</span></span></li></ul>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254422934 space=SYSML2P version=3 -->
## PAGE 00294: (2026x) Creating style sheet components

- page_id: `254422934`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254422934/2026x+Creating+style+sheet+components
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-12-03T10:37:33.714+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Views > (2026x) Symbol styles > (2026x) Custom style sheets
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

See the procedures on this page to create style sheet components.

#### Creating custom style sheet components

##### Creating global conditions for a style sheet

In addition to specifying conditions for individual rules in the style sheet (see procedure ), you can specify a global condition that applies to the entire style sheet. All individual rules declared in the style sheet apply only to the symbols that comply with the global rule.

###### Creating global conditions for a style sheet

To create global conditions for a style sheet

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating style sheets' space=''] .
2. Within the body of the part definition of the new style sheet, redefine the *styleSheetCondition*element inherited from the StyleSheet element and define it by the needed condition.
3. Within the body of the condition, declare a ref item , then r edefine the needed item and specify its values. The available ref items and their value types depend on the condition definition you are using. For a list of the condition definitions and their available ref items, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables' space=''] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables' space='']page.
4. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Synchronizing custom style sheet modifications' space='']. // the following style sheet contains a global condition 'General View Condition' followed by an individual rule for a specific element metaclass and applicable stylepackageNewStylesPackage{privateimportDS_Styles::**;privateimportCoreStylesComponents::*; 
 privateimportPredicates::*; 
 privateimportColorDefinitions::*; 
 privateimportCompartmentVisibilityDefinitions::*; 
 privateimportKerMLStyles::*; 
 privateimportSysMLStyles::*; 
partdefNewStyleSheet:>StyleSheet{ 
/* -------global condition -------*/ 
part'General View Condition':>>styleSheetCondition:ByDiagramTypeCondition{ // global style sheet condition targeting symbols by view definition 
refitem:>>diagramType=DS_Views::SymbolicViews::gv.metadata; // the condition targets views defined by the *General View*view definition 
} 
/* -----------------------------------*/ 
partpartRule:>rule{ // an individual rule targets *PartUsage* metaclasses and applies the specified style to them. Because of the global rule, only part usages displayed on a General View will be affected by the rule 
part:>>condition:ByElementMetaclassCondition{ 
refitem:>>elementMetaclass= SysML::Systems::PartUsage.metadata; 
} 
view:ShapeStyle:>>style{ 
attribute:>>textColordefault"#2A16FF"; 
} 
} 
} 
}

##### Creating custom rules

###### Creating custom rules

To create a custom rule

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating style sheets' space=''] .
2. Within the body of the part definition of the new style sheet, s pecify the rule, d eclare a part usage, specify a name for the new rule, subset the rule element inherited from the StyleSheet element, and in its body, do any of the following as needed:
  1. .
  2. .
3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Synchronizing custom style sheet modifications' space='']. packageNewStylesPackage{privateimportDS_Styles::**;privateimportCoreStylesComponents::*; 
 privateimportPredicates::*; 
 privateimportColorDefinitions::*; 
 privateimportCompartmentVisibilityDefinitions::*; 
 privateimportKerMLStyles::*; 
 privateimportSysMLStyles::*; 
partdefNewStyleSheet:> StyleSheet{/* -------new rule -------*/ 
partpartRule:>rule{// Specify the rule's condition 
 // Specify the rule's style 
}/* -----------------------------------*/ 
} 
}

##### Specifying conditions for a rule

###### Specifying conditions for a rule

To specify a condition for a rule

1. Within the body of the part element of the rule, declare a part usage and r edefine the condition element inherited from the StyleSheet element, then define it by the needed condition.
2. Within the body of the condition, declare a ref item , then r edefine the needed item and specify its values.
  1. For the FreeFormCondition , declare a calc and redefine test , then specify the needed expression. The available ref items and their value types depend on the condition definition you are using. For a list of the condition definitions and their available ref items, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables' space=''] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables' space='']page.
3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Synchronizing custom style sheet modifications' space='']. // *ByElementMetaclassCondition* examplepartpartRule:>rule{ 
part:>>condition:ByElementMetaclassCondition{// the rule's condition is specified as *ByElementMetaclassCondition*, targeting symbols by element metaclass 
refitem:>>elementMetaclass= SysML::Systems::PartUsage.metadata; // the condition targets elements with the *PartUsage* metaclasses 
} 
}// *ByMetadataAnnotationCondition*examplepart metadataApprovalRule :> rule {part :>> condition : ByMetadataAnnotationCondition {// the rule's condition is specified as *ByMetadataAnnotationCondition*, targeting symbols by element metadata 
 ref item :>> annotatingMetadataType = 'Vehicle Metadata Definitions'::Approval.metadata;// the condition targets elements with the applied *Approval* metadata 
 }}// *ByDiagramTypeCondition*examplepart generalViewRule :> rule {part :>> condition : ByDiagramTypeCondition {// the rule's condition is specified as *ByDiagramTypeCondition*, targeting symbols by view definition 
ref item :>> diagramType = DS_Views::SymbolicViews::gv.metadata; // the condition targets views defined by the *General View*view definition 
 }}// *FreeFormCondition*examplepartMyFreeformRule:>rule{ 
part:>>condition:FreeFormCondition{ // the rule's condition is specified as *FreeFormCondition*, targeting symbols by the specified expression 
calc:>>test{ 
'VCCU View'.metadata==diagram// targets element symbols on the specific view '*VCCU View*' 
 // ORelement== VCCU.metadata// targets the *VCCU* element symbol on all views (to which the style sheet is applied)} 
} 
}

##### Specifying styles for a rule

There are two ways you can specify a style for a rule:

- Local specification allows you to specify the style of a specific rule only. See the procedure .
- External specification allows you to create a custom style definition outside the rule itself, which can then be reused for style specification of different rules. See the procedure .

###### Specifying style attributes locally for the style of a rule

To specify style attributes locally for the style of a rule

1. Within the body of the part element of the rule, after the condition specification, declare a view usage, define it by the needed style definition, and redefine the style element inherited from the StyleSheet element.
2. Within the body of the view element, d eclare an attribute usage, redefine the needed attribute and specify its value. The available attributes depend on the style definitions (primary or custom)used for the view's definition:For a list of primary style definitions, their attributes, and value types, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables' space=''] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables' space='']page.To create custom style definitions, see the procedure.To define by multiple style definitions, list them in a comma-separated list.
3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Synchronizing custom style sheet modifications' space=''].

part partRule :> rule {part:>>condition:ByElementMetaclassCondition{ 
refitem:>>elementMetaclass= SysML::Systems::PartUsage.metadata; 
} 
view:ShapeStyle:>>style{ // the rule's style is defined by a primary style definition *StyleShape* 
attribute:>>textColordefault"#FF0F37";// local modification of an available attribute's value 
} 
}

###### Hiding style attributes from the Symbol Style dialog for specific element kinds

To hide style attributes from the Symbol Style dialog for specific element kinds

1. Within the body of the part element of the rule, after the condition specification, declare a view usage, define it by the needed style definition, and redefine the style element inherited from the StyleSheet element.
2. Within the body of the view element, d eclare an attribute usage and apply the InvisibleInDialog metadata to it, then redefine the style attribute you want to hide from the Symbol Style dialog for the element kind specified in the rule's condition . The available attributes depend on the style definitions (primary or custom)used for the view's definition.For a list of primary style definitions and their attributes, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables' space=''] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables' space='']page.For more information on the Symbol Style dialog, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Modifying styles of individual symbols' space=''] page.
3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Synchronizing custom style sheet modifications' space=''].

part partRule :> rule {part:>>condition:ByElementMetaclassCondition{ 
refitem:>>elementMetaclass= SysML::Systems::PartUsage.metadata; 
} 
view:ShapeStyle:>>style{ 
#InvisibleInDialogattribute:>>contentFillColor; // the style attribute *Content Fill Color* is hidden from the Symbol Style dialog for part usages} 
}

###### Creating custom style definitions

Unlike local attribute specification, creating custom style definitions allows you to reuse them for style specification of multiple rules.

You can create custom style definitions specifying the needed attributes and their values. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Custom style sheets' space=''] must subclassify [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Custom style sheets' space=''] (such as *ShapeStyle, ShapeOnBorderStyle*, etc.), inheriting their attributes, which you can modify. However, you can only add attributes that are available to the primary style definition that the custom style definition subclassifies. 
For an extensive list of primary style definitions and their available attributes, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables' space=''] on the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables' space=''] page.

To create a custom style definition

1. Within the body of the part definition of the new style sheet, d eclare a view definition element, specify its name, and subclassify a primary style definition from the DS_Styles::CoreStylesComponents::KerMLStyles package. For a list of primary style definitions, their attributes, and value types, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables' space=''] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables' space='']page.
2. Within the body of the view definition element, d eclare an attribute usage, r edefine the needed attribute and specify the attribute's value. The available attributes and their value types depend on the primary style definition you are using.For an extensive list of the primary style definitions and their available attributes, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables' space=''] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables' space='']page.
3. Specify the custom style definition for the style of a rule.
4. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Synchronizing custom style sheet modifications' space=''].

partdefNewStyleSheet:>StyleSheet{ 
partpartRule:>rule{ 
part:>>condition:ByElementMetaclassCondition{ // custom rule 
ref item :>> elementMetaclass = SysML::Systems::PartUsage.metadata; 
} 
view : PartTextStyleRule :>> style; // the style of the rule is updated to be defined by the new custom style definition 
} 
viewdefPartTextStyleRule:>ShapeStyle{ // new custom style definition subclassifies the primary style definition *ShapeStyle* 
attribute :>> textColor default "#68FF70"; 
} 
}

###### Creating custom reusable style attribute values

You can create custom style attributes whose values you can reuse by referencing them where needed. Additionally, you can create custom style classes grouping such attributes.

To create custom reusable style attribute values

1. Declare an attribute, s pecify its name, then define it by the needed value type, and s pecify its value.
2. (Optional) To group attributes within a custom style class, declare a part usage, specify its name, then place the attributes within the part's body.
3. Reuse the newly created style attribute values for or . Use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing textual notation' space=''] for attributes nested within .
4. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Synchronizing custom style sheet modifications' space=''].

partdefNewStyleSheet:>StyleSheet{ 
partpartRule:>rule{ 
part:>>condition:ByElementMetaclassCondition{ 
refitem:>>elementMetaclass= SysML::Systems::PartUsage.metadata; 
} 
view:PartTextStyleRule:>>style; // the custom style is passed to the rule's style 
} 
viewdefPartTextStyleRule:>ShapeStyle{ 
attribute:>>penColordefaultcustomColorPalette.customDarkGreen; // the custom color from a custom style class is reused in a custom style definition 
} 
partcustomColorPalette{// custom style class with reusable style attributes for custom color values are specified 
attributecustomDarkGreen:Colordefault"#357F35"; 
attributecustomLightGreen:Colordefault"#BFF7AA"; 
} 
}

##### Specifying the compartment style of a rule

To specify the compartment style of a rule

1. Within the body of the view element, declare a part usage, specify its name (optional), then d efine it by the appropriate compartment style definition. To determine which primary compartment style definitions are available for each element symbol, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables' space=''] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables' space=''] page.
2. Within the body, redefine the needed compartment style attribute followed by the appropriate attribute value. **** To determine which attributes and what value types are available to each primary compartment style definition, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables' space=''] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables' space=''] page.
3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Synchronizing custom style sheet modifications' space='']. partpartRule:>rule{ 
part:>>condition:ByElementMetaclassCondition{ 
refitem:>>elementMetaclass= SysML::Systems::PartUsage.metadata; 
} 
view:ShapeStyle:>>style{ // defined bya primary style definition 
attributeCompartment :AttributesCompartmentStyle{ // attribute for the attributes compartment's 
:>>showCompartmentdefaulttrue; // modifying the *showCompartment* value 
} 
} 
}

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">diagramType</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> = </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_Views::SymbolicViews::gv</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">metadata</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">; <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the condition targets views defined by the <em>General View </em>view definition</span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        }<br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">        /* ----------------------------<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">-------<span> </span></span>*/ </span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">partRule</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">rule</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// an individual rule targets <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><em>PartUsage</em> metaclasses and applies the specified style to them. Because of the global rule, only part usages displayed on a General View will be affected by the rule</span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">condition</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ByElementMetaclassCondition</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> { <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">                </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">ref</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">elementMetaclass</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> = SysML::Systems::PartUsage.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">metadata</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">; </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">            }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ShapeStyle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">style</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">                </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">textColor</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;#2A16FF&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">            }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h3>Creating custom rules</h3><h4>Creating custom rules</h4><p>To create a custom rule</p><hr /><ol><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating style sheets" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Create a style sheet]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Within the body of the part definition of the new style sheet, s</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">pecify the rule, d</span>eclare a part usage, specify a name for the new rule, subset the <em>rule </em>element inherited from the <em>StyleSheet </em>element, and in its body, do <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">any of the following as needed:</span><ol><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Specifying conditions for a rule"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specify the rule's condition]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Specifying styles for a rule"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specify the rule's style]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">.</span></li></ol></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Synchronizing modifications"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Synchronizing custom style sheet modifications" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Synchronize modifications]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span>.<br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3462d38f-58d9-4205-935c-b42fd15cadd7"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">NewStylesPackage</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">private</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">import</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_Styles</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">::**;<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">    private</span><span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">import</span><span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CoreStylesComponents</span>::*;<br />    <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">private</span><span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">import</span><span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Predicates</span>::*;<br />    <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">private</span><span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">import</span><span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ColorDefinitions</span>::*;<br />    <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">private</span><span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">import</span><span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CompartmentVisibilityDefinitions</span>::*;<br />    <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">private</span><span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">import</span><span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">KerMLStyles</span>::*;<br />    <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">private</span><span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">import</span><span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">SysMLStyles</span>::*;</span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">NewStyleSheet</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">StyleSheet</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">        /* -------<span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">new rule <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">-------</span></span><span> </span></span></span>*/</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="aa3e554c-5407-408a-9939-3fad6eab028c">part</ac:inline-comment-marker></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="aa3e554c-5407-408a-9939-3fad6eab028c"> </ac:inline-comment-marker></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="aa3e554c-5407-408a-9939-3fad6eab028c">partRule</ac:inline-comment-marker></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="aa3e554c-5407-408a-9939-3fad6eab028c"> :&gt; </ac:inline-comment-marker></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="aa3e554c-5407-408a-9939-3fad6eab028c">ru</ac:inline-comment-marker>le</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">       <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// Specify the rule's condition</span><br />               <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// Specify the rule's style</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        }<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">        /* ----------------------------<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">-------</span></span><span> </span></span></span>*/</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}<br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h3>Specifying conditions for a rule</h3><h4>Specifying conditions for a rule</h4><p>To specify a condition for a rule</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Within the body of the part element of the rule, declare a part usage and r</span>edefine the <em>condition </em>element inherited from the <em>StyleSheet</em> element, then define it by the needed condition.</li><li>Within the body of the condition, declare a <em>ref item</em>, then r<span>edefine the needed item </span><span>and specify its values.</span><ol><li data-uuid="324224b0-6b91-4f31-8bff-0fc1177b7e1d">For the <em>FreeFormCondition</em>, declare a <em>calc </em>and redefine <em>test</em>, then specify the needed expression.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="66f6e464-a7c3-442a-a23a-47fbe9442747"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The available ref items and their value types depend on the condition definition you are using. For a list of the condition definitions and their available ref items, see the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span><ac:link ac:anchor="Style sheet rule conditions table"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Style sheet rule conditions table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span> on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables" /></ac:link><span> </span>page.</span></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Synchronizing modifications"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Synchronizing custom style sheet modifications" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Synchronize modifications]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span>.<br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="54ec3c1e-f836-4456-9c0a-556a564fff5c"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// <em>ByElementMetaclassCondition</em> example</span></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">partRule</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">rule</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">condition</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ByElementMetaclassCondition</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the rule's condition is specified as <em>ByElementMetaclassCondition</em>, targeting symbols by element metaclass </span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">ref</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">elementMetaclass</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> = SysML::Systems::PartUsage.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">metadata</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">; <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the condition targets elements with the <em>PartUsage</em> metaclasses</span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// <em>ByMetadataAnnotationCondition </em>example</span></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">metadataApprovalRule</span> :&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">rule</span> {<br /></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">   part</span> :&gt;&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">condition</span> : <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ByMetadataAnnotationCondition</span> {<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the rule's condition is specified as <em>ByMetadataAnnotationCondition</em>, targeting symbols by element metadata</span></span><br />      <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">ref</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span> :&gt;&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">annotatingMetadataType</span> = <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">'Vehicle Metadata Definitions'</span>::Approval.<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">metadata</span>;<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the condition targets elements with the applied <em>Approval</em> metadata</span></span></span><br />   }<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// <em>ByDiagramTypeCondition </em>example</span></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">generalViewRule</span> :&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">rule</span> {<br /></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">   part</span> :&gt;&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">condition</span> : <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ByDiagramTypeCondition</span> {<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the rule's condition is specified as <em>ByDiagramTypeCondition</em>, targeting symbols by view definition</span></span><br /><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">      ref</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span> :&gt;&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">diagramType</span> = <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_Views::SymbolicViews::gv</span>.<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">metadata</span>; <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the condition targets views defined by the <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><em>General View </em>view definition</span></span></span></span><br />   }<br /></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span>}</span></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// <em>FreeFormCondition </em>example</span></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">MyFreeformRule</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">rule</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">condition</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">FreeFormCondition</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> { <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the rule's condition is specified as <em>FreeFormCondition</em>, targeting symbols by the specified expression</span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">calc</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">test</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">         '</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">VCCU View'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">metadata</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> == </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">diagram</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// targets element symbols on the specific view '<em>VCCU View</em>'<br />         // OR<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">         element</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> == VCCU.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">metadata</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// targets the <em>VCCU</em> element symbol on all views (to which the style sheet is applied)</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      <br />      }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h3>Specifying styles for a rule</h3><p>There are two ways you can specify a style for a rule:</p><ul><li>Local specification allows you to specify the style of a specific rule only. See the procedure <ac:link ac:anchor="Specifying style attributes locally for the style of a rule" />.</li><li>External specification allows you to create a custom style definition outside the rule itself, which can then be reused for style specification of different rules. See the procedure <ac:link ac:anchor="Creating custom style definitions" />.</li></ul><h4>Specifying style attributes locally for the style of a rule</h4><p>To specify style attributes locally for the style of a rule</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Within the body of the part element of the rule, after the condition specification, </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">declare a view usage, define it by the needed style definition, and </span>redefine the <em>style </em>element inherited from the <em>StyleSheet </em>element.</li><li><span>Within the body of the </span><em>view<span> </span></em><span>element, </span><span>d</span>eclare an attribute usage, redefine the needed attribute and <span>specify its value.<br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d2c2a0ef-0685-478d-9e4a-5f70ded74929"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><span>The available attributes depend on the style definitions <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">(primary or custom) </span>used for the view's definition:</span><ul><li>For a list of primary style definitions, their attributes, and value types, see the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span><ac:link ac:anchor="Symbol styles table"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Symbol styles table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span> on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables" /></ac:link><span> </span>page.</span></li><li>To create custom style definitions, see the <ac:link ac:anchor="Creating custom style definitions" /> procedure.</li></ul></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">To define by multiple style definitions, list them in a comma-separated list.</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Synchronizing modifications"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Synchronizing custom style sheet modifications" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Synchronize modifications]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span>.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ce17c2fa-38b1-4524-9b03-d6abee3ebde3"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">partRule</span> :&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">rule</span> {<br /></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">   part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">condition</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ByElementMetaclassCondition</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">ref</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">elementMetaclass</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> = SysML::Systems::PartUsage.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">metadata</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">: </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ShapeStyle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">style</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> { <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the rule's style is <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">defined by a primary style definition <em>StyleShape</em></span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">textColor</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;#FF0F37&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// local modification of an available attribute's value</span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Hiding style attributes from the Symbol Style dialog for specific element kinds</h4><p>To hide style attributes from the Symbol Style dialog for specific element kinds</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="8cf9a6dd-9439-4468-a94d-d3a142be4436"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Within the body of the part element of the rule, after the condition specification, </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">declare a view usage, define it by the needed style definition, and </span>redefine the <em>style </em>element inherited from the <em>StyleSheet </em>element.</li><li data-uuid="8d40055e-33d0-470c-abcf-c70e4f0ea1d4"><span>Within the body of the </span><em>view<span> </span></em><span>element, </span><span>d</span>eclare an attribute usage and apply the <em>InvisibleInDialog </em>metadata to it, then redefine the style attribute you want to hide from the Symbol Style dialog for the element kind specified in the rule's condition<span>.<br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="922934b4-4737-4b7b-a1b2-9b180f221f01"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><span>The available attributes depend on the style definitions <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">(primary or custom) </span>used for the view's definition. </span>For a list of primary style definitions and their attributes, see the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span><ac:link ac:anchor="Symbol styles table"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Symbol styles table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span> on the</span> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables" /></ac:link><span> </span>page.</li><li data-uuid="14d743eb-5b86-41e9-86f0-1a15c1a8cbec">For more information on the Symbol Style dialog, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Modifying styles of individual symbols" /></ac:link> page.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="4d20e455-c815-4318-87bc-6fe4f415e4f9"><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Synchronizing modifications"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Synchronizing custom style sheet modifications" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Synchronize modifications]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span>.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="18071fed-1f01-4d4f-98fd-7271fa24e1ba"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">partRule</span> :&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">rule</span> {</span><br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">   part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">condition</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ByElementMetaclassCondition</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">ref</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">elementMetaclass</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> = SysML::Systems::PartUsage.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">metadata</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">: </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ShapeStyle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">style</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> { </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">     </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">#</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">InvisibleInDialog</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">contentFillColor</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;  <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the style attribute <em>Content Fill Color</em> is hidden from the Symbol Style dialog for part usages<br /></span></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Creating custom style definitions</h4><p>Unlike local attribute specification, creating custom style definitions allows you to reuse them for style specification of multiple rules. </p><p>You can create custom style definitions specifying the needed attributes and their values. <ac:link ac:anchor="Style sheet composition"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Custom style sheets" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Custom style definitions]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> must subclassify <ac:link ac:anchor="Style sheet composition"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Custom style sheets" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[primary style definitions]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (such as <em>ShapeStyle, ShapeOnBorderStyle</em>, etc.), inheriting their attributes, which you can modify. However, you can only add attributes that are available to the primary style definition that the custom style definition subclassifies. <br />For an extensive list of primary style definitions and their available attributes, see the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span><ac:link ac:anchor="Symbol styles table"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Symbol styles table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span> on the </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables" /></ac:link> page.<br /><br /></p><p>To create a custom style definition</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Within the body of the part definition of the new style sheet, d</span>eclare a view definition element, specify its name, and subclassify a primary style definition<span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> from the </span><em>DS_Styles::CoreStylesComponents::KerMLStyles </em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">package. </span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="596add9c-8bf9-4461-ba72-634bce333c7d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For a list of primary style definitions, their attributes, and value types, see the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span><ac:link ac:anchor="Symbol styles table"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Symbol styles table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span> on the</span> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables" /></ac:link><span> </span>page.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Within the body of the view definition element, d</span><span>eclare an attribute usage, r</span><span>edefine the needed attribute and specify the attribute's value.<br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="41ce5236-0257-4e7a-a387-75d00f1383a9"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The available attributes and their value types depend on the primary style definition you are using. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">For an extensive list of the primary style definitions and their available attributes, see the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span><ac:link ac:anchor="Symbol styles table"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Symbol styles table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span> on the</span> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables" /></ac:link><span> </span>page.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Specify the custom style definition for the style of a rule.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Synchronizing modifications"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Synchronizing custom style sheet modifications" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Synchronize modifications]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span>.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8008acdb-37fd-4acc-aea6-817d5ce97e9d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">NewStyleSheet</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">StyleSheet</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">partRule</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">rule</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">condition</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ByElementMetaclassCondition</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> { <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">custom rule</span></span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">            <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">ref</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span> :&gt;&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">elementMetaclass</span> = SysML::Systems::PartUsage.<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">metadata</span>;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span> : <u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">PartTextStyleRule</span></u> :&gt;&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">style</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the style of the rule is updated to be defined by the new custom style definition</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">PartTextStyleRule</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ShapeStyle</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> { <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// new <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">custom style definition subclassifies the primary style definition <em>ShapeStyle</em></span></span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span> :&gt;&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">textColor</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;#68FF70&quot;</span>;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Creating custom reusable style attribute values</h4><p style="text-align: left;">You can create custom style attributes whose values you can reuse by referencing them where needed. Additionally, you can create custom style classes grouping such attributes. </p><p>To create custom reusable style attribute values</p><hr /><ol><li><span>Declare an attribute, s</span>pecify its name, then define it by the needed value type, and s<span>pecify its value. </span></li><li>(Optional) To group attributes within a custom style class, declare a part usage, specify its name, then place the attributes within the part's body.</li><li>Reuse the newly created style attribute values for <ac:link ac:anchor="Creating custom style definitions"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[custom style definition attributes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or <ac:link ac:anchor="Specifying style attributes locally for the style of a rule"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[local attribute specification]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. <span>Use <ac:link ac:anchor="Name resolution"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing textual notation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[feature chaining]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> for attributes nested within <ac:link ac:anchor="Creating custom reusable style attribute values"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[custom style classes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. </span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Synchronizing modifications"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Synchronizing custom style sheet modifications" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Synchronize modifications]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span>.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="78d7dca6-fd32-48a1-8bdc-0a91345f6019"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">NewStyleSheet</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">StyleSheet</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">   part </span>partRule</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">rule</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">condition</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ByElementMetaclassCondition</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">         </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">ref</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span><span SysML::Systems::PartUsage.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">metadata</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">: </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">PartTextStyleRule</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the custom style is passed to the rule's style</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span
