    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">: </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">AbstractCyanStyle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">style</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">the <em>AbstractGreenRule </em>inherited from the <em>partPortRule </em>via the <em>DefaultStyleSheet </em>style sheet is replaced with the predefined style definition <em>AbstractCyanStyle</em></span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h4><span>Modifying the style </span><span>attributes locally </span><span>for the style of a rule</span></h4><p>There are two ways you can specify a style for a rule:</p><ul><li>Local specification allows you to specify the style of a specific rule only. See the procedure <ac:link ac:anchor="Modifying the style attributes locally for the style of a rule" />.</li><li>External specification allows you to create a custom style definition outside the rule itself, which can then be reused for the style specification of different rules. See the subsection <ac:link ac:anchor="Creating custom style definitions"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating style sheet components" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Creating custom style definitions]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating style sheet components" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Creating style sheet components]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</li></ul><p>To modify the style attributes locally for the style of a rule</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Reusing style sheet rules"><ac:link-body>Reuse a style sheet<em> </em>rule</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Declare a view usage, redefine the inherited part <em>style</em>, and in its body, redefine the needed attribute, specifying the new value.</span></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="da526bc2-a83e-41f7-815d-59ea5630d4cd"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The attributes available to the specified style definition depend on the style definitions they subclassify. <span>For an extensive list of the predefined style definitions and the primary style definitions they subclassify, and their available attributes, see the <ac:link ac:anchor="Symbol styles table"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Symbol styles table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Style sheet conditions and styles tables]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Synchronizing modifications"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Synchronizing custom style sheet modifications" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Synchronize modifications]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span>.<br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c0aff3c0-f9f3-4f8b-8b22-ac476e30ed42"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">NewStyleSheet</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DefaultStyleSheet</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> { </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">customizedPartPortRule</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">partPortRule</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">style<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);"> </span>{ <br style="text-align: left;" /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">         </span>attribute</span> :&gt;&gt; fillColor <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;#D9EEDA&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;   <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// redefining the attribute <em>fillColor</em> available via the predefined style definition <em>AbstractGreenRule</em> </span></span><br style="text-align: left;" /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      } </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><span><br /></span></li></ol><h4><span>Modifying the </span><span>compartment style </span><span>of a rule</span></h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c238023d-21db-494d-b79b-b6ad468a9051"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The majority of the compartments are disabled (set to <em>false</em>) for most of the elements in the <em>DefaultStyleSheet </em>style sheet via the <em>HideAllCompartments</em> predefined compartment style definition.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To modify the compartment style of a rule </p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Reusing style sheet rules"><ac:link-body>Reuse a style sheet<em> </em>rule</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Declare a view usage, redefine the inherited part <em>style</em>. In its body, declare a part for the needed compartment and define it by its definition. In its body, redefine the needed attribute, specifying the new value.</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> </span><br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="eb8c8880-e28e-45bb-9747-60e585fc5aca"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To determine which primary compartment style definitions (including their attributes and value types) are available for each element symbol, see the <ac:link ac:anchor="Symbol compartment styles table"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Symbol compartment styles table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Style sheet conditions and styles tables" /></ac:link> page.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Synchronizing modifications"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Synchronizing custom style sheet modifications" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Synchronize modifications]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span>.<br /></span></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b872d7c9-2193-4f1f-b8bb-a09d36e490e0"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">NewStyleSheet</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DefaultStyleSheet</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">newPartPortRule</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">partPortRule</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">style</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">         </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">attributeCompartment </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">: </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">AttributesCompartmentStyle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">showCompartment</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">true</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the inherited <em>attributeCompartment</em> part's <em>showCompartment</em> attribute's value is changed to <em>true</em></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">         }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span><br /></span></span></li></ol>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254422899 space=SYSML2P version=10 -->
## PAGE 00308: (2026x) Customizing View Creation dialog

- page_id: `254422899`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254422899/2026x+Customizing+View+Creation+dialog
- version_number: 10
- version_date: `2025-12-30T10:32:22.067+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model customization > (2026x) Custom creation dialogs > (2026x) Custom View Creation dialog
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

See the following procedures for customizing the predefined *SysMLViewCreationDialog* configuration for the View Creation dialog and submenu.

The procedures on this page use the example of customizing the*SysMLViewCreationDialog***part definition. However, you can similarly customize any other custom view creation dialog customization part definition you may have.

#### NOTE: Updating the View Creation dialog/submenu

Updating the View Creation dialog/submenu

Reload the project for changes made to the customized View Creation dialog/submenu to take effect. Then, specify the active View Dialog via the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Activating View Creation dialog' space=''].

##### Reusing a View Creation dialog/submenu

To reuse a View Creation dialog/submenu

1. In the Containment tree, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing elements' space=''] .
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing Textual Editor' space=''] for the new namespace.
3. In the Textual Editor, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Package' space=''] and name it.
4. Within the body of the package, declare a part definition, specify the name for the dialog instance, subclassify the SysMLViewCreationDialog definition from the DS_UIComponents::UIComponents package, then, within the body of the definition:
  1. 
  2. 
  3. 
5. Click the Synchronize button.

package CustomViewCreationDialogs { 
 part def CustomizedViewCreationDialog :>DS_UIComponents::UIComponents::SysMLViewCreationDialog{ // redefining and renaming the *SysMLViewCreationDialog*} 
}

##### Renaming the Create View command

The default name for the View Creation dialog's invocation command is **Create View**.

To rename the Create View command

1. Within the body of the part definition element of the new View Creation dialog instance, redefine the actionName attribute with the new view creation command name.
2. Click the Synchronize button.

partdefCustomizedViewCreationDialog:> DS_UIComponents::UIComponents::SysMLViewCreationDialog{ 
attribute:>> actionNamedefault"Create Diagram";// renaming the view creation command to "Create Diagram"}

##### Modifying the View Creation dialog/submenu command categories

#### INFO: Command categories

Command categories

View Creation dialog/submenu command categories predefined in the *3DS SysML Customization* library's *DS_UIComponents::UIComponents::SysMLViewCreationDialog*are:

- sysMLViewsMenu - for the symbolic views command category in the View Creation dialog/submenu.
- sysMLTableMenu - for the tabular views command category in the View Creation dialog/submenu.

###### Creating view creation command categories for the View Creation dialog/submenu

To create view creation command categories for the View Creation dialog/submenu

- See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating View Creation dialog' space=''] procedure on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating View Creation dialog' space=''] page.

###### Removing view creation command categories from the View Creation dialog/submenu

To remove view creation command categories from the View Creation dialog/submenu

1. Within the body of the part definition element of the new View Creation dialog instance, redefine the command category you want to customize, inherited from the SysMLViewCreationDialog definition and set the multiplicity to zero.
2. Click the Synchronize button.

partdefCustomizedViewCreationDialog:> DS_UIComponents::UIComponents::SysMLViewCreationDialog{ 
part:>>sysMLTableMenu[0];// removing the *sysMLTableMenu* command category by setting the multiplicity to zero 
}

###### Modifying the view creation command category attributes of the View Creation dialog/submenu

#### INFO: Command category attributes

Command category attributes

View Creation dialog/submenu command category attributes predefined for *sysMLViewsMenu*and *sysMLTableMenu*in *DS_UIComponents::UIComponents::SysMLViewCreationDialog*are:

- attribute :>> label default "Symbolic Views" ; // attribute specifies the category name
- attribute:>> isCollapseddefaulttrue;// attribute specifies whether the category is collapsed or expanded by default

- If the dialog/submenu contains only one command category, then the category name is not displayed. In this case, both the *label* and *isCollapsed* attributes are ignored.
- If the dialog/submenu contains fewer than ten view commands, then the category cannot be collapsed. In this case, the *isCollapsed* attribute is ignored.

To modify the view creation command category attributes of the View Creation dialog/submenu

1. Within the body of the part definition element of the new View Creation instance, redefine the command category you want to customize, inherited from the SysMLViewCreationDialog definition.
2. Within the body of the redefining category, redefine the inherited attributes that you want to modify with the needed values.
3. Update the values as needed.
4. Click the Synchronize button.

partdefCustomizedViewCreationDialog:> DS_UIComponents::UIComponents::SysMLViewCreationDialog{ 
part:>>sysMLViewsMenu{ 
attribute:>> isCollapseddefaulttrue;// modified command category default collapse state 
attribute:>> labeldefault"SysML v2 Symbolic Views";// modified command category name 
} 
}

##### Modifying the View Creation dialog/submenu commands

#### INFO: Commands in categories

Commands in categories

View Creation dialog/submenu commands predefined for *sysMLViewsMenu*and *sysMLTableMenu*in *DS_UIComponents::UIComponents::SysMLViewCreationDialog*are:

- sysMLViewsMenu:
  - gvItem - command for General View
  - ivItem - command for Interconnection View
  - afvItem - command for Action Flow View
  - stvItem - command for State Transition View
- sysMLTableMenu:
  - gtItem - command for General Table
  - rtItem - command for Requirements Table

###### Removing view creation commands from the View Creation dialog/submenu command categories

To remove view creation commands from the View Creation dialog/submenu command categories

1. Place the cursor within the body of the part element redefining a View Creation dialog/submenu command category*.*
2. Declare a part usage, then redefine the command you want to remove and set its multiplicity to zero.
3. Click the Synchronize button.

partdefCustomizedViewCreationDialog:> DS_UIComponents::UIComponents::SysMLViewCreationDialog{ 
 part:>>sysMLViewsMenu{ 
part:>>afvItem[0]; // removing the *afvItem* command from the *sysMLViewsMenu*category by setting the multiplicity to zero 
} 
}

###### Renaming view creation commands in the View Creation dialog/submenu command categories

To rename view creation commands in the View Creation dialog/submenu command categories

1. Place the cursor within the body of the part element redefining a View Creation dialog/submenu command category and declare a part usage, then redefine the command you want to modify.
2. Within the body of the redefining usage, redefine the inherited *label*attribute with the needed value.
3. Click the Synchronize button.

partdefCustomizedViewCreationDialog:> DS_UIComponents::UIComponents::SysMLViewCreationDialog{ 
part:>>sysMLViewsMenu{ 
part:>>afvItem{ 
attribute:>> labeldefault"Action Flow Diagram"; // modified command name 
}} 
}

###### Adding view creation commands to the View Creation dialog/submenu command categories

To add view creation commands to the View Creation dialog/submenu command categories

- See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating View Creation dialog' space=''] procedure on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating View Creation dialog' space=''] page.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

data-uuid="e2109ce4-57f1-45d9-aeb3-e369c7253589"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Click the<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">Synchronize<span> </span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">button.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="73c043ce-2665-49c5-8629-b999684bf9a8"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CustomizedViewCreationDialog</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_UIComponents::UIComponents::</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">SysMLViewCreationDialog</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; actionName </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;<u>Create Diagram</u>&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// renaming the view creation command to &quot;Create Diagram&quot;<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Modifying the View Creation dialog/submenu command categories</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9a77c693-3e4a-4ed4-a7d8-2e8270585d2f"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Command categories</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>View Creation dialog/submenu command categories predefined in the <em>3DS SysML Customization</em> library's <em>DS_UIComponents::UIComponents::SysMLViewCreationDialog </em>are: </p><ul><li data-uuid="0d208ba7-0503-4674-9e7a-f941f0d9e53a"><em>sysMLViewsMenu</em> - for the symbolic views command category in the View Creation dialog/submenu. </li><li data-uuid="2c468e4d-621a-4523-82cc-30a56ae980eb"><em>sysMLTableMenu</em> - for the tabular views command category in the View Creation dialog/submenu.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4><span class="toc-item-body">Creating view creation command categories for the View Creation dialog/submenu</span></h4><p><span class="toc-item-body">To create view creation command categories for the View Creation dialog/submenu</span></p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="a51c8443-18a3-46a9-8f1c-af9abc9ff74e"><span class="toc-item-body">See the <ac:link ac:anchor="Creating view creation command categories for the View Creation dialog/submenu"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating View Creation dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Creating view creation command categories for the View Creation dialog/submenu]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> procedure on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating View Creation dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Creating View Creation dialog]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</span></li></ul><h4>Removing view creation command categories from the View Creation dialog/submenu</h4><p><span>To remove view creation command categories from the View Creation dialog/submenu</span></p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="aa365c65-95bb-460a-bc49-0bbf0c9d0ef1">Within the body of the part definition element of the new View Creation dialog instance, redefine the command category you want to customize, inherited from the <em>SysMLViewCreationDialog</em> definition and set the multiplicity to zero.</li><li data-uuid="55917ace-71f1-4b6d-a198-f43153652f09"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Click the<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">Synchronize<span> </span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">button.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1e8e66d6-7698-45ad-98f5-c6739db3895f"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CustomizedViewCreationDialog</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_UIComponents::UIComponents</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">::SysMLViewCreationDialog</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">sysMLTableMenu</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> [</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">0</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><u>]</u>; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// removing the <em>sysMLTableMenu</em> command category by setting the multiplicity to zero</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Modifying the view creation command category attributes of the View Creation dialog/submenu</h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7e30ba43-c8da-497e-9daf-45a436f217ce"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Command category attributes</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>View Creation dialog/submenu command category attributes predefined for <em>sysMLViewsMenu </em>and <em>sysMLTableMenu </em>in <em>DS_UIComponents::UIComponents::SysMLViewCreationDialog </em>are:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2725aea4-a54a-4e36-9039-d8ab57ed21d1"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="f39f2d1d-3b98-49cf-bc24-19055cfd44b0"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; label </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;<u>Symbolic Views</u>&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// attribute specifies the category name<br /></span></li><li data-uuid="1b7d057b-3ac9-41cb-9e13-a0d0c1722f9d"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; isCollapsed </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">true</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">; </span>// attribute specifies whether the category is collapsed or expanded by default</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c1f372b4-2387-4654-8e9e-76748d2ef534"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="4cce9b81-4155-4f89-b96f-fcbbef889c68"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">If the dialog/submenu contains only one command category, then the category name is not displayed. In this case, both the <em>label</em> and <em>isCollapsed</em> attributes are ignored.</span></li><li data-uuid="7ac17c98-5d7e-4715-9e45-627033e07fdd"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">If the dialog/submenu contains fewer than ten view commands, then the category cannot be collapsed. In this case, the <em>isCollapsed</em> attribute is ignored.</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To modify the view creation command category attributes of the View Creation dialog/submenu</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="c1a3070f-5c6b-4aa3-a030-defdf6a6ef6a">Within the body of the part definition element of the new  View Creation instance, redefine the command category you want to customize, inherited from the <em>SysMLViewCreationDialog</em> definition.</li><li data-uuid="9d2bc424-07c8-4401-be3f-5e49c3e3c4df"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Within the body of the redefining category, </span>redefine <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">the inherited attributes that you want to modify with the needed values.</span></li><li data-uuid="9d2bc424-07c8-4401-be3f-5e49c3e3c4df"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Update the values as needed.</span></li><li data-uuid="37ec15cb-4691-49d7-8d63-b1d8478f84ef"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Click the<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">Synchronize<span> </span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">button.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5d85b0fb-cb16-4ef0-8d6a-b5ead1b9832f"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CustomizedViewCreationDialog</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_UIComponents::UIComponents</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">::SysMLViewCreationDialog</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">sysMLViewsMenu</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; isCollapsed </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">true</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// modified command category default collapse state</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; label </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;<u>SysML v2 Symbolic Views</u>&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// modified command category name</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Modifying the View Creation dialog/submenu commands</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="465518da-8e26-400a-b5b8-7fd47b90ee9e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Commands in categories</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>View Creation dialog/submenu commands predefined for <em>sysMLViewsMenu </em>and <em>sysMLTableMenu </em>in <em>DS_UIComponents::UIComponents::SysMLViewCreationDialog </em>are:</p><ul><li data-uuid="5e50ad2a-7b75-49d7-87e7-a3e3ee5b4270"><em>sysMLViewsMenu:</em><ul><li><em>gvItem - </em>command for General View</li><li><em>ivItem - </em>command for Interconnection View</li><li><em>afvItem - </em>command for Action Flow View</li><li><em>stvItem - </em>command for State Transition View</li></ul></li><li data-uuid="92850224-2ef2-4e11-9dd0-f5773caba018"><em>sysMLTableMenu:</em><ul><li><em>gtItem </em>-<em> </em>command for General Table</li><li><em>rtItem</em> - command for Requirements Table</li></ul></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Removing view creation commands from the View Creation dialog/submenu command categories</h4><p>To remove view creation commands from the View Creation dialog/submenu command categories</p><hr /><ol style="text-align: left;"><li data-uuid="d4a14b66-8231-41cb-b0fc-b49767269475"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Place the cursor within the body of the part element redefining a View Creation dialog/submenu command category<em>.</em></span></li><li data-uuid="9b0b04eb-1607-45d9-b0d8-c2f7ab6e65dd"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Declare a part usage, then redefine the command you want to remove and set its multiplicity to zero.</span></li><li data-uuid="345dca75-a7b2-46a0-aa5c-483afdd5a55a"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Click the<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">Synchronize<span> </span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">button.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="48143ee6-7510-4b2c-acf2-ca062bd903c1"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CustomizedViewCreationDialog</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_UIComponents::UIComponents</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">::SysMLViewCreationDialog</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br />   <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">sysMLViewsMenu</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">afvItem</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> [</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">0</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><u>]</u>; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// removing the <em><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">afvItem</span></em> command from the <em>sysMLViewsMenu </em>category by setting the multiplicity to zero</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Renaming view creation commands in the View Creation dialog/submenu command categories</h4><p>To rename view creation commands in the View Creation dialog/submenu command categories</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="32b106af-1ebb-4823-85f4-89d12d6c6f99"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Place the cursor within the body of the part element redefining a View Creation dialog/submenu command category </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">and declare a part usage, then redefine the command you want to modify.</span></li><li data-uuid="6c874d68-3f66-48d5-958a-e93fd4f1eba0"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Within the body of the redefining usage, redefine the inherited <em>label </em>attribute with the needed value.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><br /></span></li><li data-uuid="be761cfb-aa2f-4020-a7d8-24c381848ffc"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Click the<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">Synchronize<span> </span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">button.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="657cc2ff-7dc8-4a04-8146-1f8a6bb0c486"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CustomizedViewCreationDialog</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_UIComponents::UIComponents</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">::SysMLViewCreationDialog</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">sysMLViewsMenu</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">afvItem</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">{</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">         </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:&gt;&gt; label </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;<u>Action Flow Diagram</u>&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// modified command name</span> </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      }<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Adding view creation commands to the View Creation dialog/submenu command categories</h4><p>To add view creation commands to the View Creation dialog/submenu command categories</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="e10258c1-074d-4e2f-82cf-c8c0939729fc">See the <ac:link ac:anchor="Adding view creation commands to the View Creation dialog/submenu command categories"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating View Creation dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Adding view creation commands to the View Creation dialog/submenu command categories]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> procedure on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating View Creation dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Creating View Creation dialog]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</li></ul>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254422355 space=SYSML2P version=6 -->
## PAGE 00309: (2026x) Dependency

- page_id: `254422355`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254422355/2026x+Dependency
- version_number: 6
- version_date: `2025-12-11T19:13:55.382+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Relations > (2026x) Traceability
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A dependency is a kind of relationship between any number of client (source) and supplier (target) elements. This implies that a change to a supplier element may result in a change to a client element.Dependencies can be useful for representing relationships between elements in an abstract way.Dependencies can be binary, with one client and supplier, or n-ary, with one client and more than one supplier.The n-ary dependency is displayed as a dot symbol with segments consisting of dependency paths and client/supplier symbols.

50%

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The image displays a *Use*dependency where the package *Service Layer*is the client (source) and*Data Layer*is the supplier (target). The supplier end of the dependency path is indicated with an arrowhead.

50%

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The image displays an unnamed n-ary dependency where the package *Service Layer*is the client (source). The packages*Data Layer*and*External Interface Layer*are the supplier (target).The supplier end of the dependency path is indicated with an arrowhead.

Dependency is deleted if client or supplier count in the dependency is reduced zero.

##### Creating dependencies

###### Creating dependencies via the Textual Editor

To create dependencies via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the dependency keyword.
2. Specify the element name (optional), then the from keyword and the name of the client (source) element. Then follow with the keyword to and the name(s) of the supplier (target) element(s) listed in a comma-seperated list. If the dependency has no name, the keyword*from*can be omitted.
3. (Optional) Specify a body with any annotating elements owned by the dependency via annotation relationships.
4. Click the Synchronize button. package'Data Layer';package'Service Layer';package'External Interface Layer';dependencyfrom'Service Layer'to'External Interface Layer'; // unnamed dependencydependencyUsefrom'Service Layer'to'External Interface Layer'; // named dependencydependency from 'Service Layer' to 'External Interface Layer', 'Data Layer'; // unnamed n-ary dependency (with more than one supplier (target))dependency'Service Layer'to'Data Layer'{ // unnamed dependency with a body 
/* 'Service Layer' is the dependency client, 'Data Layer' is the supplier. */ 
}

###### Creating dependencies via the view palette

To create dependencies via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Other Relationships group, click the **dependency** button.
2. In the view pane, click on the client (source) and then on the supplier (target) to create a dependency path between them.

###### Creating dependenciesvia the Create Relationship command

To create dependencies via the Create Relationship command

1. Right-click on the client (source) element.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Create Relationship , then in the submenu, click Outgoing , and then in the submenu, click dependency . The Element Picker dialog opens.
3. In the Element Picker dialog, do one of the following:
  1. In a view, click the element symbol you want to be the supplier (target). Hold Shift to select multiple elements.
  2. Click the Add [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The element selection dialog opens. In the element selection dialog, select the element you want to be the supplier (target) and click OK .
  3. Drag and drop the needed element you want to be the supplier (target) from the Containment tree onto the Element Picker .
4. In the Element Picker dialog, click OK .

###### Creating n-ary dependencies via the view

To create n-ary dependencies via the view

1. .
2. Select the n-ary dot symbol and in its smart manipulator, do one of the following:
  1. Click the client command. Click on the element symbol you want to specify as the dependency's client. Repeat for all the needed elements.
  2. Click the supplier command. Click on the element symbol you want to specify as the dependency's supplier. Repeat for all the needed elements.

##### Converting binary and n-ary dependencies

###### Converting binary dependency to n-ary via the view

To convert binary dependency to n-ary via the view

1. In the view pane, right-click the binary dependency you want to convert to an n-ary.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Refactor , then Convert to N-ary Dot . The binary dependency is converted and the n-ary dot symbol is displayed.

###### Converting n-arydependencytobinaryvia the view

To convert n-ary dependency to binary via the view

1. In the view pane, right-click the n-ary dot symbol of the dependency you want to convert to a binary.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Refactor , then Convert to Path . The n-ary dependency dot symbol is converted and the binary path is displayed. The **Convert to Path**command is only available if the dependency has one client and supplier.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254423094 space=SYSML2P version=6 -->
## PAGE 00310: (2026x) Derive requirement

- page_id: `254423094`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254423094/2026x+Derive+requirement
- version_number: 6
- version_date: `2025-12-11T19:26:20.667+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Requirements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Derived requirements are created using the derive relationship via the derivation connection. The derive relationship links more detailed requirements to their original, higher-level requirements. A derivation connection asserts that one or more derived requirements are derived from a single original requirement. The single end for the original requirement should subset the original requirement, while the rest of the ends should subset derived requirements. Once the original requirement is satisfied, the derived ones must also be satisfied.

50%

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The image displays an unnamed binary derivation where the requirement *heating & cooling*is the original and*heating*is the derived one. The original and derived ends are connected with the connection path.

50%

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The image displays an unnamed n-ary derivation where the requirement *heating & cooling*is the original and *heating*is the derived one.The original and derived ends are connected with the connection path.

Derivation is deleted if original or derived requirement count in the derivation is reduced zero.

##### Deriving requirements

###### Deriving requirements via the Textual Editor

To derive requirements via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the derivation and import the RequirementDerivation library.
2. Declare the #derivation conncetion keyword, specify the derivation name (optional), [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specializations' space=''] (optional).
3. In the derivation body:
  1. Specify the end #original keyword [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Reference subsetting' space=''] the original requirement.
  2. Specify the end #derive keyword [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Reference subsetting' space=''] the derived requirement. Repeat for multiple derived requirements.
4. Click the Synchronize button.

// --------- requirements and requirement derivations in separate packages

packageRequirements{// requirements package 
requirement'heating & cooling'; 
requirementheating; 
requirementcooling; 
} 
packageDerivations{// derivations package 
privateimportRequirementDerivation::*;// *RequirementDerivation* library import 
privateimportRequirements::*;// requirements package import 
#derivationconnection : 'Derived System Requirements'{// unnamed specialized (feature typing) binary derivation connection 
end#original::>'heating & cooling';// original requirement end 
end#derive::>'heating';// derived requirement end 
} 
}

// --------- requirements and requirement derivations in the same package

packageRequirements{// requirements package 
privateimportRequirementDerivation::*;// *RequirementDerivation* library import 
requirement'heating & cooling'; 
requirementheating; 
requirementcooling;#derivationconnection'heating & cooling derivations'{// named n-ary derivation connectionend#original::>'heating & cooling';// original requirement endend#derive::>'heating';// derived requirement endend#derive::>'cooling';// derived requirement end}}

###### Deriving requirements via the view palette

To derive requirements via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click one of the following:
  1. The **derivation** button to create a derivation connection.
    1. In the view pane, click on the requirement you want to specify as the original one, then the one you want to specify as derived.
  2. The **n-ary****derivation** button to create an n-ary derivation connection.
    1. Click in the view pane where you want the n-ary derivation dot symbol to be created.
    2. Select the n-ary dot symbol and in its smart manipulator, click the **original** end command.
    3. Draw the end from the n-ary connection symbol to the requirement symbol you want to specify as the original one.
    4. Select the n-ary dot symbol and in its smart manipulator, click the **derived** end command.
    5. Draw the end from the n-ary connection symbol to the requirement symbol you want to specify as the derived one. Repeat for all elements you want to connect.

If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to the **derivation**,**n-ary derivation**,****or the**derivation****def**button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.

###### Creating additional n-ary derivations via the view

To create additional n-ary derivations via the view

1. Select the n-ary dot symbol and in its smart manipulator, do one of the following:
  1. Click the original end command. Click on the requirement symbol you want to specify as the original requirement.
  2. Click the **derived end** command. Click on the requirement symbol you want to specify as the derived requirement. Repeat for all the needed elements.

##### Converting binary and n-ary derivations

###### Converting binary derivation to n-ary via the view

To convert binary derivation to n-ary via the view

1. In the view pane, right-click the binary derivation you want to convert to an n-ary.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Refactor , then Convert to N-ary Dot . The binary derivation is converted and the n-ary dot symbol is displayed.

###### Converting n-aryderivationtobinaryvia the view

To convert n-ary derivation to binary via the view

1. In the view pane, right-click the n-ary dot symbol of the derivation you want to convert to a binary.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Refactor , then Convert to Path . The n-ary derivation dot symbol is converted and the binary path is displayed. The **Convert to Path**command is only available if the derivation has one client and supplier.

##### Creating derivation connection definition elements

###### Creating derivation definitions via the Textual Editor

To create derivation definitions via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the #derivation connection def keyword.
2. Name the element, then specify the derivation definition features in the element body:
  1. Specify the end #original keyword, followed by the end name (optional) and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specializations' space=''] of the original requirement.
  2. Specify the end #derive keyword, followed by the end name (optional) and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specializations' space=''] of the derived requirement.
3. Click the **Synchronize**button. #derivationconnectiondef'Derived system requirements'{ 
end#originaloriginalReq:vehicleRequirements; 
end#derivederivedReq:engineRequirements; 
}

###### Auto-creating derivation definition elements via the Extract Definition command

To auto-create derivation definition elements via the Extract Definition command

- See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Extract Definition and Usage' space='']page.

###### Creating derivation definitions via the view palette

To create derivation definitions via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the **derivation** **def** button to create a derivation definition element. If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to the **derivation** or**n-ary derivation**button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.
2. Click in the view pane where you want to create the derivation definition symbol. Specify the derivation definition ends (via the Textual Editor or the Specification panel).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

Once the original requirement is satisfied, the derived ones must also be satisfied.</span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="section" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2cef7390-8a54-4fe0-9277-1baedd3f9cce"><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="32c505b8-170f-4d6f-bc1a-0d5d346edf28"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="binary_derivation.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The image displays an unnamed binary derivation where the requirement <em>heating &amp; cooling </em><span>is the original and </span><em>heating </em><span>is the derived one. The original and derived ends are connected with the connection path.</span></h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a3b8aefb-22a0-41c2-8b91-5b48f8ca76a1"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="n-ary_derivation.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The image displays an unnamed n-ary derivation where the requirement <em>heating &amp; cooling </em><span>is the original and <em>heating </em>is the derived one. </span><span>The original and derived ends are connected with the connection path.</span></h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c45965e4-c2f0-4147-8f06-8524b0e6feaa"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Derivation is deleted if original or derived requirement count in the derivation is reduced zero.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Deriving requirements</h3><h4>Deriving requirements via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To derive requirements via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the derivation and import the <em>RequirementDerivation </em>library.</li><li>Declare the <em>#derivation conncetion</em> keyword, specify the derivation name (optional),<span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Specializations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[specialization]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">(optional).</span></li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">In the derivation body:</span><ol><li>Specify the <em>end #original </em>keyword <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Reference subsetting" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[reference subsetting]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> the original requirement. </li><li>Specify the <em>end #derive </em>keyword <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Reference subsetting" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[reference subsetting]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> the derived requirement. Repeat for multiple derived requirements.</li></ol></li><li>Click the <strong>Synchronize<span> </span></strong>button.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0ef5b49d-69ea-4624-8235-f74f8cc7f679"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// --------- requirements and requirement derivations in separate packages</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Requirements</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {                                                            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// requirements package</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">requirement</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'heating &amp; cooling'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">requirement</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">heating</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">cooling</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Derivations</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {                                                                </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// derivations package</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">private</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">import</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">RequirementDerivation</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">::*;                              </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// <em>RequirementDerivation</em> library import</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">private</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">import</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Requirements</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">::*;                                             </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// requirements package import</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    #</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">derivation</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">connection : <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'Derived System Requirements'</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// unnamed specialized (feature typing) binary derivation connection</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">end</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> #</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">original</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> ::&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'heating &amp; cooling'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;                                </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// original requirement end</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">end</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> #</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// derived requirement end</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// --------- requirements and requirement derivations in the same package</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Requirements</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {                                                          </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">RequirementDerivation</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">::*; style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">requirement</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'heating &amp; cooling'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">heating</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    #<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">derivation</span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">connection</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'heating &amp; cooling derivations'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {  </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// named n-ary derivation connection<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">end</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> #</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">original</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> ::&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// original requirement end<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">end</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> #</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">derive</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> ::&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'heating'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;                                                </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// derived requirement end<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">end</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> #</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">derive</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> ::&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'cooling'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;                                                </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// derived requirement end<br /></span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    } <br /></span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Deriving requirements via the view palette</h4><p>To derive requirements via the view palette</p><hr /><ol><li>In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click one of the following:<br /><ol><li>The<span> <strong>derivation </strong></span>button to create a derivation connection.<ol><li>In the view pane, click on the requirement you want to specify as the original one, then the one you want to specify as derived.</li></ol></li><li>The<span> <strong>n-ary </strong><strong>derivation</strong></span><span> </span>button to create an n-ary derivation connection.<ol><li>Click in the view pane where you want the n-ary derivation dot symbol to be created.</li><li>Select the n-ary dot symbol and in its smart manipulator, click the<span> <strong>original </strong></span><strong>end<span> </span></strong>command. </li><li>Draw the end from the n-ary connection symbol to the requirement symbol you want to specify as the original one.</li><li>Select the n-ary dot symbol and in its smart manipulator, click the<span> <strong>derived </strong></span><strong>end<span> </span></strong>command. </li><li>Draw the end from the n-ary connection symbol to the requirement symbol you want to specify as the derived one. Repeat for all elements you want to connect.</li></ol></li></ol></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="208c68ea-9cec-45e6-a15b-dd18b8d3f1f3"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to the <span><strong>derivation</strong>,<strong> n-ary derivation</strong>,<strong> </strong></span>or the<span> <strong>derivation </strong></span><strong>def<span> </span></strong>button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Creating additional n-ary derivations via the view</h4><p>To create additional n-ary derivations via the view</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the n-ary dot symbol and in its smart manipulator, do one of the following:<ol><li>Click the<span> </span><strong>original end<span> </span></strong>command. Click on the requirement symbol you want to specify as the original requirement. </li><li>Click the<span> <strong>derived end</strong></span><strong><span> </span></strong>command. Click on the requirement symbol you want to specify as the derived requirement. Repeat for all the needed elements.</li></ol></li></ol><h3>Converting binary and n-ary derivations</h3><h4>Converting binary derivation to n-ary via the view</h4><p>To convert binary derivation to n-ary via the view</p><hr /><ol><li>In the view pane, right-click the binary derivation you want to convert to an n-ary.</li><li>In the shortcut menu, click <strong>Refactor</strong>, then <strong>Convert to N-ary Dot</strong>. The binary derivation is converted and the n-ary dot symbol is displayed.</li></ol><h4>Converting <span>n-ary </span><span>derivation </span><span>to </span><span>binary </span><span>via the view</span></h4><p>To convert n-ary derivation to binary via the view</p><hr /><ol><li>In the view pane, right-click the n-ary dot symbol of the derivation you want to convert to a binary.</li><li>In the shortcut menu, click <strong>Refactor</strong>, then <strong>Convert to Path</strong>. The n-ary derivation dot symbol is converted and the binary path is displayed.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0dcc5e6d-b75e-4dc3-9a76-b8575964a7c5"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <strong>Convert to Path </strong>command is only available if the derivation has one client and supplier. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h3 class="auto-cursor-target">Creating derivation connection definition elements</h3><h4>Creating derivation definitions via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To create derivation definitions via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the <em>#derivation connection def</em> keyword.</li><li>Name the element, then specify the derivation definition features in the element body:<ol><li>Specify the <em>end</em> <em>#original </em>keyword, followed by the end name (optional) and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Specializations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[specialization]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of the original requirement. </li><li>Specify the <em>end</em> <em>#derive </em>keyword, followed by the end name (optional) and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Specializations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[specialization]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of the derived requirement. </li></ol></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong>button. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="802b05e2-76b4-4c33-bb2a-fa228b066a7c"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">#derivation </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">connection</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'Derived system requirements' </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">{</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">end</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> #original </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">originalReq</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">vehicleRequirements</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">end</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> #derive </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">derivedReq</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">engineRequirements</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h4>Auto-creating derivation definition elements via the Extract Definition command</h4><p><span>To auto-create derivation definition elements via the Extract Definition command</span></p><hr /><ul><li><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Extract Definition and Usage" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Extract Definition and Usage]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">page.</span></span></li></ul><h4>Creating derivation definitions via the view palette</h4><p>To create derivation definitions via the view palette</p><hr /><ol><li>In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the<span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> <strong>derivation</strong></span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> <strong>def </strong></span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">button to create a derivation definition element.<br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="35819f6b-9a74-40cd-bfc8-eac27c667eb0"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to the <strong>derivation</strong> or<strong> n-ary derivation </strong>button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Click in the view pane where you want to create the derivation definition symbol. Specify the derivation definition ends (via the Textual Editor or the Specification panel).</li></ol></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254422498 space=SYSML2P version=3 -->
## PAGE 00311: (2026x) Directed features for actions

- page_id: `254422498`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254422498/2026x+Directed+features+for+actions
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-12-03T08:57:20.312+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Behavior > (2026x) Actions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Actions may have features with directions in, out or inout that are considered to be parameters of the action. Features with direction in or inout are input parameters, and features with direction out or inout are output parameters. An action usage inherits the parameters of its definitions, if any, and it can also define its own parameters or redefine those of its definitions. Directed features may also be bound to indicate the equivalence of their values.

##### Specifying directed features for actions

###### Specifying directed features for actions via the Textual Editor

To specify directed features for actions via the Textual Editor

- In the Textual Editor, within the action's body, specify the needed direction keyword in or out, followed by the needed feature name. actiondefTakePicture{ 
inscene:Scene; // *in* directed feature 
outpicture:Picture; // *out* directed feature}

###### Specifying directed features for actions via the view palette

To specify directed features for actions via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Actions group, click one of the following:
  1. The **in ref** button.
  2. The **out ref** button.
2. Hover the cursor over the view pane and click on the action for which you want to create the element.

###### Specifying directed features for actions via the smart manipulator in a view

To specify directed features for actions via the smart manipulator in a view

- In a view, select an action symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the in ref or out ref command.

##### Binding directed features

Directed features can also bebound. An output parameter of one action usage may be bound to the input parameter of another action usage. Such a binding indicates that the values of the target input parameter will always be the same as the values of the source output parameter. For more information on binding, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Binding' space=''] page.

actiondefTakePicture{ 
inscene:Scene; 
outpicture:Picture;

// binding *in*parameters TakePicture.scene with focus.scene 
bindscene=focus.scene; 
 
actionfocus:Focus{inscene;outimage; } 
firstfocusthenshoot; 
flowfocus.imagetoshoot.image; 
actionshoot:Shoot{inimage;outpicture; } 
 
// binding *out*parameters TakePicture.picture with shoot.picture 
bindpicture=shoot.picture; 
}

The binding of action parameters does not model the case when there is an actual transfer of items between actions. Transfers can be modeled using flows, succession flows as well as send and accept actions. For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Transfers between actions' space=''] page.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Actions may have features with directions in, out or inout that are considered to be parameters of the action. Features with direction in or inout are input parameters, and features with direction out or inout are output parameters. An action usage inherits the parameters of its definitions, if any, and it can also define its own parameters or redefine those of its definitions. Directed features may also be bound to indicate the equivalence of their values.</p><h3>Specifying directed features for actions</h3><h4>Specifying directed features for actions via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To specify directed features for actions via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ul><li>In the Textual Editor, within the action's body, specify the needed direction keyword <em>in<span> </span></em>or <em>out,<span> </span></em>followed by the needed feature name.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" action for which you want to create the element.</li></ol><h4>Specifying directed features for actions via the smart manipulator in a view</h4><p>To specify directed features for actions via the smart manipulator in a view</p><hr /><ul><li>In a view, select an action symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the<strong><span> in ref </span></strong>or<span> </span><strong><span>out ref </span></strong>command.</li></ul><h3>Binding directed features</h3><p>Directed features can also be<span> </span>bound. An output parameter of one action usage may be bound to the input parameter of another action usage. Such a binding indicates that the values of the target input parameter will always be the same as the values of the source output parameter. For more information on binding, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Binding" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Binding]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>
````
