<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254422624 space=SYSML2P version=2 -->
## PAGE 00312: (2026x) Directed features for states

- page_id: `254422624`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254422624/2026x+Directed+features+for+states
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-10-14T15:23:00.764+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Behavior > (2026x) States
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Specify features with directions *in*, *out,*or *inout*for states that act as the parameters of the state. Directed features can also be [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Binding' space=''].

##### Specifying directed features for states

###### Specifying directed features for states via the Textual Editor

To specify directed features for states via the Textual Editor

- In the Textual Editor, within the state's body, specify the needed direction keyword in or out, followed by the needed feature name.

statedefVehicleStates{inoperatingVehicle:Vehicle;}

###### Specifying directed features for states via the smart manipulator in a view

To specify directed features for states via the smart manipulator in a view

- In a view, select a state symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the in ref or out ref command.

###### Specifying directed features for states via the view palette

To specify directed features for states via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Actions group, click one of the following:
  1. The **in ref** button.
  2. The **out ref** button.
2. Hover the cursor over the view pane and click on the state for which you want to create the element.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE


<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254422731 space=SYSML2P version=7 -->
## PAGE 00313: (2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views

- page_id: `254422731`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254422731/2026x+Displaying+elements+in+symbolic+views
- version_number: 7
- version_date: `2025-12-17T15:50:04.693+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Views > (2026x) Displaying elements in views
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can use the Display commands to display element symbols in the graphical representation of a symbolic view.

##### Display commands

| Display command category and its commands | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Nested-view style | The category contains commands that render elements in the nested-view . Owned and inherited features of the selected element symbol are represented as nested symbols. |
| Display Features | The command displays owned and inherited features of any metatype. |
| Display Features Recursively | The command displays all owned and inherited features of any metatype recursively. |
| Display Parts | The command displays owned and inherited parts. |
| Display Parts Recursively | The command displays owned and inherited parts recursively. |
| Display Actions | The command displays owned and inherited actions. |
| Display Actions Recursively | The command displays owned and inherited actions recursively. |
| Display Swimlanes | The command displays owned and inherited swimlane symbols, and any element symbols related to each swimlane.The command is only available for specific and . |
| Display Owned Members | The command displays owned members based on the 'ownedMember' property's value. The Display Others In-Between Relationships command is also enacted.The command is only available for . |
| Display Owned Members Recursively | The command displays owned members recursively based on the 'ownedMember' property's value. The Display Others In-Between Relationships command is also enacted.The command is only available for . |
| Tree-view style | The category contains commands that render elements in the tree-view .Owned and inherited features of the selected element symbol are represented as symbols related via Feature Membership relationship paths.Contains all the same commands as the Nested-view style category, except for Display Actions, Display Actions Recursively, Display Swimlanes.The Display Features command displays connectors between features only in the nested-view style. |
| Relationships | The category contains commands for displaying relationship paths of the selected symbol. |
| Display Connectors | The command displays all types of connectors (connection, interface, succession, binding, etc.) that exist between the selected element symbol and any others displayed in the view. Connector paths can also be . |
| Display Specializations | The command displays all types of specializations (subclassification, feature typing, subsetting, etc.) that exist between the selected element symbol and any others displayed in the view. |
| Display Others | The command displays other relationships (annotation, dependency, type featuring, import, and all memberships) that exist between the selected element symbol and any others displayed in the view. |
| In-Between Relationships | The category contains commands for displaying relationship paths only between the selected element symbols.Contains all the same commands as the Relationships category.This category is available only when at least two symbols are selected in a view. |
| Other | The category contains commands for displaying any other element symbols. |
| Display Ports | The command displays owned and inherited ports.The command's availability is element metatype-dependent. |
| Display Parameters | The command displays owned and inherited directional features.The command's availability is element metatype-dependent. |
| Display Ends | The command displays end segments of the n-ary connection and the n-ary interface symbols.The command is only available for . |
| Display Exposed Elements | The command displays elements exposed for the view. Exposed elements depend on the (e.g., recursivity) and . The command is available when invoked for the diagram pane or a .This command is also available via the hyperlink in the notification displayed on any symbolic view containing an . |

#### NOTE: Recursive commands

Recursive commands

Recursive commands are indicated by the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] recursive command button displayed next to the non-recursive command.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Display Parts command is used to display parts owned by the *drone in SAR mission*part. On the left, the command is executed in the nested-view style, while the right shows the tree-view style.

##### Displaying element symbols in a diagram with symbol(s)Displaying element symbols in a view

There must be at least one element symbol already displayed in a view for the Display commands to be available (except for the Display Exposed Elements command).

###### Displaying element symbols

To display element symbols

1. In a view, select the specific symbol(s) whose features, connectors, etc., you want to display. To select multiple symbols, use the following shortcuts...Hold Shift+Left Mouse Button on each symbol to select multiple symbols at once.Press Ctrl+A to select all symbols on the docked symbol.Select a symbol in the view and press Ctrl+Alt+B to select sibling symbols.Select a symbol in the view and press Ctrl+Alt+S to select symbols of the same type.Select a symbol in the view and press Ctrl+Alt+C to select connected symbols.Hold down the Left Mouse Button anywhere in the view pane and drag to select multiple symbols.Use the **Select**buttons in the **Select**option group in the view panel.
2. Do one of the following:
  1. Click the Display button [ATTACHMENT filename='display_button.png'] in the view toolbar. Click the appropriate display command in the dropdown menu.
  2. Right-click the element symbol(s). In the shortcut menu, select Display . Click the appropriate display command in the submenu.

##### Displaying connectors in a view

In a view, a connector (connection, bind, flow, interface, succession, succession flow) can be displayed as a path symbol using the**Display Connectors**command. See theprocedure.

###### Proxy connectors

Nested elements that are connected via connectors may be displayed via proxy connector points. The proxy connector point symbol at the intersection between an element symbol and a connector indicates that the element to which the connector is connected is nested deeper within the element to which the proxy connector point symbol is attached. The connector label indicates the element to which the connector is connected via feature chain.

50%

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

50%

partwheelHubAssembly:WheelHubAssembly{ 
partlugBoltJoints:LugBoltJoint[0..5]; 
partwheel:WheelAssembly[1] { 
partw:Wheel[1] { 
partrim:TireMountingRim[2]; 
partmountingHoles:LugBoltMountingHole[5]; 
} 
} 
connect[0..1]lugBoltJointsto[1]wheel.w.mountingHoles; 
}

###### In the textual notation, a connection connects two parts: part *lugBoltJoints*and part *mountingHoles* nested within part *w*, which is nested within the part *wheel*. 
Since the nested part symbols*w*and*mountingHoles*are not displayed in the view, the proxy connector path is drawn between*lugBoltJoints*and*wheel*, which is a displayed parent element symbol of*w*and*mountingHoles.*To represent that the connector is connected to a nested element, a proxy connector point symbol is displayed at the intersection of the part*wheel*and the connector path.A connector label with the*w.mountingHoles*feature chain informs you which element symbol the connector is connected to.

###### Elaborating a connector

The connector path symbol can be additionally elaborated for improved visualization of the connector and its related elements.

To elaborate/simplify a connector

1. Do one of the following:
  1. To elaborate a connector in a view, click the connector symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the Elaborate [IMAGE alt='' src=''] command. The connector is elaborated.
  2. To simplfiy a connector into a path symbol only, click the connector symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the Simplify [IMAGE alt='' src=''] command. The connector is simplified into a path symbol only.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Displaying n-ary connectors in a view

In addition to binary connectors, you can also have n-ary connectors. An n-ary connector is a complex connector between three or more elements. The n-ary connector is displayed as a dot symbol with segments connecting the ends of the connector.

The following binary connectors can also be n-ary:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) N-ary connection' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) N-ary interface' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Derive requirement' space='']

A [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Dependency' space=''] can also be n-ary or binary. However, as it is not a connector, the following procedures do not apply to it. Dependencies can be displayed as paths for the n-ary dot symbol via the **Display Others**command.

50%

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The image displays an unnamed binary derivation where the requirement *heating & cooling*is the original and*heating*is the derived one. The original and derived ends are connected with the connection path.

50%

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The image displays an unnamed n-ary derivation where the requirement *heating & cooling*is the original and *heating*is the derived one.The original and derived ends are connected with the connection path.

###### Displaying n-ary dot and its ends in a view

To display n-ary dot and its ends in a view

- Select the containing element symbol and click the Display command, then in its dropdown menu click Display Features . [ATTACHMENT filename='Displaying n-ary dot and its ends in a view.png']

###### Displaying n-ary connector ends in a view

To display n-ary connector ends in a view

- Select the n-ary dot symbol in the view, then click the Display command. In its dropdown menu, click Display Ends . [ATTACHMENT filename='Displaying n-ary connector ends in a view.png']

###### Displaying n-ary connectors for an element symbol in a view

To display n-ary connectors for an element symbol in a view

- Select the symbol whose n-ary connectors you want to display. Click the Display command, then Display Connectors in the Relationships category. [ATTACHMENT filename='Displaying n-ary connectors for an element symbol in a view.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">You can use the Display commands to display element symbols in the graphical representation of a symbolic view. </span></p><h3>Display commands</h3></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th scope="col">Display command category <br />and its commands</th><th scope="col">Description</th></tr><tr><td class="highlight-grey" data-highlight-colour="grey"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);" title=""><strong>Nested-view style</strong></span></td><td class="highlight-grey" data-highlight-colour="grey"><p title=""><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The category contains commands that render elements in the nested-view <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Rendering views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[rendering style]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. </span></p><p title=""><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">O</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">wned and inherited features of the selected element symbol are represented as nested symbols.</span></p></td></tr><tr><td style="margin-left: 80.0px;">   Display Features</td><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The command displays owned and inherited features of any metatype.</span></p></td></tr><tr><td>   Display Features Recursively</td><td><p>The command displays all owned and inherited features of any metatype recursively.</p></td></tr><tr><td>   Display Parts</td><td><span>The command displays owned and inherited parts.</span></td></tr><tr><td>   Display Parts Recursively</td><td><span>The command displays owned and inherited parts recursively.</span></td></tr><tr><td>   Display Actions</td><td><p><span>The command displays owned and inherited actions.</span></p></td></tr><tr><td>   Display Actions Recursively</td><td><p><span>The command displays owned and inherited actions recursively.</span></p></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">   Display Swimlanes</span></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The command displays owned and inherited swimlane symbols, and any element symbols related to each swimlane.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="75370ada-85bc-4336-86ba-5fbc76e5cd2b"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The command is only available for specific <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Modeling actions with swimlanes" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[perform actions]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Use case scenario" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[include use cases]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">   Display Owned Members</span></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span>The command displays owned members based on the 'ownedMember' property's value. </span></span></p><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span>The <strong>Display Others</strong> In-Between Relationships command is also enacted.</span></span></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7d1d6f8c-37b8-4550-826d-1978ee913a15"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span>The command is only available for <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Package" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[packages]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">   Display Owned Members Recursively</span></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span>The command displays owned members recursively based on the 'ownedMember' property's value. </span></span></p><ul><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Display Others</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> In-Between Relationships command is also enacted.</span></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c94082d4-7e6b-409e-9d3d-da42cc1774fd"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span>The command is only available for <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Package" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[packages]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-grey" data-highlight-colour="grey"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);" title=""><strong>Tree-view style</strong></span></td><td class="highlight-grey" data-highlight-colour="grey"><p title=""><span>The category contains commands that render elements in the tree-view </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Rendering views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[rendering style]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span>.</span></p><p title=""><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">O</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">wned and inherited features of the selected element symbol are represented as symbols related via Feature Membership relationship paths.</span></p><ul title=""><li data-uuid="6c51ea38-75c0-4f4f-98de-b673abe64f18"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Contains all the same commands as the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);" title=""><strong>Nested-view style </strong>category, except for Display Actions, Display Actions Recursively, Display Swimlanes.</span></span></li><li data-uuid="a943e155-e603-435d-8ab2-29b9ef148836"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The <strong>Display Features</strong> command displays connectors between features only in the nested-view style.</span></li></ul></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-grey" data-highlight-colour="grey"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);" title=""><strong>Relationships</strong></span></td><td class="highlight-grey" data-highlight-colour="grey"><span title="">The category contains commands for displaying relationship paths of the selected symbol.</span></td></tr><tr><td>   Display Connectors</td><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The command displays all types of connectors (connection, interface, succession, binding, etc.) that exist between the selected element symbol and any others displayed in the view. </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Connector paths can also be </span><ac:link ac:anchor="Elaborating a connector"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[elaborated]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">.</span></p></td></tr><tr><td>   Display Specializations</td><td><span>The command displays all types of specializations (subclassification, feature typing, subsetting, etc.) that exist <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">between the selected element symbol and any others displayed in the view</span>.</span></td></tr><tr><td>   Display Others</td><td><span>The command displays other relationships (annotation, dependency, type featuring, import, and all memberships) that exist between the selected element symbol and any others displayed in the view.</span></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-grey" data-highlight-colour="grey"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);" title=""><strong>In-Between Relationships</strong></span></td><td class="highlight-grey" data-highlight-colour="grey"><p title=""><span>The category contains commands for displaying relationship paths only between the selected element symbols.</span></p><ul title=""><li data-uuid="33ab12ff-5f17-4e76-b389-d5a7f8643c77"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);" title="">Contains all the same commands as the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);" title=""><strong>Relationships</strong> category.</span></span></li><li data-uuid="f5f2bab8-0bda-4461-8ff3-b19bd11fcac7"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);" title="">This category is available only when at least two symbols are selected in a view.</span></li></ul></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-grey" data-highlight-colour="grey"><strong title="">Other</strong></td><td class="highlight-grey" data-highlight-colour="grey"><span title="">The category contains commands for displaying any other element symbols.</span></td></tr><tr><td>   Display Ports</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span>The command displays owned and inherited ports.</span></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ea0381f5-1e71-42b5-8879-4601ea095343"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>The command's availability is element metatype-dependent.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td>   Display Parameters </td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span>The command displays owned and inherited directional features.</span></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="71db2b8d-7fa0-4306-8ff7-8971ca47c54b"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>The command's availability is element metatype-dependent.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td>   Display Ends</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The command displays end segments of the n-ary connection and the n-ary interface symbols.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="18987458-ba47-4c34-847d-ee44388c3570"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The command is only available for <ac:link ac:anchor="Displaying n-ary connectors in a view"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[n-ary dot symbols]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td><span>   Display Exposed Elements</span></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span>The command displays elements exposed for the view. <span>Exposed elements depend on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Exposing elements for views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[exposition kind]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (e.g., recursivity) and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Filtering elements for views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[filters]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. </span></span></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7866e435-7592-4369-ade6-4bcb219fed2e"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="4acb477e-c9ee-4f2b-9122-83b716c28268"><span>The command is available when invoked for the diagram pane or a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Docked symbols" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[docked symbol]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li data-uuid="18dd3bff-bbf5-408d-a8d2-3c366eeee6eb"><p>This command is also available via the hyperlink in the notification displayed on any symbolic view containing an <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Exposing elements for views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[expose relationship]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="display exposed elements notification.png" /></ac:image></p></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr></tbody></table><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0f9bdb1c-48d3-4406-b1a5-fc676442b857"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Recursive commands</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Recursive commands are indicated by the <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="recursive_button.png" /></ac:image> recursive command button displayed next to the non-recursive command.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="recursive_cmd.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_commands.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The Display Parts command is used to display parts owned by the <em>drone in SAR mission </em>part. On the left, the command is executed in the nested-view style, while the right shows the tree-view style.</h6><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fd72916b-65f7-44c1-ba2b-3b9ed1c54c30"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Displaying element symbols in a diagram with symbol(s)</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Displaying element symbols in a view</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="722af941-19b0-4426-a909-ef6eb0ae1b9b"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">There must be at least one element symbol already displayed in a view for the Display commands to be available (except for the Display Exposed Elements command).</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4><span style="font-weight: 600;letter-spacing: -0.003em;">Displaying element symbols</span></h4><p>To display element symbols</p><hr /><ol><li class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">In a view, select the specific symbol(s) whose features, connectors, etc., you want to display.<br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f1c2dfbd-2cfc-428a-b545-87f6715e5c90"><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0f41dad1-5ad0-45d3-a18e-743b57ba085f"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">To select multiple symbols, use the following shortcuts...</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ol><li>Hold Shift+Left Mouse Button on each symbol to select multiple symbols at once.</li><li>Press Ctrl+A to select all symbols on the docked symbol. </li><li>Select a symbol in the view and press Ctrl+Alt+B to select sibling symbols.</li><li>Select a symbol in the view and press Ctrl+Alt+S to select symbols of the same type.</li><li>Select a symbol in the view and press Ctrl+Alt+C to select connected symbols.</li><li>Hold down the Left Mouse Button anywhere in the view pane and drag to select multiple symbols.</li><li>Use the <strong>Select </strong>buttons in the <strong>Select </strong>option group in the view panel.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Do one of the following:</span></p><ol><li>Click the <strong>Display</strong> button <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_button.png" /></ac:image> in the view toolbar. Click the appropriate display command in the dropdown menu.</li><li>Right-click the element symbol(s). In the shortcut menu, select <strong>Display</strong>. Click the appropriate display command in the submenu.</li></ol></li></ol><h3>Displaying connectors in a view</h3><p>In a view, a connector (connection, bind, flow, interface, succession, succession flow) can be displayed as a path symbol <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">using the </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Display Connectors </strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">command. S</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">ee the </span><ac:link ac:anchor="Displaying element symbols" /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> procedure.</span></p><h4>Proxy connectors</h4><p>Nested elements that are connected via connectors may be displayed via proxy connector points. The proxy connector point symbol at the intersection between an element symbol and a connector indicates that the element to which the connector is connected is nested deeper within the element to which the proxy connector point symbol is attached. The connector label indicates the element to which the connector is connected via feature chain.</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="section" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0b62cb38-d1ca-4efd-af82-be2fb80f2bda"><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a4d6bd1e-49db-493e-8901-bc61ef25e8c4"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Proxy_connectors.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="67c97922-2190-4b14-89bb-ff9834d45c70"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="544bb35c-59cb-48bf-978e-4d57ff4349f6"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">wheelHubAssembly</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">WheelHubAssembly</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">lugBoltJoints</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">LugBoltJoint</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> [</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">0</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">.5</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">];</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">wheel</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">WheelAssembly</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> [</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">1</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">] {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">w</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Wheel</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> [</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">1</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">] {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">rim</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">TireMountingRim</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> [</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">2</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">];</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">mountingHoles</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">LugBoltMountingHole</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> [</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">5</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">];</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">connect</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> [<span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">0</span>.<span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">.1</span>] </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">lugBoltJoints</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">to</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> [<span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">1</span>] </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">wheel</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">w</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">mountingHoles</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h6 style="text-align: center;">In the textual notation, a connection connects two parts: part <em>lugBoltJoints </em>and part <em>mountingHoles</em> nested within part <em>w</em>, which is nested within the part <em>wheel</em>.<br /><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Since the nested part symbols </span><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">w</em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> and </span><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">mountingHoles </em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">are not displayed in the view, the proxy connector path is drawn between </span><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">lugBoltJoints </em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">and </span><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">wheel</em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">, which is a displayed parent element symbol of </span><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">w</em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> and </span><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">mountingHoles.<br /></em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">To represent that the connector is connected to a nested element, a proxy connector point symbol is displayed at the intersection of the part </span><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">wheel </em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">and the connector path.<br /></span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">A connector label with the </span><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">w.mountingHoles</em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> feature chain informs you which element symbol the connector is connected to. </span></h6><h4><span style="letter-spacing: -0.003em;">Elaborating a connector</span></h4><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">The connector path symbol can be additionally elaborated for improved visualization of the connector and its related elements.</span></p><p>To elaborate/simplify a connector</p><hr /><ol><li>Do one of the following:<ol><li>To elaborate a connector in a view, click the connector symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the <strong>Elaborate <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="elaborate_button.png" /></ac:image><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"> </span></strong>command. The connector is elaborated.</li><li>To simplfiy a connector into a path symbol only, click the connector symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the <strong>Simplify <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="simplify_button.png" /></ac:image> </strong>command. The connector is simplified into a path symbol only.</li></ol></li></ol><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Elaborating_a_connector.png" /></ac:image></p><h3 class="auto-cursor-target">Displaying n-ary connectors in a view</h3><p>In addition to binary connectors, you can also have n-ary connectors. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">An n-ary connector is a complex connector between three or more elements. The n-ary connector is displayed as a dot symbol with segments connecting the ends of the connector.</span></p><p>The following binary connectors can also be n-ary:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) N-ary connection" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Connection]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) N-ary interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Interface]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Derive requirement" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Derivation]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="13f43002-a869-49a2-b0fa-21b855d3bb07"><ac:rich-text-body><p>A <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Dependency" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[dependency]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> can also be n-ary or binary. However, as it is not a connector, the following procedures do not apply to it. Dependencies can be displayed as paths for the n-ary dot symbol via the <strong>Display Others </strong>command.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="section" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5e6bbe1f-1458-4bc1-9725-027fceba27c9"><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7d233dd2-1b3c-4eb4-80f0-62615daa7a7c"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="binary_derivation.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The image displays an unnamed binary derivation where the requirement <em>heating &amp; cooling </em><span>is the original and </span><em>heating </em><span>is the derived one. The original and derived ends are connected with the connection path.</span></h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2098e62f-9f19-4b4c-b8a1-ca39d95bd91f"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="n-ary_derivation.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The image displays an unnamed n-ary derivation where the requirement <em>heating &amp; cooling </em><span>is the original and <em>heating </em>is the derived one. </span><span>The original and derived ends are connected with the connection path.</span></h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h4>Displaying n-ary dot and its ends in a view</h4><p>To display n-ary dot and its ends in a view</p><hr /><ul><li>Select the containing element symbol and click the <strong>Display </strong>command, then in its dropdown menu click <strong>Display Features</strong>. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Displaying n-ary dot and its ends in a view.png" /></ac:image></li></ul><h4>Displaying n-ary connector ends in a view</h4><p>To display n-ary connector ends in a view</p><hr /><ul><li>Select the n-ary dot symbol in the view, then click the <strong>Display</strong> command. In its dropdown menu, click <strong>Display Ends</strong>. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Displaying n-ary connector ends in a view.png" /></ac:image></li></ul><h4>Displaying n-ary connectors for an element symbol in a view</h4><p>To display n-ary connectors for an element symbol in a view</p><hr /><ul><li>Select the symbol whose n-ary connectors you want to display. Click the <strong>Display </strong>command, then <strong>Display Connectors</strong> in the<span> </span><strong>Relationships<span> </span></strong>category.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Displaying n-ary connectors for an element symbol in a view.png" /></ac:image></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254422770 space=SYSML2P version=3 -->
## PAGE 00314: (2026x) Displaying elements in tabular views

- page_id: `254422770`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254422770/2026x+Displaying+elements+in+tabular+views
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2026-01-15T18:38:35.224+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Views > (2026x) Displaying elements in views
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Display elements in diagrammatic representations of tabular views.

##### Displaying elements in tabular views

To display elements in tabular views

1. Ensure the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating tabular views' space=''] view has:
  1. Tabular [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Rendering views' space=''] .
  2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Exposing elements for views' space=''] .
  3. (Optional) [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Filtering elements for views' space=''] .
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Opening views' space=''] the view. The exposed and filtered elements are displayed in the tabular view.
3. (Optional) To reflect any changes in the model, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='refresh_table_button.png'] Refresh button in the view toolbar to update the view.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Display elements in diagrammatic representations of tabular views.</p><h3>Displaying elements in tabular views</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To display elements in tabular views</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Ensure the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating tabular views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[created]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> view has:<ol><li>Tabular <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Rendering views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[rendering]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Exposing elements for views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Expose relationship]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. </li><li>(Optional) <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Filtering elements for views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Filters]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li></ol></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Opening views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> the view. The exposed and filtered elements are displayed in the tabular view.</li><li>(Optional) To reflect any changes in the model, click the <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="refresh_table_button.png" /></ac:image> <strong>Refresh</strong> button in the view toolbar to update the view.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254423103 space=SYSML2P version=2 -->
## PAGE 00316: (2026x) Docked symbols

- page_id: `254423103`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254423103/2026x+Docked+symbols
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-10-14T15:11:38.850+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model management > (2026x) Working with views > (2026x) View kinds > (2026x) Graphical views > (2026x) Symbolic views > (2026x) Working with symbols
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In SysML v2, an element symbol can be docked to a view, acting as a context and a kind of frame for a view.

**Docked symbol details:**

- The element represented as the docked symbol is the owner of elements whose symbols are contained by the docked symbol.
- Element symbols cannot be created outside the docked symbols' shape in the view pane. Creating element symbols outside the docked symbol's frame expands the frame to contain the new symbols.
- A view can have only one docked symbol.
- The docked element symbol can be undocked and vice versa. See the procedures below.
- Executing the 'Select in Containment tree' command:
  - on the docked symbol's body - selects the corresponding view.
  - on the docked symbol's header - selects the corresponding element .

##### Docking a symbol to a view

To dock a symbol to a view

- To create a view with a docked symbol:
  - In the Containment tree, right-click a usage or a definition element and in the shortcut menu, click **Create view**.
- To dock a symbol displayed in a view:
  - Select the element symbol in the view and click the **Dock** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button on the corner of the element frame. The **Dock**button is available only if the view displays one element symbol (e.g., the docking option is not available if there are two part usage symbols displayed in the view).

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Docking a symbol to a view

##### Undocking a symbol from a view

To undock a symbol from a view

- Right-click the header of the docked symbol and in the shortcut menu, select **Refactor**, then **Undock**. Right-clicking anywhere but the header of the docked symbol will not display the **Undock**command in the shortcut menu.Once you undock a symbol, it remains the same size, taking up the entire view pane. Click the **Make Preferred Size** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button to shrink the symbol, allowing for more space in the view pane.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Undocking a symbol from a view

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254423036 space=SYSML2P version=5 -->
## PAGE 00317: (2026x) Drag and Drop handler

- page_id: `254423036`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254423036/2026x+Drag+and+Drop+handler
- version_number: 5
- version_date: `2025-12-17T15:03:36.476+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Element management > (2026x) Working with elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Dragging and dropping elements or element symbols enables an efficient modeling practice. Using the Drag and Drop handler functionality allows you to easily perform feature typing, subsetting, element creation, and other modeling commands.

##### Using the Drag and Drop handler

To use the Drag and Drop handler

1. Do one of the following:
  1. Select the element(s) in the Containment tree.
  2. In a view, select an element symbol and in its smart manipulator, click and hold the Drag and Drop handler [ATTACHMENT filename='drag_and_drop_handler.png'] . Dragging and dropping the symbol itself instead of the Drag and Drop handler [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Element ownership change' space='']. To take advantage of the functionalities provided by the Drag and Drop handler, make sure to drag the handler, not the symbol.
2. Drag and drop the element/symbol on:
  1. The target element symbol in a view.
  2. The appropriate field in the Specification panel. You can only drag an element symbol onto another element symbol in a view or the appropriate field in the Specification panel, never onto an element in the Containment tree. The latter modifies the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Element ownership change' space=''].You can also drag an element symbol from one view and drop it onto an element symbol on another open view. Drag the symbol while holding down the Drag and Drop handler over the target view tab to display the open view.For a list of all fields that can be specified via drag and drop in the Specification panel, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification panel' space=''] page.
3. The Drag and Drop selection menu appears. Click the appropriate command in the menu.

##### Drag and Drop handler commands

Once you drag and drop an element/symbol onto another symbol, the drag and drop selection menu appears. It displays multiple commands whose availability depends on the source (the dragged element/symbol) and target (the symbol on which the dragged source is dropped). See the table below for available commands based on source and target element symbols.

| Command name | Source | Target |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Set TypeIf an element already has a set type, performing the Set Type command again replaces the set type. To set an additional type, hold the CTRL key while executing the command. | Any definition element | Any usage element |
| Set Payload | Any definition element | Flow Connection |
| Set Performed Action | Action | Perform Action |
| Set Use Case Included | Use Case | Include Use Case |
| Create Perform Action | Action, Action def | Part, Action, State, Part def, Action def, State def |
| Create Exhibit State | State, State def | Part, State, Action, Part def, State def, Action def |
| Create Include Use Case | Use Case, Use Case def | Part, Use Case, Part def, Use Case def |
| Create Objective / Create Require Constraint of Objective | Constraint, Constraint def | Case, Case def |
| Create Actor | Part, Part def | Case, Requirement, Case def, Requirement def |
| Create Stakeholder | Part, Part def | Requirement, Requirement def |
| Create Frame Concern | Concern, Concern def | Requirement, Requirement def |
| Create Require Constraint | Constraint, Constraint def | Requirement, Requirement def |
| Create Assume Constraint | Constraint, Constraint def | Requirement, Requirement def |
| Create Assert Constraint | Constraint, Constraint def | Requirement, Requirement def |
| Create Satisfy Requirement | Requirement, Requirement def | Part |
| Create Subject | Any usage element,Any definition element | Case, Requirement, Case def, Requirement def |
| Apply Metadata | Metadata def | Any element |
| Create Usage | Any definition element | Any element |
| Create Subsetting Usage | Any usage element | Any element |
| Set Referenced Feature | Constraint | Assume, Require, Assert constraint |
| Concern | Frame concern |  |
| Requirement | Satisfy requirement |  |
| Set Subsetted Feature | Any usage element | Any usage element |
| Set Redefined Feature | Any usage element | Any usage element |
| Set Value | Any feature | Any feature |
| Set Superclassifier | Any definition element | Any definition element |
| Create Symbol | Any element | Any element |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Dragging and dropping elements or element symbols enables an efficient modeling practice. Using the Drag and Drop handler functionality allows you to easily perform feature typing, subsetting, element creation, and other modeling commands. </p><h3>Using the Drag and Drop handler</h3><p>To use the Drag and Drop handler</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="47025575-d93b-455c-98d9-595c7250a966">Do one of the following:<ol><li>Select the element(s) in the Containment tree.</li><li>In a view, select an element symbol and in its smart manipulator, click and hold the <strong>Drag and Drop</strong> handler <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="drag_and_drop_handler.png" /></ac:image>.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8ad23bda-3eee-4c7a-a876-604aa6d7d064"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Dragging and dropping the symbol itself instead of the Drag and Drop handler <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Element ownership change" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[changes the element's ownership]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. To take advantage of the functionalities provided by the Drag and Drop handler, make sure to drag the handler, not the symbol<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="5c8a98aa-7810-4cf9-9a80-7e8df65eca68"><p class="auto-cursor-target">Drag and drop the element/symbol on:</p><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">The target element symbol in a view.</p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">The appropriate field in the Specification panel.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a919802e-8de9-48f5-a232-551e6eb9441a"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>You can only drag an element symbol onto another element symbol in a view or the appropriate field in the Specification panel, never onto an element in the Containment tree. The latter modifies the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Element ownership change" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[element's ownership]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>You can also drag an element symbol from one view and drop it onto an element symbol on another open view. Drag the symbol while holding down the Drag and Drop handler over the target view tab to display the open view.</li><li>For a list of all fields that can be specified via drag and drop in the Specification panel, see the <ac:link ac:anchor="Modifying additional properties via the Specification panel"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Specification panel" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specification panel]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="52d2f91d-e275-4852-bc82-585ac7237ade">The Drag and Drop selection menu appears. Click the appropriate command in the menu.</li></ol><h3>Drag and Drop handler commands</h3><p>Once you drag and drop an element/symbol onto another symbol, the drag and drop selection menu appears. It displays multiple commands whose availability depends on the source (the dragged element/symbol) and target (the symbol on which the dragged source is dropped). See the table below for available commands based on source and target element symbols. </p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th scope="col">Command name</th><th scope="col">Source</th><th scope="col">Target</th></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Set Type</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="69ef6f62-0f40-4d4b-9c97-e73cdc85e5f6"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">If an element already has a set type, performing the <strong>Set Type</strong> command again replaces the set type. <br />To set an additional type, hold the CTRL key while executing the command. </span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td><td>Any definition element</td><td>Any usage element</td></tr><tr><td>Set Payload</td><td>Any definition element</td><td>Flow Connection</td></tr><tr><td>Set Performed Action</td><td>Action</td><td>Perform Action</td></tr><tr><td>Set Use Case Included</td><td>Use Case</td><td>Include Use Case</td></tr><tr><td>Create Perform Action</td><td>Action, Action def</td><td>Part, Action, State, Part def, Action Use Case def</td><td>Part, Use Case, Part def, Use Case def</td></tr><tr><td>Create Objective / <br />Create Require Constraint of Objective</td><td>Constraint, Constraint def</td><td>Case, Case def</td></tr><tr><td>Create Actor</td><td>Part, Part def</td><td>Case, Requirement, Case def, Requirement def</td></tr><tr><td>Create Stakeholder</td><td>Part, Part def</td><td>Requirement, element</td></tr><tr><td>Create Usage</td><td>Any definition element</td><td>Any element</td></tr><tr><td>Create Subsetting Usage</td><td>Any usage element</td><td>Any element</td></tr><tr><td rowspan="3">Set Referenced Feature</td><td>Constraint</td><td>Assume, Require, Assert constraint</td></tr><tr><td>Concern</td><td>Frame concern</td></tr><tr><td>Requirement</td><td>Satisfy requirement</td></tr><tr><td>Set Subsetted Feature</td><td>Any usage element</td><td>Any usage element</td></tr><tr><td>Set Redefined Feature</td><td>Any usage element</td><td>Any usage <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254421987 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
````
## PAGE 00318: (2026x) Editor Tabs

- page_id: `254421987`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254421987/2026x+Editor+Tabs
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-12T14:33:03.920+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Project management > (2026x) Modeling tool environment > (2026x) User interface
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Editor tabs allow you to access views, textual editor, and model samples.

##### Editor tab types

The modeling tool contains the following editor tabs:

- Welcome tab. The welcome tab is displayed once the modeling tool is launched. It allows you to create new or open other projects, view model samples provided with the modeling tool installation, etc.
- Samples tab. It allows you to display the model samples provided with the modeling tool installation.
- View Editor tab. It allows you to display and work with view. You can have multiple view tabs open simultaneously.
- Textual Editor tab. It allows you to display the model in textual notation view and further develop the model using textual notation.
- Specification tab. It allows you to review and modify properties of specific elements.

##### Editor tabs commands

Editor tabs' commands can be found:

- In the Main Menu , click Window , then Editor Tabs . In the dropdown menu, view and select the appropriate command.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Editor tabs allow you to access views, textual editor, and model samples.</p><h3><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="c54f6dbc-5c61-4b6c-a314-2ad6f152913a">Editor tab types</ac:inline-comment-marker></h3><p>The modeling tool contains the following editor tabs:</p><ul><li>Welcome tab.<br />The welcome tab is displayed once the modeling tool is launched. It allows you to create new or open other projects, view model samples provided with the modeling tool installation, etc.</li><li>Samples tab.<br />It allows you to display the model samples provided with the modeling tool installation.</li><li>View Editor tab. <br />It allows you to display and work with view. You can have multiple view tabs open simultaneously.</li><li>Textual Editor tab. <br />It allows you to display the model in textual notation view and further develop the model using textual notation.</li><li data-uuid="975212db-3869-4ac2-9eec-3ae21f774437">Specification tab.<br />It allows you to review and modify properties of specific elements.  </li></ul><h3>Editor tabs commands</h3><p>Editor tabs' commands can be found:</p><ul><li>In the <strong>Main Menu</strong>, click <strong>Window</strong>, then <strong>Editor Tabs</strong>. In the dropdown menu, view and select the appropriate command.</li></ul><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254422799 space=SYSML2P version=3 -->
## PAGE 00320: (2026x) Element names

- page_id: `254422799`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254422799/2026x+Element+names
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-10-22T09:52:46.908+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Element management > (2026x) Working with elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

See the information on this page for naming elements.

For information on name resolution (i.e., feature chaining and qualified names), see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing textual notation' space=''] section on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing textual notation' space=''] page.

- Declared name
  - In addition to the keyword indicating element kind, the declaration of an element may also specify a declared name. The declared name should be fully descriptive, particularly in the context of the element definition. Declared names must be represented with a specific lexical structure when written in the textual notation. For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing textual notation' space=''] section on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing textual notation' space=''] page. part'LED Flashlight' { // unrestricted element name that includes whitespace 
 partbulb; // basic element name without whitespace 
}

- Declared short name
  - The declared short name, if given, should be an abbreviated name useful for referring to the element. The declared short name is distinguished by being surrounded by the delimiting characters < and >. The declared short name, if used, must precede the declared name. part <cyl> cylinders; // a part usage with the declared short name <cyl> and declared name cylindersrequirement<'1'>fullVehicleMassLimit:MassRequirement; // a requirement with the declared short name <'1'>, which is also considered its requirement ID.

- Name and short name
  - If neither a declared name or a declared short name are given in the declaration of a usage, then it is determined by the name or short name of the redefined usage. Note that <> symbols should not be used when indicating the short name. part def Engine { 
 part <cyl> cylinders : Cylinder [2 ..*]; 
} 
part def FourCylinderEngine :> Engine { 
 part redefines cylinders [4]; // Engine::cylinders is redefined with a new usage, whose declared name or short name is empty, but its name is "cylinders". 
// OR 
 part redefines cyl[4]; // Engine::cylinders is redefined with a new usage, whose declared name or short name is empty, but its short name is "cyl". Note that <> symbols should not be used when indicating the short name. 
}

##### Specifying the declared name

###### Specifying the declared name via the Textual Editor

To specify the declared name via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, follow the element keyword or declared short name with the needed basic or unrestricted name. Declared names must be represented with a specific lexical structure when written in the textual notation. For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing textual notation' space=''] section on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing textual notation' space=''] page.
2. Click the Synchronize button. part 'LED Flashlight'{ // unrestricted element name that includes whitespace 
 part bulb; // basic element name without whitespace 
}

###### Specifying the declared name via the Specification Panel

To specify the declared name via the Specification Panel

- See the procedure [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification panel' space=''] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification panel' space=''] page.

###### Specifying the declared name via the Containment tree

To specify the declared name via the Containment tree

- In the Containment tree, double-click an element and type the needed name.

###### Specifying the declared name via the element symbol in a view

To specify the declared name via the element symbol in a view

- On a symbol on view, double-click the name line under the element keyword and type the needed name.

##### Specifying the declared short name

###### Specifying the declared short name via the Textual Editor

To specify the declared short name via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, follow the element keyword with the <> symbols with the needed short name within them.
2. Click the Synchronize button. partdefEngine{ 
part<cyl>cylinders:Cylinder[2..*]; // a part usage with the declared short name <cyl> and declared name cylinders 
}part def FourCylinderEngine :> Engine {part redefines cyl[4]; // Engine::cylinders is redefined with a new usage, whose declared name or short name is empty, but its short name is "cyl". Note that <> symbols should not be used when indicating the short name. 
}

###### Specifying the declared short name via the Specification Panel

To specify the declared short name via the Specification Panel

- See the procedure [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification panel' space=''] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification panel' space=''] page.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// a part usage with the declared short name &lt;cyl&gt; and declared name cylinders</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">FourCylinderEngine</span> :&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Engine</span> {<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><br />    <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">redefines</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">cyl</span>[<span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">4</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">];  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// Engine::cylinders is redefined with a new usage, whose declared name or short name is empty, but its short name is &quot;cyl&quot;. Note that &lt;&gt; symbols should not be used when indicating the short name.</span></span></span></span><br />}</span></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h4>Specifying the declared short name via the Specification Panel</h4><p>To specify the declared short name via the Specification Panel</p><hr /><ul><li>See the procedure<span> </span><ac:link ac:anchor="Modifying the declared short name of the element via the Specification panel"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Specification panel" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Modifying the declared short name of the element via the Specification panel]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> on the<span> </span><ac:link ac:anchor="Modifying the declared short name of the element via the Specification panel"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Specification panel" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specification panel]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page. </li></ul><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254422448 space=SYSML2P version=3 -->
## PAGE 00321: (2026x) Element navigation

- page_id: `254422448`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254422448/2026x+Element+navigation
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-12-03T08:48:37.768+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Element management
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Use the Go To command in the element shortcut menu to navigate from one element to its related elements based on their relationships. In the Go To submenu, the related elements are grouped into categories indicating the relationships between elements, such as Flow, Connection, Type, Subsetted Feature, Redefined Feature, etc.

##### Navigating elements via the Go To command

To navigate elements via the Go To command

Do one of the following:

- Right-click an element in the Containment tree or an element symbol on a view . In the shortcut menu, click the Go To command, and in its submenu, click the needed element.
- Select an element in the Containment tree or an element symbol on a view . In the main menu, click Analyze , then Go To . In the submenu, click the needed element. The element is selected in the Containment tree. If the **Go To**submenu contains more than twenty elements, it displays the **Related Elements** command that opens a submenu with the full related elements list.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Go To command's submenu displays elements related to the *vccu*element. Clicking the *'part vccu'* element under the Subsetting Feature category displays the *vccu*part element that subsets the *vccu*element for which the command was invoked.

##### Navigating elements via the Navigation tab

- See the procedure [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification panel' space=''] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification panel' space=''] page.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span>Use the Go To command in the element shortcut menu to navigate from one element to its related elements based on their relationships. In the Go To submenu, the related elements are grouped into categories indicating the relationships between elements, such as Flow, Connection, <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Type, Subsetted Feature, Redefined Feature, etc.</span></span></p><h3><span>Navigating elements via the Go To command</span></h3><p><span>To navigate elements via the Go To command</span></p><hr /><p>Do one of the following:</p><ul><li>Right-click an element in the Containment tree or an element symbol on a <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">view</span>. In the main menu, click <strong>Analyze</strong>, then <strong>Go To</strong>. In the submenu, click the needed element.<br />The element is selected in the Containment tree.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7a8b0775-8bcc-4cad-b27d-f5e759f45c18"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the <strong>Go To </strong>submenu contains more than twenty elements, it displays the <strong>Related Elements</strong> command that opens a submenu with the full related elements list. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="go_to_command.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The Go To command's submenu displays elements related to the <em>vccu </em>element. Clicking the <em>'part vccu'</em> element under the Subsetting Feature category displays the <em>vccu </em>part element that subsets the <em>vccu </em>element for which the command was invoked.  </h6><h3><span>Navigating elements via the Navigation tab</span></h3><ul><li><span>See the procedure </span><ac:link ac:anchor="Navigating elements via the Navigation tab"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Specification panel" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Navigating elements via the Navigation tab]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Specification panel" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specification panel]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254422865 space=SYSML2P version=2 -->
## PAGE 00322: (2026x) Element ownership change

- page_id: `254422865`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254422865/2026x+Element+ownership+change
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-10-14T15:13:30.902+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Element management > (2026x) Working with elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This page outlines the procedures regarding element ownership change.

##### Changing element ownership

###### Changing element ownership via the Textual Editor

To change element ownership via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, select the element notation you want to move, including its name and the closing semicolon or the contained body. partflashlight{// to remove this part element, select it along with its name and body contained within the curly braces 
partbulb;// to remove this part element, select the entire line 
}
2. Cut the selection in one of the following ways:
  1. On the keyboard, press Ctrl+X .
  2. Right-click the selection and in the shortcut menu, click Cut .
3. Place the cursor within the curly braces of the intended owning element.
4. Paste the selection in one of the following ways:
  1. On the keyboard, press Ctrl+V .
  2. Right-click the selection and in the shortcut menu, click Paste .
5. Click the Synchronize button.

###### Changing element ownership via the Containment tree

To change element ownership via the Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, click the element whose owner you want to change.
2. Drag it onto the intended owner. The element is moved to the new owning element.

###### Changing element ownership via element symbol in a view

To change element ownership via element symbol in a view

1. In a view, click the symbol of the element whose owner you want to change.
2. Drag it onto the element symbol of the intended owner. The element is moved to the new owning element. Dragging and dropping the symbol itself changes the element's ownership which is different from the functionalities provided by the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Drag and Drop handler' space=''].

###### Changing element ownership via the smart manipulator in a view

To change element ownership via the smart manipulator in a view

1. In a view, select an element symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the feature membership icon.
2. Click on the element you want to be the owner, then the element you want to designate as the owned one. A feature membership path is created between the elements.

###### Changing element ownership via the view palette

To change element ownership via the view palette

1. In the View Palette, under the Other Relationships group, select feature membership .
2. Draw a line with the diamond symbol at the end of the element symbol you want to be the owner.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254422632 space=SYSML2P version=4 -->
## PAGE 00323: (2026x) Entry, do, and exit actions for states 

- page_id: `254422632`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254422632/2026x+Entry+do+and+exit+actions+for+states%C2%A0
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2025-12-03T10:33:59.757+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Behavior > (2026x) States
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Once you create the needed states for your model, you can further specify them as needed. Most commonly, states are specified with specially identified action usages, such as the *entry*, *do*, and *exit* actions.

##### Specifying entry, do, and exit actions for states

A state definition or usage can contain specially identified action usages that are only performed while the state is activated.

- An entry action starts when the state is activated.
- A do action starts after the entry action completes and continues while the state is active.
- An exit action starts when the state is exited, and the state becomes inactive once the exit action is completed.

###### Specifying entry, do, and exit actions for states via the Textual Editor

To specify entry, do, and exit actions for states via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, within the curly braces of the state's body, do one of the following:
  1. Define empty actions by declaring entry/do/exit keyword followed by a semicolon.
  2. Define actions within the state by declaring entry action/do action/exit action keyword, specify the element name , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specializations' space=''] (optional), then either end the element specification or specify the element body.
  3. Specify actions referencing those defined elsewhere in the model by declaring entry/do/exit keyword followed by the name of the action you want to reference, then either end the element specification or specify the element body.
2. Click the Synchronize button.

stateon{

// -------- Defining empty actions - Step 1.a 
entry; 
do; 
exit;

// -------- Defining actions within the state - Step 1.b

entryactionperformSelfTest{invehicle=operatingVehicle;} 
doactionprovidePower{} 
exitactionapplyParkingBrake:ApplyParkingBrake; // defining and feature typing actions within the state

// -------- Specifying actions that reference actions defined elsewhere - Step 1.c

entryperformSelfTest; 
doprovidePower; 
exitapplyParkingBrake; 
}

###### Specifying entry actions for states via the view palette

To specify entry actions for states via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the States group, click the entry **action**button.
2. Click in the view pane where you want to create the entry action symbol.

###### Specifying entry, do, and exit actions for states via the symbol compartment in a view

For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Compartments' space=''] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Compartments' space=''] page.

To specify entry, do, and exit actions for states via the symbol compartment in a view

- Do one of the following:
  - Select a state symbol and click the Create Element button on it. In the dropdown menu, select the element you want to create. The new element is created and displayed in its appropriate compartment.
  - Select a state symbol and on the right side of the actions compartment, click the Create Element button. In the displayed menu, select the needed action. The new element is created and displayed in the actions compartment.

###### Specifying entry, do, and exit actions for states via the Specification panel

To specify entry, do, and exit actions for states via the Specification panel

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification panel' space=''].
2. In the Properties section bar, click the **Create Element** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The shortcut menu appears. Click the needed element kind in the shortcut menu.
3. In the **actions** compartment section's bar, click the **Create Element** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. In the shortcut menu, select the needed action. The element is created in the **actions** compartment.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Once you create the needed states for your model, you can further specify them as needed. Most commonly, states are specified with specially identified action usages, such as the <em>entry</em>, <em>do</em>, and <em>exit</em> actions.</p><h3>Specifying entry, do, and exit actions for states </h3><p>A state definition or usage can contain specially identified action usages that are only performed while the state is activated.</p><ul><li>An entry action starts when the state is activated.</li><li>A do action starts after the entry action completes and continues while the state is active.</li><li>An exit action starts when the state is exited, and the state becomes inactive once the exit action is completed.</li></ul><h4>Specifying entry, do, and exit actions for states via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To specify entry, do, and exit actions for states via the Textual
````
