style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">maxBrakingDistance</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> = </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">brakingRequirement::maxBrakingDistance</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">frame</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">concern</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">brakingRequirementConcern</span> ::&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">brakingConcern</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// step 1.b - named usage frames another concern usage via reference subsetting</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span>        :&gt;&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">maxBrakingDistance</span> = <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">brakingRequirement::maxBrakingDistance</span>;<br />    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Framing concerns via the Drag and Drop handler in a view</h4><p>To frame concerns via the Drag and Drop handler in a view</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="63e06baa-7a5c-4ac1-8ac2-7c4ff55dca4a">Drag and drop a concern element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Drag and Drop handler" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Drag and Drop handler]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> on the needed element symbol, executing the <strong>Create Frame Concern </strong>command.</li></ul><h4>Framing concerns via the Specification panel</h4><p>To frame concerns via the Specification panel</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="828b094e-9d38-497d-a1b9-0a09aaa5a170">Drag and drop a concern element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's Drag and Drop handler and drop it on the <strong>Required Constraint</strong><strong> </strong>field in the Properties section of the Specification panel for the frame concern usage. <span><br /></span>The appropriate concern is specified for the element.</li></ul><h3>Framing a concern via the owned frame concern usage itself as the framed concern</h3><h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1b41e5ef-6b50-426d-996a-1394065931fa"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Framing concerns via the Textual Editor1</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Framing concerns via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To frame concerns via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ol><li class="auto-cursor-target" data-uuid="ba583a33-985d-4fe8-bc04-dbb67c93bc68">In the Textual Editor, within the body of the element that you want to frame a concern, declare the <em>frame concern </em><span>keyword followed by its name.</span></li><li data-uuid="13bcefc5-adbd-4a8b-8573-717387c12a3c"><span>Specify the concern body.</span></li><li class="auto-cursor-target" data-uuid="16ce83a1-4a40-483a-ba09-bafe6f5a3078"><span>Click the </span><strong>Synchronize </strong><span>button.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="71efa7a2-2e9e-4d61-8363-d4801b33a1ff"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">requirement</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">brakingRequirement</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">subject</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">vehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Vehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">actor</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">environment</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'Driving Environment'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">speedLimit</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">SpeedValue</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">maxBrakingDistance</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DistanceValue</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">frame</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">concern</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">brakingRequirementConcern</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {    <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// named framed concern</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">maxBrakingDistance</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DistanceValue</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">maxBrakingDistance</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> = </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">brakingRequirement::maxBrakingDistance</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Framing concerns via the symbol compartment in a view</h4><p>To frame concerns via the symbol compartment in a view</p><hr /><ol><li class="auto-cursor-target" data-uuid="b6b0328e-d9ba-46d2-b694-157f2d2cc03a">Do one of the following:<ol><li class="auto-cursor-target">Select a requirement symbol and click the <strong>Create Element</strong> button on it. In the dropdown menu, click the <strong>frame concern</strong> element.</li><li>Select a requirement symbol and on the right side of the <strong>frames </strong>compartment, click the<span> </span><strong>Create frame concern<span> </span></strong>button.<br />The new feature is created and displayed in its appropriate compartment.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="b83bf122-d4cd-4230-a076-3e5a6a2cc021"><span>Specify the concern.<br /></span>The new element is created and displayed in the<span> </span><strong>frames </strong><span>compartment.</span></li></ol><h4 class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d414ba3b-9c4a-4caf-a84a-0d155acc1633"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Framing concerns via the Specification panel2</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Framing concerns via the Specification panel</h4><p>To frame concerns via the Specification panel</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="6c573eb6-94fa-42ff-ba0f-b46c7c65312e"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Specification panel"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Specification panel" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open the Specification panel]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li data-uuid="f5ed96db-58cc-4186-a2ab-de95afb1d6f6"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Do one of the following:</span><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the Properties section bar, click the <strong>Create Element</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_element_in_specification_pane_button.png" /></ac:image> button. The shortcut menu appears. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click the <strong>frame concern </strong>element in the shortcut menu.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the <strong>frame concern </strong>compartment section's bar, click the <strong>Create frame concern </strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_element_in_compartment_via_specification_pane_button.png" /></ac:image> button.</span></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="d478800f-428f-4a64-9dac-fecbd00a582e">Specify the concern.<br />The element is created in the <strong>frames </strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">compartment.</span></li></ol></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254422780 space=SYSML2P version=2 -->
## PAGE 00338: (2026x) Guard conditions

- page_id: `254422780`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254422780/2026x+Guard+conditions
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-10-14T15:21:32.588+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Behavior > (2026x) Actions > (2026x) Controlling actions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A succession within the body of an action definition or usage may be given a guard condition represented as a Boolean expression, forming a conditional succession. A conditional succession****asserts that the target action can start only if the guard condition is *true*.The conditional succession shorthand notation is particularly useful for notating several conditional successions outgoing from a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Control nodes' space='']*decide* node.

##### Specifying guard conditions for successions

###### Specifying guard conditions for successions via the Textual Editor

To specify guard conditions for successions via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor between the succession's source and target and declare the *if*keyword followed by the condition. The target of a conditional succession must be specified as a qualified name or feature chain and cannot be a full action usage declaration, even when the shorthand notation is used.You can specify multiple guards by declaring multiple conditional successions.
2. (Optional) Declare the *else*keyword followed by its succession target. It indicates a succession to be taken if the guards evaluate to *false*.
3. Click the**Synchronize**button.

// ------------ Full conditional succession

actiontransportPassenger{ 
... 
decide decideNode;firstdecideNode; // specifies the succession source 
ifanotherDestinationthenmergeNode; // 'ifanotherDestination' specifies a guard condition, followed by a succession 'thenmergeNode' 
elsedone; // specifies a succession to be taken if the guard condition evaluates to *false* 
}

//------------Shorthandconditionalsuccession

actiontransportPassenger{... 
decidedecideNode; // omits the succession sourceas it is identified from the lexically previous action 'decidedecideNode' 
ifanotherDestinationthenmergeNode; // 'ifanotherDestination' specifies a guard condition, followed by a succession 'thenmergeNode' 
elsedone; // specifies a succession to be taken if the guard condition evaluates to *false* 
}

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A succession within the body of an action definition or usage may be given a guard condition <span>represented as a Boolean expression, forming a conditional succession. A conditional succession<strong> </strong>asserts that the target action can start only if the guard condition is <em>true</em>. </span>The conditional succession shorthand notation is particularly useful for notating several conditional successions outgoing from a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Control nodes" /><ac:link-body><em>decide</em> node</ac:link-body></ac:link>. </p><h3>Specifying guard conditions for successions</h3><h4>Specifying guard conditions for successions via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To specify guard conditions for successions via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Textual Editor, place the cursor between the succession's source and target and declare the <em>if </em>keyword followed by the condition.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f5d45654-d808-4e06-802a-1473bbb0c766"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>The target of a conditional succession must be specified as a qualified name or feature chain and cannot be a full action usage declaration, even when the shorthand notation is used.</li><li>You can specify multiple guards by declaring multiple conditional successions.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">(Optional) Declare the <em>else </em>keyword followed by its succession target. It indicates a succession to be taken if the guards evaluate to <em>false</em>.</span></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Click the </span><strong style="text-align: left;">Synchronize<span> </span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">button.</span></p></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d297bbee-9a34-46ce-9e00-8c715472d3a9"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// ------------ Full conditional succession</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">transportPassenger</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">    ...</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span>decide</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">decideNode</span>;   <br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span>first</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">decideNode</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;                                          <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span>// specifies the succession <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">source </span></span></span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">if</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">anotherDestination</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">then</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">mergeNode</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;      <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span>// '<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">if</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">anotherDestination' <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">specifies a guard condition, followed by a succession '<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">then</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">mergeNode'</span></span></span></span></span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254423130 space=SYSML2P version=2 -->
## PAGE 00339: (2026x) If action

- page_id: `254423130`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254423130/2026x+If+action
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-10-14T15:21:39.440+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Behavior > (2026x) Actions > (2026x) Controlling actions > (2026x) Structured control actions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An if action usage evaluates a condition expression and then performs a then clause action usage if the expression evaluates to *true*, or, optionally, an else clause action usage if the expression evaluates to *false*.

##### Specifying if actions for actions

###### Specifying if actions for actions via the Textual Editor

To specify if actions for actions via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the if action.
2. Specify the if action usage:
  1. Do one of the following:
    1. For an unnamed if action usage, declare the keyword if .
    2. For a named if action usage, declare the keyword action , specify the element name, and then the keyword if .
  2. Follow with the condition expression for the if action usage.
  3. Specify a then clause by doing one of the following:
    1. For an unnamed then clause, follow with the body for the then clause.
    2. For a named then clause, declare the keyword action , specify the element name, and follow with the body for the then clause.
3. (Optional) Specify the else if clause action usage:
  1. Do one of the following:
    1. For an unnamed else if action usage, declare the keyword else if .
    2. For a named else if action usage, declare the keyword else , then the keyword action , specify the element name, and then the keyword if .
  2. Follow with the condition expression for the if action usage.
  3. Specify a then clause by doing one of the following:
    1. For an unnamed then clause, follow with the body for the then clause.
    2. For a named then clause, declare the keyword action , specify the element name, and follow with the body for the then clause.
4. (Optional) Specify the else clause action usage:
  1. Do one of the following:
    1. For an unnamed else action usage, declare the keyword else .
    2. For a named else action usage, declare the keyword else , then the keyword action , and specify the element name.
  2. Specify the body for the else clause.
5. Click the Synchronize button.

// unnamed if, then clause, and else clause action usages: 
actiontestAlarm{ 
ifthreat.level==high{// unnamed if action usage followed by the if condition expression and an unnamed then clause action usage 
performsoundAlarm{incause=threat;}// body of the then clause action usage 
}elseifthreat.level==medium{// unnamed else clause action usage which is itself an if action usage, followed by the if condition expression and an unnamed then clause action usage 
actionsendNotification{inmsg=threat;}// body of the else if action usage 
}else{// unnamed else clause action usage 
actionbeginMonitoring{intarget=threat;}// body of the else action usage 
} 
}

// named if, then clause, and else clause action usages: 
actiontestAlarm{ 
actionthreatHighifthreat.level==highactionalarm{// named 'threatHigh' if action usage followed by the if condition expression and a named 'alarm' then clause action usage 
performsoundAlarm{incause=threat;}// body of the 'alarm' then clause action usage 
}elseactionthreatMediumifthreat.level==mediumactionnotify{// named 'sendNotification' else clause action usage which is itself an if action usage, followed by the if condition expression and a named 'notify' then clause action usage 
actionsendNotification{inmsg=threat;}// body of the 'sendNotification' else if action usage 
}elseactionmonitor{// named 'monitor' else clause action usage 
actionbeginMonitoring{intarget=threat;}// body of the 'monitor' else action usage 
} 
}

###### Specifying if actions for actions via the view palette

To specify if actions for actions via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Other Actions group, click the if button to create an if action usage element.
2. Click on an action symbol in the view pane where you want to create the if symbol.
3. (Optional) Specify the element via the Textual Editor.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254422888 space=SYSML2P version=3 -->
## PAGE 00340: (2026x) Importing and filtering elements

- page_id: `254422888`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254422888/2026x+Importing+and+filtering+elements
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-12-17T15:06:08.500+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Element management > (2026x) Working with elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An import relationship allows one namespace to import memberships from another namespace. The member elements from imported memberships become (unowned) members of the importing namespace in addition to being members of the imported namespace.

##### Element import kinds and visibility

###### Import kinds

The import kind is specified as a specific suffix added to the import's qualified name.

| Import kind | Suffix | Imports | Example |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Membership import | none | Only the specified namespace (i.e., element import). | package 'Vehicle Information' { part vehicleSpecs {} part vehicleRecord { item vehicleModel; }}package 'Vehicle Details' { // membership import: private import 'Vehicle Information'; // imports 'Vehicle Information'} |
| Namespace import | ::* | The content of the specified namespace. | package 'Vehicle Information' { part vehicleSpecs {} part vehicleRecord { item vehicleModel; }}package 'Vehicle Details' { // namespace import: private import 'Vehicle Information'::*; // imports vehicleSpecs, vehicleRecord} |
| Recursive membership import | ::** | The specified namespace and its content, including sub-namespaces and their elements. | package 'Vehicle Information' { part vehicleSpecs {} part vehicleRecord { item vehicleModel; }}package 'Vehicle Details' { // recursive membership import: private import 'Vehicle Information'::**; // imports 'Vehicle Information', vehicleSpecs, vehicleRecord, vehicleModel} |
| Recursive namespace import | ::*::** | The content of the specified namespace, including sub-namespaces and their elements. | package 'Vehicle Information' { part vehicleSpecs {} part vehicleRecord { item vehicleModel; }}package 'Vehicle Details' { // recursive namespace import: private import 'Vehicle Information'::*::**; // imports vehicleSpecs, vehicleRecord, vehicleModel} |

###### Import visibility

The import [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Visibility' space=''] is specified as a specific keyword declared prior to the *import* keyword. The import visibility keywords are as follows:

- public - membership is visible outside the namespace.
- private - membership is invisible from outside the namespace.
- protected - membership is invisible outside the namespace, except in inheritance, where it behaves like public.

##### Element import

###### Importing elements

To import elements

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to import the element(s).
2. Specify the import , declaring the needed keyword.
3. Declare the keyword import .
4. Specify the qualified name of the membership/namespace you want to import, followed by the .
5. (Optional) .
6. Click the Synchronize button.

You can specify multiple*import*relationships for the same view.

package'Vehicle Information'{partvehicleSpecs{}partvehicleRecord{itemvehicleModel;}}

package'Vehicle Details'{ 
privateimport'Vehicle Information'::**;// private recursive membership import of '*Vehicle Information', vehicleSpecs, vehicleRecord, vehicleModel* 
}

#### NOTE: Reviewing imported elements

Reviewing imported elements

To review the elements imported for a specific element, open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification window' space='']**Specification** window and check the values of the **Imported Membership**property.

###### Importing library elements

Import is especially useful for accessing library elements instead of using a qualified name each time.

#### SECTION: Import instead of qualified name

#### COLUMN: Qualified name instead of import

50%

#### PANEL: Qualified name instead of import

Qualified name instead of import

// instead of using a qualified name each time:

package'Employee Data'{ 
partemployeeData{ 
// accessing 'String' and 'Real' data types from the *ScalarValues* library via qualified name: 
itememployeeName:ScalarValues::String; 
itemID:ScalarValues::Real; 
} 
}

#### COLUMN: Import instead of qualified name

50%

#### PANEL: Import instead of qualified name

Import instead of qualified name

// use import to access library elements:

package'Employee Data'{ 
private importScalarValues::**; // importing *ScalarValues* library 
partemployeeData{ 
itememployeeName:String; // no need for qualified name 
itemID:Real; // no need for qualified name 
} 
}

##### Element import filtering

Namespaces have the capability to filter elements upon import based on certain conditions, usually defined in terms of the metadata provided by annotations of those elements. Only elements that meet all filter conditions become imported members of the namespace.

- Filter conditions can be used only for [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Package' space=''] () and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Views basics' space=''] (element exposition) elements.
- Filter condition expression must be model-level evaluable. Any SysML v2 expression can be used for the filter condition specification, but it must return*true/false*.
- @ , istype , and hastype classification operators are commonly used to check if an element has the given metadata annotation.
- Element metaclass metadatas are located in used projects at Standard Libraries > SysML > Systems . Filter conditions require a qualified name for metadata-based filtering (e.g., @SysML::Systems::PartUsage ).

###### Separately declared filter conditions

To specify separately declared filter conditions

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor within the body of the package after an import declaration.
2. Declare the keyword filter followed by filter condition expression, including the needed operator and the qualified name of the element you want to import .
3. Click the Synchronize button.

#### PANEL: Separately declared filter conditions

Separately declared filter conditions

packageMetadataDefinitions{metadatadefSafety{attributeisMandatory:Boolean;}}

packageVehicle{ 
privateimportMetadataDefinitions::**; 
partvehicle{ 
partinterior{ 
partalarm; 
partseatBelt[2] {@Safety{isMandatory=true;}} 
partfrontSeat[2]; 
partdriverAirBag{@Safety{isMandatory=false;}} 
} 
} 
}

package'Vehicle Features'{ 
privateimportVehicle::**; // imports package *Vehicle*// you can use multiple filter condition declarations:filter@SysML::Systems::PartUsage;// imports part usage elements from the package *Vehicle*filter@MetadataDefinitions::SafetyandMetadataDefinitions::Safety::isMandatory;// imports elements with *Safety*metadata attribute *isMandatory*value *true*from previously filtered part usage elements imported from the package *Vehicle*}

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">An import relationship allows one namespace to import memberships from another namespace. The member elements from imported memberships become (unowned) members of the importing namespace in addition to being members of the imported namespace.</span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3>Element import kinds and visibility </h3><h4><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Import kinds</span><span style="font-weight: 400;letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> </span></h4><p><span style="font-weight: 400;letter-spacing: 0.0px;">The import kind is specified as a specific suffix added to the import's qualified name.  </span></p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 81.0867%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 19.7853%;" /><col style="width: 6.67269%;" /><col style="width: 30.3375%;" /><col style="width: 43.1959%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th scope="col">Import kind</th><th scope="col">Suffix</th><th scope="col">Imports</th><th scope="col">Example</th></tr><tr><td><p><span><strong>Membership import</strong></span></p></td><td>none</td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Only the specified namespace (i.e., element import).</span></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="353c3679-05b4-4b41-972b-520e8a1ded9a"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'Vehicle Information'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">vehicleSpecs</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {}</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">vehicleRecord</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">vehicleModel</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'Vehicle Details'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// membership import:</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span>private<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span>import<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'Vehicle Information'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// imports '<em>Vehicle Information'</em></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span><br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Namespace import</strong></p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">::*</span></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The content of the specified namespace.</span></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="78e9d07a-2dca-4801-adb3-9b45863c2ba8"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'Vehicle Information'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">vehicleSpecs</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {}</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">vehicleRecord</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">vehicleModel</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'Vehicle Details'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// namespace import:</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span>private<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span>import<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'Vehicle Information'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">::*; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// imports <em>vehicleSpecs, vehicleRecord</em></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span><br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><span><strong>Recursive membership import</strong></span></p><span><strong><br /></strong></span></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">::**</span></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The specified namespace and its content, including sub-namespaces and their elements.</span></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e3b89a84-a3cc-4b55-bb68-cba7db8e903f"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'Vehicle Information'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">vehicleSpecs</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {}</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">vehicleRecord</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">vehicleModel</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'Vehicle Details'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// recursive membership import:</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span>private<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span>import<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'Vehicle Information'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">::**; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// imports <em>'Vehicle Information', vehicleSpecs, vehicleRecord, vehicleModel</em></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span><br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><span><strong>Recursive namespace import</strong></span></p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">::*::**</span></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The content of the specified namespace, including sub-namespaces and their elements.</span></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ee50aaa9-14e0-42b4-a95f-34f18e3fcac5"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'Vehicle Information'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">vehicleSpecs</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {}</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">vehicleRecord</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">vehicleModel</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'Vehicle Details'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// recursive namespace import:</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span>private<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span>import<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'Vehicle Information'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">::*::**; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// imports <em>vehicleSpecs, vehicleRecord, vehicleModel</em></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span><br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr></tbody></table><h4>Import visibility</h4><p>The import <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Visibility" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[visibility]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> is specified as a specific keyword declared prior to the <em>import</em> keyword. The import visibility keywords are as follows:</p><ul><li><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">public</em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> - membership is visible outside the namespace.</span></li><li><em>private</em> - membership is invisible from outside the namespace.</li><li><em>protected</em> - membership is invisible outside the namespace, except in inheritance, where it behaves like public.</li></ul><h3>Element import</h3><h4>Importing elements</h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To import elements</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to import the element(s).</span></li><li>Specify the import <ac:link ac:anchor="Import visibility"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[visibility]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, declaring the needed keyword.</li><li>Declare the keyword <em>import</em>.</li><li>Specify the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">qualified name of the membership/namespace you want to import, followed by the <ac:link ac:anchor="Import kinds"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[appropriate suffix for a recursive/membership/namespace import]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">(Optional) </span><ac:link ac:anchor="Element import filtering"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specify a filter for the import]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">. </span></li><li>Click the <strong>Synchronize<span> </span></strong>button.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0edb985d-9686-4f2a-9e6c-f01e1cd7abe8"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can specify multiple<span> </span><em>import</em><span> </span>relationships for the same view. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2dae463f-236c-45dd-9883-ecc7044ea2d8"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'Vehicle Information'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">vehicleSpecs</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {}<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">vehicleRecord</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">vehicleModel</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;<br /></span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }<br /></span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'Vehicle Details'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span>private<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span>import<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'Vehicle Information'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">::**; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// private recursive membership import of '<em>Vehicle Information', vehicleSpecs, vehicleRecord, vehicleModel</em></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="db258086-34ec-4ea1-a064-14f563cef664"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Reviewing imported elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>To review the elements imported for a specific element, open its <ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Specification window"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Specification window" /><ac:link-body><strong>Specification</strong> window</ac:link-body></ac:link> and check the values of the <strong>Imported Membership </strong>property.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4><span>Importing library elements</span></h4><p><span>Import is especially useful for accessing library elements instead of using a qualified name each time.</span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="section" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="87754a6c-049d-479c-b354-43e6c90afd90"><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="69db5776-53c2-46a2-a8a0-9b9a0fd7ffdf"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="deb9aa25-bd7f-488c-b27c-339db1203e0e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Qualified name instead of import</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// instead of using a qualified name each time:</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> '</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Employee Data'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">employeeData</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {<br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">        // accessing 'String' and 'Real' data types from the <em>ScalarValues</em> library via qualified name:</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">employeeName</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ScalarValues::</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">String</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">ID</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ScalarValues::Real</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}<br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c26e52dd-43b8-47c4-9728-dbfedf2fb90c"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1eb6a049-d753-4d74-84ed-e805e9ca1c62"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Import instead of qualified name </ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// use import to access library elements:</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> '<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Employee Data'</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">private import</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ScalarValues</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">::**;  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// importing <em>ScalarValues</em> library</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">employeeData</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">employeeName</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">String</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;   <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// no need for qualified name</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">ID</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Real</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;   <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// no need for qualified name</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3>Element import filtering</h3><p>Namespaces have the capability to filter elements upon import based on certain conditions, usually defined in terms of the metadata provided by annotations of those elements. Only elements that meet all filter conditions become imported members of the namespace.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="69834189-7a61-4af4-ac3a-db7ce99b7a9b"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><span>Filter conditions can be used only for <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Package" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[package]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (<ac:link><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[element import]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>) and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Views basics" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[view]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (element exposition) elements.</span></li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Filter condition expression must be model-level evaluable. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Any SysML v2 expression can be used for the filter condition specification, but it must return </span><em style="text-align: left;">true/false</em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">.</span></span></li><li><em>@</em>, <em>istype</em>, and <em>hastype</em> classification operators are commonly used to check if an element has the given metadata annotation.</li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Element metaclass metadatas are located in used projects at </span><em style="text-align: left;">Standard Libraries &gt; SysML &gt; Systems</em><em style="text-align: left;">. </em>Filter conditions require a qualified name for metadata-based filtering (e.g., <em>@SysML::Systems::PartUsage</em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">).</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Separately declared filter conditions</h4><p><span>To specify separately declared filter conditions </span></p><hr /><ol><li>In the Textual Editor, place the cursor within the body of the package after an <em>import</em><em> </em>declaration.</li><li>Declare the keyword<span> </span><em>filter </em>followed by filter condition expression, including the needed operator and the qualified name of the element you want to import<span>.</span></li><li>Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong>button.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d35791f4-83c6-41f4-87e5-d3d23b9af69c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Separately declared filter conditions</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">MetadataDefinitions</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">metadata</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Safety</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">isMandatory</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Boolean</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Vehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span>private<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span>import<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">MetadataDefinitions</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">::**;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span>part<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">vehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span>part<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">interior</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span>part<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">alarm</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span>part<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">seatBelt</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> [</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">2</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">] { </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">@</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Safety</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">{</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">isMandatory</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> = </span>true<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;} </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span>part<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">frontSeat</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> [</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">2</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">];</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span>part<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">driverAirBag</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> { </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">@</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Safety</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">{</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">isMandatory</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> = </span>false<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;} </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'Vehicle Features'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> { </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span>private<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span>import<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Vehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">::**;  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// imports package <em>Vehicle</em> <br /></span></span></span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">    // you can use multiple filter condition declarations:<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">filter</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> @ </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">SysML::Systems::PartUsage</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// imports part usage elements from the package <em>Vehicle<br /></em></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">filter</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> @ </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">MetadataDefinitions::Safety</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">and</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">MetadataDefinitions::Safety::isMandatory</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// <span>imports elements with <em>Safety </em>metadata attribute <em>isMandatory </em>value <em>true </em>from previously filtered part usage elements imported from the package <em>Vehicle <br /></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></span></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254422388 space=SYSML2P version=8 -->
## PAGE 00341: (2026x) Include use case

- page_id: `254422388`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254422388/2026x+Include+use+case
- version_number: 8
- version_date: `2025-12-17T15:20:02.851+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Behavior > (2026x) Use cases
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An include use case usage is a use caseusage that is also a kind of perform action usage. A use case definition or usage may contain an include use case usage to specify that the behavior of the containing use case includes the behavior of the included use case. The subject of the included use case is the same as the subject of the containing use case, so the subject parameter of the included use case must have a definition that is compatible with the definition of the containing use case. Actor parameters of the included use case may be bound to corresponding actor parameters of the containing use case as necessary.

An include use case usage can be a feature of:

- a part definition or usage, specifying that the referenced use case is included in the containing part.
- a use case definition or usage, whereby the behavior of the containing use case includes the behavior of the included use case.

There are two ways a use case can be included by another element:

50%

- by referencing the use case usage defined elsewhere in the model.
  - The element symbol is displayed with the include keyword (unnamed element) or the include use case keyword (named element). [ATTACHMENT filename='include_symbol.png'] [ATTACHMENT filename='include_use_case_symbol_2.png']
  - See the procedures:
    - :
      - 
      - 
      - 

50%

- by containing an owned include use case, i.e., the included use case is the include use case usage itself.
  - The element symbol is displayed with the include use case keyword. [ATTACHMENT filename='include_use_case_symbol.png']
  - See the procedures:
    - :
      - 
      - 
      - 
      - 
      - 

##### Including a use case by referencing a use case usage defined elsewhere in the model

###### Including use cases via the Textual Editor1Including use cases via the Textual Editor

To include use cases via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, within the body of the element that you want to include a use case, do one of the following:
  1. For an unnamed included use case, declare the include keyword followed by the name of the included use case.
  2. For a named one, declare the include use case keyword, specify the element name, then [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Reference subsetting' space=''] the included use case.
2. Click the Synchronize button.

use case 'drive vehicle';//referenced use case

use case 'provide transportation' {

// unnamed included use case referencing a use case defined elsewhere in the model 
 include 'drive vehicle' {}

// named included use case referencing a use case defined elsewhere in the model 
 include use case enter references 'drive vehicle' {} 
 include use case enter ::> 'drive vehicle' {}

}

###### Including use cases via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

To include use cases via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

- Drag a use case element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Drag and Drop handler' space=''] and drop it on:
  - the target element symbol that you want to include the use case by referencing it using the **Create Include Use Case** command.
  - the include use case usage symbol that you want to include the use case by referencing it using the **Set Use Case** **Included** command.

###### Including use cases via the Specification panel1Including use cases via the Specification panel

To include use cases via the Specification panel

- Drag and drop a use case element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's Drag and Drop handler and drop it on the **Included Use Case** field in the Properties section of the Specification panel for the include use case element. The appropriate use case is specified for the element.

##### Including a use case via the ownedinclude use caseusageitselfas theincluded use case

###### Including use cases via the Textual Editor

To include use cases via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, within the body of the element that you want to include a use case, declare the include use case keyword followed by its name.
2. Click the Synchronize button.

usecase'provide transportation'{

// included use case is the defined include use case usage itself contained by the parent 
 include use case 'drive vehicle' {} 
}

###### Including use cases via the Create Element command

To include use cases via the Create Element command

1. In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click **Create Element**.
2. In the Create Element dialog, select the include use case element.
3. Name the element.
4. Specify the included use case's features.

###### Including use cases via the view palette

To include use cases via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Cases group, click the include use case button.
2. Click in the view pane where you want to create the include use case symbol.
3. Name the element.
4. Specify the included use case's features.

###### Including use cases via the symbol compartment in a view

To include use cases via the symbol compartment in a view

- Do one of the following:
  - Select a use case symbol and click the Create Element button on it. In the dropdown menu, click the **include use case** button. The element is created and displayed in the includes compartment.
  - Select a use case symbol and on the right side of the includes compartment, click the Create include use case button. The element is created and displayed in the includes compartment.

###### Including use cases via the Specification panel

To include use cases via the Specification panel

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification panel' space=''] for the use case element.
2. Do one of the following:
  1. In the Properties section bar, click the **Create Element** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The shortcut menu appears. Click the include use case button in the shortcut menu.
  2. In the **includes** compartment section's bar, click the **Create include use case** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The element is created in the **includes** compartment.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
