Drop handler in a view</p><hr /><ul><li>Drag a use case element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Drag and Drop handler" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Drag and Drop handler]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and drop it on:<ul><li>the target element symbol that you want to include the use case <span>by referencing it </span>using the<span> <strong>Create Include Use Case</strong> </span>command.<span> </span></li><li>the include use case usage symbol that you want to include the use case <span>by referencing it </span>using the<span> <strong>Set Use Case</strong> <strong>Included </strong></span>command.<span> </span></li></ul></li></ul><h4><span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5e67949d-2e83-48b0-973b-2267523138c2"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Including use cases via the Specification panel1</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Including use cases via the Specification panel</span></h4><p>To include use cases via the Specification panel</p><hr /><ul><li>Drag and drop a use case element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's Drag and Drop handler and drop it on the<span> <strong>Included Use Case</strong></span><strong><span> </span></strong>field in the Properties section of the Specification panel for the include use case element. <span><br /></span>The appropriate use case is specified for the element.</li></ul><h3>Including a use case <span style="letter-spacing: -0.006em;">via the owned </span><span style="letter-spacing: -0.006em;">include use case </span><span style="letter-spacing: -0.006em;">usage </span><span style="letter-spacing: -0.006em;">itself </span><span style="letter-spacing: -0.006em;">as the </span><span style="letter-spacing: -0.006em;">included use case</span></h3><h4>Including use cases via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To include use cases via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ol><li class="auto-cursor-target">In the Textual Editor, within the body of the element that you want to include a use case, declare the <em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">include use case </em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">keyword followed by its name.</span></li><li>Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong>button.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="96b24203-8e6b-4f91-9f5f-db995b0c852a"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">use</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">case</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'provide transportation'</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">included use case</span></span> is the defined include use case usage itself contained by the parent</span></span><br />    <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">include</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">use</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">case</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'drive vehicle'</span> {}<br />}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Including use cases via the Create Element command</h4><p>To include use cases via the Create Element command</p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click <strong>Create Element</strong>.</p></li><li>In the <strong>Create Element</strong> dialog, select the <strong>include use case </strong>element.</li><li>Name the element.</li><li>Specify the included use case's features.</li></ol><h4>Including use cases via the view palette</h4><p>To include use cases via the view palette</p><hr /><ol><li>In the view palette, under the Cases group, click the <strong>include use case </strong><span>button.</span></li><li>Click in the view pane where you want to create the include use case symbol.</li><li>Name the element.</li><li>Specify the included use case's features. </li></ol><h4>Including use cases via the symbol compartment in a view </h4><p>To include use cases via the symbol compartment in a view </p><hr /><ul><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>Select a use case symbol and click the<span> </span><strong>Create Element</strong><span> </span>button on it. In the dropdown menu, click the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><strong>include use case </strong></span>button. The element is created and displayed in the <strong>includes</strong> compartment.</li><li>Select a use case symbol and on the right side of the <strong>includes</strong> compartment, click the<span> </span><strong>Create <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">include use case</span><span> </span></strong>button. The element is created and displayed in the <strong>includes</strong> compartment.</li></ul></li></ul><h4>Including use cases via the Specification panel</h4><p>To include use cases via the Specification panel</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Specification panel"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Specification panel" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open the Specification panel]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> for the use case element.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Do one of the following:</span><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the Properties section bar, click the <strong>Create Element</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_element_in_specification_pane_button.png" /></ac:image> button. The shortcut menu appears. </span>Click the <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">include use case </strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">button in the shortcut menu. </span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the <strong>includes</strong> compartment section's bar, click the <strong>Create include use case</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_element_in_compartment_via_specification_pane_button.png" /></ac:image> button. <br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The element is created in the <strong>includes</strong> compartment.</span></li></ol></li></ol></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=312247456 space=SYSML2P version=7 -->
## PAGE 00342: (2026x) Indirectly used projects

- page_id: `312247456`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/312247456/2026x+Indirectly+used+projects
- version_number: 7
- version_date: `2026-06-10T11:53:30.128+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Project management > (2026x) Working with projects > (2026x) Managing local used projects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### EXPAND: Click here to expand the documentation for the 2026x version...

Click here to expand the documentation for the 2026x version...

The projects used in the main project may also use other projects, called indirectly used projects. When you use a project directly that may have other used projects, these indirectly used projects are loaded in the main project. Once loaded, the indirectly used projects use the same project version that is used in the directly used project. However, if the indirectly used project is later modified and you want this change to be reflected in the main project, you must update the project version in the directly used project, then update the version of the directly used project in the main project.

In the main project, you can see the indirectly used projects:

- Under the Containment tree's Used Projects node, displayed at the same node level as the project that uses it directly.
- The Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog's Indirectly used projects category.
- The Project Usages dialog, nested under the project that uses it directly.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Using indirectly used projects directly 2026xUsing indirectly used projects directly

To use indirectly used projects directly

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local used projects' space='']Open the **Advanced Project Usages Configuration**dialog .
2. In the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog, do one of the following:
  1. S elect a used project and in the dialog's toolbar, click the **Use Project Directly** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button .
  2. Right-click a used project and in the shortcut menu, click the **Use Project Directly** command . The cell in the Action column for the removed used project states ' Use Project Directly '. [ATTACHMENT filename='Using indirectly used projects directly 2026x.png']
3. (Optional) To undo the action, do one of the following:
  1. In the dialog's toolbar, click the Reset Changes for Selection [ATTACHMENT filename='reset_changes_for_selection_button.png'] button.
  2. Right-click the used project and in the shortcut menu, click the Reset Changes for Selection command.
4. Do one of the following:
  1. To save changes and keep the dialog open, click the Apply button.
  2. To save changes and close the dialog, click the OK button.
  3. To discard changes and close the dialog, click the Cancel button.
5. Click the OK button to close the Project Usages dialog.

###### Opening indirectly used projects

To open indirectly used projects

See the procedures:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Opening used projects' space=''] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Opening used projects' space=''] .

###### Removing indirectly used projects

Indirectly used projects can only be removed from the main project in the following cases:

- If you [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Removing used projects' space=''] .
- If you first, then [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Removing used projects' space=''] .

#### NOTE: 2026x HotFix1 version

2026x HotFix1 version

This documentation page is for the 2026x HotFix1 version of the SysML v2 Plugin. If you are working with the 2026x version, expand the section above. However, we highly advise upgrading to the 2026x HotFix1 version.

The projects used in the main project may also use other projects, called indirectly used projects. When you use a project directly that may have other used projects, these indirectly used projects are loaded in the main project. Once loaded, the indirectly used projects use the same project version that is used in the directly used project. However, if the indirectly used project is later modified and you want this change to be reflected in the main project, you must update the project version in the directly used project, then update the version of the directly used project in the main project.

In the main project, you can see the indirectly used projects:

- Under the Containment tree's Used Projects node, displayed at the same node level as the project that uses it directly.
- The Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog's Indirectly used projects category.
- The Project Usages dialog, nested under the project that uses it directly.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Using indirectly used projects directly

To use indirectly used projects directly

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local used projects' space='']Open the **Advanced Project Usages Configuration**dialog .
2. In the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog, do one of the following:
  1. S elect a used project and in the dialog's toolbar, click the **Use Project Directly** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button .
  2. Right-click a used project and in the shortcut menu, click the **Use Project Directly** command . The cell in the Action column for the removed used project states ' Use Project Directly '. [ATTACHMENT filename='Using indirectly used projects directly .png']
3. (Optional) To undo the action, do one of the following:
  1. In the dialog's toolbar, click the Reset Changes for Selection [ATTACHMENT filename='reset_changes_for_selection_button.png'] button.
  2. Right-click the used project and in the shortcut menu, click the Reset Changes for Selection command.
4. Do one of the following:
  1. To save changes and keep the dialog open, click the Apply button.
  2. To save changes and close the dialog, click the OK button.
  3. To discard changes and close the dialog, click the Cancel button.
5. Click the OK button to close the Project Usages dialog.

###### Opening indirectly used projects

To open indirectly used projects

See the procedures:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Opening used projects' space=''] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Opening used projects' space=''] .

###### Removing indirectly used projects

Indirectly used projects can only be removed from the main project in the following cases:

- If you [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Removing used projects' space=''] .
- If you first, then [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Removing used projects' space=''] .

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=314182530 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00343: (2026x) Individual

- page_id: `314182530`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/314182530/2026x+Individual
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-07-01T14:57:23.246+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Common elements > (2026x) Occurrences
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Any occurrence can be specified as an individual, representing an entity with a distinct identity within a modeled system, allowing for its full or partial characterization.

##### Specifying elements as individuals

###### Specifying elements as individuals via the Textual Editor

To specify elements as individuals via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, declare the *individual* keyword prior to the declaration of the element that you want to designate as an individual.
2. Click the **Synchronize**button. individualpartdefMyCar:>Car;

###### Specifying elements as individuals via the element shortcut menu

To specify elements as individuals via the element shortcut menu

- Right-click an element in the Containment tree or its symbol in a view and in its shortcut menu, in the Properties group, select the Is Individual property.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Any occurrence can be specified as an individual, representing an entity with a distinct identity within a modeled system, allowing for its full or partial characterization.</p><h3>Specifying elements as individuals</h3><h4>Specifying elements as individuals via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To specify elements as individuals via the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Textual Editor</span></p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="30fdb5f3-5c9b-487e-b9ee-a4c6a34cbc59"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the Textual Editor, declare the <em>individual</em> keyword prior to the declaration of the element that you want to designate as an individual.</span></li><li data-uuid="70c198d6-91e4-4492-8bce-ca488838f16e"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong>button. </span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="75f9ae3d-b9b5-4f4b-90f4-6cd962e5fe38"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">individual</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">MyCar</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Car</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h4>Specifying elements as individuals via the element shortcut menu</h4><p>To specify elements as individuals via the element shortcut menu</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="bd4d2b42-0ef3-483f-8ca3-871cd42b7bf4">Right-click an element in the Containment tree or its symbol in a view and in its shortcut menu, in the <strong>Properties</strong> group, select the <strong>Is Individual </strong>property.</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254423026 space=SYSML2P version=3 -->
## PAGE 00344: (2026x) Installation and system requirements

- page_id: `254423026`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254423026/2026x+Installation+and+system+requirements
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-12-03T08:25:17.455+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Getting started
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

For information regarding installation and system requirements, visit the [Installation, licensing, and system requirements](https://docs.nomagic.com/IL/?contextKey=installation-licensing-and-system-requirements&version=2026x) page.

To use SysML v2 features in the modeling tool, the SysML v1 Plugin and Cameo Requirements Modeler must be installed in addition to the SysML v2 Plugin and the SysML v2 Evaluation Plugin. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Prerequisites' space=''] page.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>For information regarding installation and system requirements, visit the <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/IL/?contextKey=installation-licensing-and-system-requirements&amp;version=2026x">Installation, licensing, and system requirements</a> page.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8c300796-e00f-4b4b-b6e1-a434daa52ba5"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To use SysML v2 features in the modeling tool, the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">SysML v1 Plugin and Cameo Requirements Modeler</span> must be installed in addition to the SysML v2 Plugin and the SysML v2 Evaluation Plugin. See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Prerequisites" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Prerequisites]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254422370 space=SYSML2P version=6 -->
## PAGE 00345: (2026x) Interface

- page_id: `254422370`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254422370/2026x+Interface
- version_number: 6
- version_date: `2025-12-03T08:55:05.027+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Structure
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An interface is a connection whose ends are ports. As such, an interface facilitates the specification and reuse of compatible connections between parts. An interface definition is a kind of connection definition whose ends are restricted to be port definitions, while an interface usage is a kind of connection that is a usage of an interface definition with ends restricted to be port usages. As such, an interface usage connects compatible ports.

- The ends of an interface definition or usage must be port usages.
- The port keyword is optional for interface ends. The keyword is mandatory if the ends have owned [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Cross features' space=''] .

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
The image displays the *'fuel interface'* interface connector where *supplierPort*reference subsets *tankAssy.fuelTankPort*connecting to *consumerPort*which reference subsets *eng.engineFuelPort*. 
Additionally, the connector is also [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying elements in symbolic views' space=''].

##### Creating interface usage and definition elements

###### Creating interface usage elements via the Textual Editor

To create interface usage elements via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the interface keyword.
2. Specify the element name (optional), [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specializations' space=''] (optional), then the connect keyword, then state the feature ports explicitly (using the connect...to syntax ) or in a comma-separated list of related features. If the interface has no name and no specialization, the keyword *connect*can be omitted.You can also name interface end ports, associating them to feature ports via[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Reference subsetting' space=''].
3. Click the Synchronize button.

interfacedefFuelInterface{ 
end portsupplierPort:FuelOutPort; 
end portconsumerPort:FuelInPort; 
}

partvehicle{ 
parttankAssy{portfuelTankPort;} 
parteng{portengineFuelPort;}

// unnamed unspecialized interface usage connecting feature ports with explicit declaration:interfacetankAssy.fuelTankPorttoeng.engineFuelPort;//unnamed unspecialized interface usage connecting featureports in a comma-separated list:interface(tankAssy.fuelTankPort,eng.engineFuelPort); //unnamed unspecialized interface usage with named end portsconnecting featureports in a comma-separated list:interface(sPort ::>tankAssy.fuelTankPort, cPort ::>eng.engineFuelPort);

// unnamed specialized (feature typing) interface usage with named end portsconnectingfeatureportswith explicit declaration:interface : FuelInterface connect sPort ::> tankAssy.fuelTankPort to cPort ::> eng.engineFuelPort;

// named unspecializedinterface usage connecting featureports with explicit declarations: 
interface fuelInterface connecttankAssy.fuelTankPort to eng.engineFuelPort;

// named specialized (feature typing) interface usage with named end portsconnectingfeatureportswith explicit declaration:interface 'fuel interface' : FuelInterface connect sPort ::> tankAssy.fuelTankPort to cPort ::> eng.engineFuelPort;// named specialized (feature typing) interface usage with named end portsconnecting featureportsin a comma-separated list:interface'fuel interface':FuelInterfaceconnect(sPort::>tankAssy.fuelTankPort,cPort::>eng.engineFuelPort);}

###### Creating interface usage elements via the smart manipulator in a view

To create interface usage elements via the smart manipulator in a view

1. In a view, select an element symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the interface command.
2. Click on the element you want to connect via an interface. An interface connector is created between the elements with ports on both ends.

###### Creating interface usage elements via the Create Relationship command

To create interface usage elements via the Create Relationship command

1. Right-click one of the elements you want to connect.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Create Relationship , then in the submenu, click Outgoing , and then in the submenu, click interface . The Element Picker dialog opens.
3. In the Element Picker dialog, do one of the following:
  1. In a view, click the element symbol you want to connect. Hold Shift to select multiple elements.
  2. Click the Add [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The element selection dialog opens. In the element selection dialog, select the element you want to connect and click OK .
  3. Drag and drop the needed element you want to connect from the Containment tree onto the Element Picker .
4. In the Element Picker dialog, click OK .

###### Creating interface usage and definition elements via the view palette

To create interface usage and definition elements via the view palette

- To create an interface definition:
  1. In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the **interface** def button to create an interface definition element.
  2. Click in the view pane where you want the interface definition symbol to be created.
- To create an interface usage:
  1. In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the **interface** button to create an interface usage.
  2. In a view, click on the first of the two element symbols you want to connect, then on the second. An interface connector is created between the elements with ports on both ends. If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to either the **interface, n-ary interface**or the **interface****def**button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.

###### Creating interface definition elements via the Textual Editor

To create interface definition elements via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the keyword interface def .
2. Name the element, then specify the interface definition features in the element body:
  1. Specify the interface definition ends by declaring the end keyword, followed by the end name and/or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specializations' space=''] of the needed port.
3. Click the Synchronize button.

interfacedefFuelInterface{ // interface definition 
end portsupplierPort:FuelOutPort; 
end portconsumerPort:FuelInPort; 
}

###### Creating interface definition elements via the Create Element command

To create interface definition elements via the Create Element command

1. In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click **Create Element**.
2. In the Create Element submenu, click interface def for interface definition.

###### Auto-creating interface definition elements via the Extract Definition command

To auto-create an interface definition element via the Extract Definition command

- See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Extract Definition and Usage' space='']page.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">tankAssy</span>.<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelTankPort</span> <span interface'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">FuelInterface</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">connect</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> (</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">sPort</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> ::&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">tankAssy</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelTankPort</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">, </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">cPort</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> ::&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">eng</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">engineFuelPort)</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4 class="auto-cursor-target">Creating interface usage elements via the smart manipulator in a view</h4><p>To create interface usage elements via the smart manipulator in a view</p><hr /><ol><li>In a view, select an element symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the <strong>interface </strong>command.</li><li>Click on the element you want to connect via an interface. An interface connector is created between the elements with ports on both ends.</li></ol><h4>Creating interface usage elements <span>via the Create Relationship command</span></h4><p>To create interface usage elements via the Create Relationship command</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click one of the elements you want to connect.</li><li>In the shortcut menu, click <strong>Create Relationship</strong>, then in the submenu, click <strong>Outgoing</strong>, and then in the submenu, click <strong>interface</strong>. The <strong>Element Picker </strong>dialog opens.</li><li>In the <strong>Element Picker </strong>dialog, do one of the following:<ol><li>In a view, click the element symbol you want to connect. Hold Shift to select multiple elements. </li><li>Click the <strong>Add <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="add_button.png" /></ac:image> </strong>button. The element selection dialog opens. In the element selection dialog, select the element you want to connect and click <strong>OK</strong>.</li><li>Drag and drop the needed element you want to connect from the Containment tree onto the <strong>Element Picker</strong>.</li></ol></li><li>In the <strong>Element Picker</strong><strong> </strong>dialog, click <strong>OK</strong>. </li></ol><h4 class="auto-cursor-target">Creating interface usage and definition elements via the view palette</h4><p>To create interface usage and definition elements via the view palette</p><hr /><ul><li>To create an interface definition:<ol><li>In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the<span> <strong>interface </strong></span><strong>def </strong><span>button to create an interface definition element.</span></li><li>Click in the view pane where you want the interface definition symbol to be created.</li></ol></li><li>To create an interface usage:<ol><li>In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the<span> <strong>interface </strong></span><span>button to create an interface usage.</span></li><li>In a view, click on the first of the two element symbols you want to connect, then on the second. An interface connector is created between the elements with ports on both ends.<ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="769c3a3a-7b27-471d-b151-165748c95ab6"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to either the <span><strong>interface, n-ary interface </strong></span>or the <span><strong>interface </strong></span><strong>def </strong>button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></li></ul><h4 class="auto-cursor-target">Creating interface definition elements via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To create interface definition elements via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the keyword<span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> </span><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">interface def</em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">.</span></li><li>Name the element, then specify the interface definition features in the element body:<ol><li>Specify the interface definition ends by declaring the <em>end</em> keyword, followed by the end name and/or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Specializations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ specialization ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>of the needed port.</li></ol></li><li>Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong>button.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="30044d15-f229-4626-9e04-2b2739874b7d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">interface</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">FuelInterface</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {                <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// interface definition</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">end port</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">supplierPort</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">FuelOutPort</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">end port</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">consumerPort</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">FuelInPort</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Creating interface definition elements via the Create Element command</h4><p>To create interface definition elements via the Create Element command</p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click <strong>Create Element</strong>.</p></li><li>In the Create Element submenu, click <strong>interface def</strong> for interface definition.</li></ol><h4>Auto-creating interface definition elements via the Extract Definition command</h4><p><span>To auto-create an interface definition element via the Extract Definition command</span></p><hr /><ul><li><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Extract Definition and Usage" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Extract Definition and Usage]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">page.</span></span></li></ul>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254423142 space=SYSML2P version=5 -->
## PAGE 00346: (2026x) Item

- page_id: `254423142`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254423142/2026x+Item
- version_number: 5
- version_date: `2025-12-03T08:55:26.644+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model composition > (2026x) Structure
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An item definition defines identifiable objects that may be acted on over time, such as an input, output, or a stored item, but which do not necessarily perform actions themselves (the latter would be a part element). An item usage is a usage of one or more item definitions. Item usages may be:

- Representations of inputs and outputs (e.g., water, fuel, electrical signals, data).
- Flowing through and/or stored by a system.
- Referencial, referencing discrete items that interact with or pass through the system.

##### Creating item usage and definition elements

###### Creating item usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor

To create item usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the keyword:
  1. *item def*for an item definition.
  2. *item* for an item usage.
2. Name the element.
3. (Optional) Specify the item's features in the element body.
4. Click the Synchronize button.

partdefFuelTank{ 
itemdefFuel;// item definition 
} 
itemdefPerson;// item definition

partdefVehicle{ 
refitemdriver:Person;// referential item 
partfuelTank:FuelTank{ 
itemfuel:Fuel;// continuous item usage stored by a system 
} 
}

###### Creating item usage and definition elements via the Create Element command

To create item usage and definition elements via the Create Element command

1. In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click **Create Element**.
2. In the Create Element dialog, select one of the following:
  1. item def to create an item definition element.
  2. item to create an item usage element.
3. Name the element.
4. (Optional) Specify the item's features.

###### Creating item usage and definition elements via the view palette

To create item usage and definition elements via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Items/Ports/Attributes group, click one of the following:
  1. The **item** def button to create an item definition element.
  2. The **item** button to create an item usage element. If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to either the **item**or the **item def**button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.
2. Click in the view pane where you want to create the item symbol.
3. (Optional) Specify the item's features.

###### Creating item usage elements via the symbol compartment in a view

For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Compartments' space=''] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Compartments' space=''] page.

To create item usage elements via the symbol compartment in a view

- Do one of the following:
  - Select the element symbol for which you want to create an item and click the Create Element button on it. In the dropdown menu, click item .
  - Select the element symbol for which you want to create an item and on the right side of the items compartment, click the Create item button. The item is created and displayed in the items compartment.

###### Creating item usage elements via the Specification panel

To create item usage elements via the Specification panel

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification panel' space=''] of the element for which you want to create an item.
2. Do one of the following:
  1. In the Properties section bar, click the **Create Element** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The shortcut menu appears. Click the **item** element in the shortcut menu.
  2. In the **items**compartment's section bar, click the **Create item**[IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The item is created in the **items** compartment.

###### Auto-creating item definition elements via the Extract Definition command

To auto-create item definition elements via the Extract Definition command

- See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Extract Definition and Usage' space='']page.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

following:<ul><li>Select the element symbol for which you want to create an item and click the<span> </span><strong>Create Element</strong><span> </span>button on it. In the dropdown menu, click <strong>item</strong>. </li><li>Select the element symbol for which you want to create an item and on the right side of the <strong>items</strong> compartment, click the<span> </span><strong>Create <span>item </span></strong>button. <br />The item is created and displayed in the <strong>items</strong> compartment.</li></ul></li></ul><h4 class="auto-cursor-target">Creating item usage elements via the Specification panel</h4><p>To create item usage elements via the Specification panel</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Specification panel"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Specification panel" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open the Specification panel]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of the element for which you want to create an item.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Do one of the following:</span><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the Properties section bar, click the <strong>Create Element</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_element_in_specification_pane_button.png" /></ac:image> button. The shortcut menu appears. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click the <strong>item</strong> element in the shortcut menu.<br /></span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the <strong>items </strong>compartment's section bar, click the <strong>Create item </strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_element_in_compartment_via_specification_pane_button.png" /></ac:image> button. <br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The item is created in the <strong>items</strong> compartment.</span></li></ol></li></ol><h4>Auto-creating item definition elements via the Extract Definition command</h4><p><span>To auto-create item definition elements via the Extract Definition command</span></p><hr /><ul><li><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Extract Definition and Usage" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Extract Definition and Usage]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">page.</span></span></li></ul>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254422782 space=SYSML2P version=2 -->
## PAGE 00347: (2026x) Key concepts

- page_id: `254422782`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254422782/2026x+Key+concepts
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-12-03T08:38:29.383+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Element management > (2026x) Working with elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This page provides descriptions of basic concepts related to the SysML v2 elements that are relevant to know before you begin modeling.

For a comprehensive list and examples of SysML v2 elements, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Model composition' space=''] section.

The general structure of the model is comprised of the global namespace, which encloses one or more namespaces that contain model elements optionally organized into packages.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Model organization.

##### Key concepts

Below is a list of some of the SysML v2 elements key concepts, along with expanded descriptions following.

- 
  - 
  - 
  - 
- 
  - 
  - 
  - 
    - 
    - 
    - 
    - 
  - 
    - Definition specialization :
    - Usage specializations: , , ,

##### Namespace

A namespace is a kind of element that can contain other elements and assign names to them.The contained elements are referred to as member elements, whichare related to a namespace via a membership relationship. It can specify both the name by which the member element is known within the containing namespace and whether the visibility of the element's membership extends beyond the namespace.

**Kinds of namespaces:**

- Global namespace
- Root namespaces
- Packages
- All definition and usage elements

###### Global namespace

A global namespace acts as a container for all root namespaces within a single project, including all SysML model libraries. There is no syntax for the global namespace, thus it is not represented in graphical or textual notation. In the modeling tool, the global namespace is represented by the top-level <project_name> package. Elements within any root namespace can refer to top-level elements of any other root namespace without qualification, as root namespaces themselves are not named. The global namespace essentially encapsulates all root namespaces and their elements, providing a mechanism for cross-reference among them.

###### Root namespace

A root namespace is defined as a namespace without an owner. It contains top-level elements, and every element that is not a root namespace must belong to an ownership tree of a top-level element in some root namespace. The declaration of a root namespace is implicit within SysML, and its content is represented by its top-level elements. Root namespaces are sorted by creation date.The root namespace is automatically named after the initial package created for it and cannot be renamed.For a procedure on root namespace creation, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing elements' space=''] page.

###### Package

A package is a specific type of namespace designed for organizing and grouping elements within a model. It serves as a container that can include both structural and behavioral elements, facilitating modular design and reuse. Packages can be used to structure the model in a hierarchical manner, enabling better organization and management of model elements. This approach allows the logical grouping of related elements, improving the clarity and maintainability of the system model.In the modeling tool, a package element can only be created for aroot namespace within the Containment tree or via Textual Editor. For procedures on package management, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Package' space=''] page.

##### Elements and relationships

The following concepts apply to all model elements.

###### Elements

- Every model element is assigned an element ID, which remains unchanged from the element's creation until its deletion, even if the element properties change.
- Every model element may have one or more alias IDs relative to one or more namespaces.

###### Relationships

- A relationship is a kind of element that relates two or more model elements.
- Relationships can be:
  - Binary - relating exactly two elements.
  - Non-binary - relating more than two elements.
  - Directed - a relationship that is directed from source to target.
  - Undirected - a relationship within which all elements are targets without any source elements.
- A relationship may also serve as the source or target of other relationships.
- There are several kinds of relationships:
  - Specializations : [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Subclassification' space=''] , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Feature typing' space=''] , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Subsetting' space=''] , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Reference subsetting' space=''] , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Redefinition' space='']
  - Connectors : [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Connection' space=''] , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Interface' space=''] , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Flow' space=''] , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Succession' space=''] , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Succession flow' space=''] , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Binding' space=''] , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Allocation' space=''] , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Derive requirement' space='']
  - Other relationships : [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Annotations' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Dependency' space=''] , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Feature membership' space='']

##### Definition and Usage

In SysML v2, definition and usage are the primary constructs facilitating reusability for different kinds of elements (requirements, use cases, interfaces, ports, attributes, etc.).

###### Definition

A Definition element classifies the element kind (requirement, use case, etc.).In the modeling tool's graphical notation, definitions are always represented as rectangles. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

In the modeling tool's Textual Editor, a definition is represented by the keyword def.

part defVehicle;

###### Usage

A Usage element is a usage of a definition element within a specific context.A usage must be defined/typed by at least one definition that corresponds in kind (requirement usage defined/typed by requirement definition).If the usage is undefined by the user, it is implicitly defined by the appropriate kind defined in the Systems Library.In the modeling tool's graphical notation, usages are always represented as rectangles with rounded corners. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

In the modeling tool's Textual Editor, usage has no keyword. It is represented simply by typing the element kind (e.g., requirement, use case, etc.).

partengine:Vehicle;

###### Features

A definition can own usage elements, which are referred to as its features.A usage can also own other usage elements as features.

###### Graphical compartment and body

An element may contain other elements, which can be represented in the:

- Graphical compartments (graphical notation). The graphical compartment(s) is displayed in the owning element's symbol. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- Body (textual notation). The body is bound by curly braces, e.g., {...}. requirement defMassLimitReq{ 
doc/* The actual mass shall be...*/ 
}

##### Specialization relationships

SysML v2 provides specialization relationships used to specialize definition and usage elements. Specialization lets modelers build specific elements by inheriting features from more general ones. They can then tailor or adjust these features to fit different situations.Below is a list of various types of specialization relationships:

**SubclassificationSubclassification**: used to specialize a definition element. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Subclassification' space=''] page to learn more.

**Feature TypingFeature typing**: specifies a definition for a usage. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Feature typing' space=''] page to learn more.

**SubsettingSubsetting**: used to specialize a usage element. Inherited features can be subsetted by multiple features. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Subsetting' space=''] page to learn more.

**Reference SubsettingReference subsetting**: is a kind of subsetting relationship. While a subsetting usage can have multiple subsetted usages, a reference subsetting usage can only have one specific subsetted usage. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Reference subsetting' space=''] page to learn more.

**RedefinitionRedefinition**: is a kind of subsetting used to replace an inherited feature by redefining. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Redefinition' space=''] page to learn more.

Specialization lets modelers build specific elements by inheriting features from more general ones. They can then tailor or adjust these features to fit different situations.<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> Below is a list of various types of specialization relationships:</span></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8dac97c5-5839-44e5-aa16-ea6f75e8bda4"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Subclassification</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Subclassification</strong>: used to specialize a definition element. See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Subclassification" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Subclassification]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page to learn more.</span></p><p><strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3aee5317-26b7-463e-bbf6-32b882f35933"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Feature Typing</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Feature typing</span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">: specifies a definition for a usage. See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Feature typing" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Feature typing]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page to learn more.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ac6a9bce-807a-40aa-a997-11a334c8dff3"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Subsetting</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Subsetting</strong>: used to specialize a usage element. Inherited features can be subsetted by multiple features. See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Subsetting" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Subsetting]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page to learn more.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5a6108bf-0e4f-46f0-8abc-8d6e11a48b5d"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Reference Subsetting</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Reference subsetting</strong>: is a kind of subsetting relationship. While a subsetting usage can have multiple subsetted usages, a reference subsetting usage can only have one specific subsetted usage. See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Reference subsetting" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Reference subsetting]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page to learn more.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e5a3ac4a-7ff2-47e4-a2f5-d011de35c866"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Redefinition</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Redefinition</strong>: is a kind of subsetting used to replace an inherited feature by redefining. See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Redefinition" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Redefinition]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page to learn more.</span></p><p><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254422603 space=SYSML2P version=2 -->
## PAGE 00348: (2026x) Libraries & samples

- page_id: `254422603`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254422603/2026x+Libraries+samples
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-10-08T14:30:57.377+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Project management > (2026x) Modeling tool environment
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Libraries

Each SysML v2 project is automatically loaded with used projects:

- Standard Libraries. It contains standard SysML v2 libraries.
- *3DS SysML Customization*. It contains concepts designed for view creation and symbol styles customization.

##### Samples

You can review the SysML v2 project samples provided with the installation, found at:

- <modeling tool installation directory>\samples

##### Opening sample projects

You can use existing sample projects to explore SysML v2 model examples.

To open a sample project

1. In the Main toolbar, select Help , in the dropdown menu, click Show Samples . A new tab opens displaying all available sample projects.
2. Click an appropriate sample project under the **SysML v2** section. Use the shortcut keys Ctrl+F to open the **Find** dialog. Enter the desired keyword to locate specific samples. The sample project is opened.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

It contains concepts designed for view creation and symbol styles customization.</span></li></ul><h3>Samples</h3><p>You can review the SysML v2 project samples provided with the installation, found at:</p><ul><li><em style="text-align: left;">&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\samples</em><em style="text-align: left;"><br /></em></li></ul><h3><span>Opening sample projects</span></h3><p>You can use existing sample projects to explore SysML v2 model examples.</p><p>To open a sample project</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="1351e0b8-914d-4dcb-879a-5d84299dbcfa">In the Main toolbar, select <strong>Help</strong>, in the dropdown menu, click <strong>Show Samples</strong>. A new tab opens displaying all available sample projects.</li><li data-uuid="f27f1183-ff4b-421e-80fc-177e78e1efc9"><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click an appropriate sample project under the <strong>SysML v2</strong> section.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fb73d0b3-6652-4614-8ccc-62616a1112e4"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Use the shortcut keys Ctrl+F to open the <strong>Find</strong> dialog. Enter the desired keyword to locate specific samples.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">The sample project is opened.</p></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=312247031 space=SYSML2P version=5 -->
## PAGE 00349: (2026x) Local repository information

- page_id: `312247031`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/312247031/2026x+Local+repository+information
- version_number: 5
- version_date: `2026-06-10T11:32:54.572+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Project management > (2026x) Working with projects > (2026x) Managing local projects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### EXPAND: Click here to expand the documentation for the 2026x version...

Click here to expand the documentation for the 2026x version...

Locally saved projects are stored in the local repository within the modeling tool’s configuration files. You can access this repository in your file system; however, the projects are saved in a format readable only by the modeling tool and should be opened only as described in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing projects in the local repository' space='']****procedure.

To access the local depository within the modeling tool’s configuration files in your file system

1. In the modeling tool's main menu, go to Help > About <modeling tool name> .
2. In the Environment tab, click the file path next to Configuration Files . The opened repository contains the localRepository with the local projects (e.g., ...\AppData\Local\.<modeling tool name>\2026x\localRepository ).

Some buttons in the Manage Projects dialog are either disabled or not recommended for usage (e.g., **Clone**, **Version Properties**, **Project History**, **Edit Branches**, **Set Password**, and **Update from Local Project**) as they will be removed in future releases.

#### NOTE: 2026x HotFix1 version

2026x HotFix1 version

This documentation page is for the 2026x HotFix1 version of the SysML v2 Plugin. If you are working with the 2026x version, expand the section above. However, we highly advise upgrading to the 2026x HotFix1 version.

##### Local repository information

Locally saved projects are stored in the local repository within the modeling tool’s configuration files. You can access this repository in your file system; however, the projects are saved in a format readable only by the modeling tool and should be opened only as described in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing projects in the local repository' space='']****procedure.

###### Accessing the configuration files

To access the local depository within the modeling tool’s configuration files in your file system

1. In the modeling tool's main menu, go to Help > About <modeling tool name> .
2. In the Environment tab, click the file path next to Configuration Files . The opened repository contains the localRepository with the local projects (e.g., ...\AppData\Local\.<modeling tool name>\<modeling tool version>\localRepository ).

###### Local repository instances

Generally, there is one local repository per modeling tool installation. However, running multiple instances of the same modeling tool at once creates additional local repository instances in your file system if it uses the same location for configuration files.

If the original location for the local repository is already used by a running instance of the modeling tool, an additional local repository instance is created in the configuration files for each additional running instance. The instances correspond to the number of applications you run at once and the order in which they are launched:

- The location of the original local repository instance is ***localRepository**, (* e.g., ...\AppData\Local\.<modeling tool name>\<modeling tool version>\localRepository).
- The second instance's location is ***instance_1**\localRepository*, ( e.g., ...\AppData\Local\.<modeling tool name>\<modeling tool version>\instance_1\localRepository).
- The third instance's location is ***instance_2**\localRepository*, ( e.g., ...\AppData\Local\.<modeling tool name>\<modeling tool version>\instance_2\localRepository), etc.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

In such a case, the following notification is displayed in the modeling tool.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Troubleshooting

###### Fixing broken projects

- Affected version : 2026x HotFix1. We highly advise upgrading to the 2026x HotFix2 version.
- Issues :
  - The project is saved with unresolved references. ReferenceNotResolvedException or EObjectNotFoundException errors are thrown.
  - The Textual Editor cannot be opened.

To resolve the issue

1. Download the[ATTACHMENT filename='FixEObjectNotFoundErrors-sysml_v2-2026xHF1.groovy'] file.
2. Open the modeling tool and in the main toolbar, click Tools > Macros > Organize Macros . The Organize Macros dialog opens.
3. In the dialog, click**Add**and specify:
  1. **Name:**A descriptive name for the script (e.g., "FixReferences").
  2. **Macro Language:**Select**Groovy**.
  3. **File:**Click the "..." button to browse and select the*.groovy*file.
  4. Click OK .
4. Once added, run the script:
  1. Open the problematic project.
  2. In the main toolbar, click Tools > Macros > select the <*.groovy script name*>.
  3. The **Notification Window** opens, informing you that the script has been executed.
5. Save the project, close, and reopen.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="48f33d29-d38b-4b35-8e1d-30d8e68b2a82"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Click here to expand the documentation for the 2026x version...</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Locally saved projects are stored in the local repository within the modeling tool’s configuration files. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254421983 space=SYSML2P version=3 -->
````
## PAGE 00350: (2026x) Main menu

- page_id: `254421983`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254421983/2026x+Main+menu
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-12-17T14:26:20.609+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Project management > (2026x) Modeling tool environment > (2026x) User interface
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The main menu located at the top of the modeling tool window contains the following options:

Currently, multiple commands displayed in the dropdown menus of the main menu options that are listed below are disabled.

| Menu option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| File | Contains basic project management-related commands, e.g., Local Repository, Open Project, Save Project, etc. |
| Edit | Use this menu when working with views in the view pane. The Edit menu contains the commands for selecting, cutting, copying, and pasting symbols. |
| View | Contains commands allowing you to zoom in or zoom out of a view and of your modeling tool. |
| Layout | Use this menu for managing the layout of the symbols on an active view. |
| Options | Use this menu to specify project options and environment options. |
| Tools | Use this menu to access the Patch tool. |
| Analyze | At the moment, the commands provided in this menu are not available in SysML v2. |
| Collaborate | Contains the commands for working with server projects. |
| Window | Allows you to manage the layout and display of various windows in your modeling tool. |
| Help | Among other commands, this menu allows you to open online documentation, manage resources, and access modeling tool-related information. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The <strong>Edit</strong> menu contains the commands for selecting, cutting, copying, and pasting symbols.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>View</strong></td><td>Contains commands allowing you to zoom in or zoom out of a <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">view</span> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Toolbars" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[customize the toolbars]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of your modeling tool.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Layout</strong></td><td>Use this menu for managing the layout of the symbols on an active view.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Options</strong></td><td>Use this menu to specify project options and environment options.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Tools</strong></td><td><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="92ec7cab-860a-4b55-a4af-9668ef5efe6f">Use this menu to access the Patch tool. </ac:inline-comment-marker></p></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Analyze</strong></td><td><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="e15ba95c-43fb-478c-bd3b-fd75caed7fe8">At the moment, the commands provided in this menu are not available in SysML v2. </ac:inline-comment-marker></p></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Collaborate</strong></td><td><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="9b26ee28-9a3e-4e91-a1e7-f31e97fa0f4e">Contains</ac:inline-comment-marker> the commands for working with server projects.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Window</strong></td><td>Allows you to manage the <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="cae614e9-a028-4893-bc5a-c8551f3630c9">layout</ac:inline-comment-marker> and display of various windows in your modeling tool.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Help</strong></td><td>Among other commands, this menu allows you to open online documentation, manage resources, and access modeling tool-related information.</td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254422223 space=SYSML2P version=10 -->
## PAGE 00351: (2026x) Managing elements

- page_id: `254422223`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254422223/2026x+Managing+elements
- version_number: 10
- version_date: `2025-12-03T08:39:01.983+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Element management > (2026x) Working with elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This page outlines the basic common procedures regarding element management.

#### NOTE: Useful information for element management

Useful information for element management

- For more information on working with the Textual Editor and textual notation, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Working with Textual Editor' space=''] section.
- For a comprehensive list of SysML v2 elements, their textual notation keywords, and relevant procedures, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Model composition' space=''] section.

##### Declaring a root namespace

A root namespace contains the model elements. It does not have textual representation. For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Key concepts' space=''] page.

To declare a root namespace

1. In the Containment tree, right-click the global namespace (by default, the global namespace name is the same as the project name).
2. In the shortcut menu, select Create Namespace .

##### Creating an element

The procedures below describe the basic options for creating the majority of the model elements. For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Key concepts' space=''] page.

###### Creating an element via the Textual Editor

To create an element via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, type the keyword for the element you want to create followed by its name. partflashlight;
2. Click the Synchronize button.

###### Creating an element via the Create Element command

To create an element via the Create Element command

1. In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click **Create Element**. The **Create Element**dialog is displayed.
2. In the Create Element dialog, select the element you want to create.
3. Type the name for the newly created element.

###### Creating an element via the view palette

To create an element via the view palette

1. In the View Palette, click on the element you want to create.
2. Hover the cursor over the V iew Pane and click on the location where you want the new element to be created.
3. Type the name for the newly created element.

###### Creating an element via the smart manipulator in a view

Creating an element via the smart manipulator is only available for a limited selection of elements (e.g., ports, connectors).

To create an element via the smart manipulator in a view

- In a view , select a symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the command for the needed element.

###### Creating an element via the symbol compartment in a view

To create an element via the symbol compartment in a view

- See the procedure [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Compartments' space=''] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Compartments' space=''] page.

###### Creating an element via the Specification panel

To create an element via the Specification panel

- Do one of the following:
  - See the procedure [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification panel' space=''] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification panel' space=''] page.
  - See the procedure [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification panel' space=''] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification panel' space=''] page.

###### Creating an element via the Drag and Drop handler In a view

To create an element via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

- See the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Drag and Drop handler' space='']page.

###### Auto-creating a definition element via the Extract Definition command

To auto-create a definition element via the Extract Definition command

- See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Extract Definition and Usage' space='']page.

###### Creating a relationship element via the Create Relationship command

To create a relationship element via the Create Relationship command

1. Right-click element(s) or element symbol(s) for which you want to create relationship(s).
2. In the shortcut menu, click Create Relationship , then in the submenu, click either Outgoing or Incoming , and then in the submenu, click the relationship you want to create. The Element Picker dialog opens.
3. In the Element Picker dialog, do one of the following:
  1. In a view , click the element symbol. Hold Shift to select multiple elements.
  2. Click the Add [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The element selection dialog opens. In the element selection dialog, select the element(s) and click OK .
  3. Drag and drop the needed element(s) from the Containment tree onto the Element Picker .
4. In the Element Picker dialog, click OK . The specified relationship is created between the selected elements.

##### Copying, pasting, and cutting an element

Elements cannot be copied and pasted between separate SysML v2 projects.

###### Copying an element via the Textual Editor

To copy an element via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, select the element you want to copy and do one of the following:
  1. Right-click the selection and in the shortcut menu click the Copy command.
  2. Press Ctrl+C on the keyboard.

###### Copying an element via the Containment tree

To copy an element via the Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, select an element you want to copy and do one of the following:
  1. Right-click the element and in the shortcut menu click the Copy command.
  2. In the main menu, click Edit > Copy .
  3. Press Ctrl+C on the keyboard.
  4. Press Ctrl , then drag and drop the selected element to the element where you want the selected one to be copied and pasted.

###### Copying an element symbol in a view

To copy an element symbol in a view

1. In a view , select an element symbol you want to copy and do one of the following:
  1. In the view toolbar, click the Copy [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button.
  2. In the main menu, click Edit > Copy .
  3. Press Ctrl+C on the keyboard.

###### Pasting an element via the Textual Editor

To paste an element via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to paste the copied notation and do one of the following:
  1. Right-click and in the shortcut menu click the Paste command.
  2. Press Ctrl+V on the keyboard.

###### Pasting an element via the Containment tree

To paste an element via the Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, select an element where you want to paste the copied element and do one of the following:
  1. Right-click the element and in the shortcut menu click the Paste command.
  2. In the main menu, click Edit > Paste .
  3. Press Ctrl+V on the keyboard.

###### Pasting an element or its symbol via a view

To paste an element or its symbol via a view

1. In a view , select an element symbol for which you want to paste the copied element symbol and do one of the following:
  1. In the view toolbar, click the Paste [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button to paste the copied symbol without creating a new element.
  2. In the main menu, click one of the following:
    1. Click Edit > Paste to paste the copied symbol without creating a new element.
    2. Click Edit > Paste with New Element to paste the copied symbol and create a new element for it.
  3. Press one of the following shortcut keys:
    1. Press Ctrl+V to to paste the copied symbol without creating a new element.
    2. Press Ctrl+E to paste the copied symbol and create a new element for it.

###### Cutting an element via the Textual Editor

To cut an element via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, select the element you want to cut and do one of the following:
  1. Right-click the selection and in the shortcut menu click the Cut command.
  2. Press Ctrl+X on the keyboard.

###### Cutting an element via the Containment tree

To cut an element via the Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, select an element you want to cut and do one of the following:
  1. Right-click the element and in the shortcut menu click the Cut command.
  2. In the main menu, click Edit > Cut .
  3. Press Ctrl+X on the keyboard.

##### Deleting an element

###### Deleting an element via the Textual Editor

To delete an element via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, select the element keyword and its notational text. partflashlight;
2. On the keyboard, press either one of the following keys:
  1. Delete key.
  2. Backspace key.
3. Click the Synchronize button.

###### Deleting an element via the Containment tree

To delete an element via the Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, select an element you want to delete and do one of the following:
  1. Right-click the element and in the shortcut menu click the Delete command.
  2. In the main menu, click Edit > Delete .
  3. Press Delete key or Ctrl+D on the keyboard.

###### Deleting an element or its symbol via a view

To delete an element or its symbol via a view

1. In a view , select an element symbol you want to delete and do one of the following:
  1. In the view toolbar, click one of the following:
    1. Delete button to delete the element from the model.
    2. Remove from Diagram button to remove the element symbol from the view .
  2. In the main menu, click one of the following:
    1. Click Edit > Delete to delete the element from the model.
    2. Click Edit > Remove from Diagram to remove the element symbol from the view .
  3. Press one of the following shortcut keys:
    1. Press Ctrl+D to delete the element from the model.
    2. Press Delete to remove the element symbol from the view .

###### Deleting an element via the compartment in the Specification panel

To delete an element via the compartment in the Specification panel

- See the procedure [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification panel' space=''] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification panel' space=''] page.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
