<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=278724936 space=SYSML2P version=21 -->
## PAGE 00352: (2026x) Managing local projects

- page_id: `278724936`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/278724936/2026x+Managing+local+projects
- version_number: 21
- version_date: `2026-06-10T11:31:45.041+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Project management > (2026x) Working with projects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### EXPAND: Click here to expand the documentation for the 2026x version...

Click here to expand the documentation for the 2026x version...

Local projects are managed via the local repository instead of working with individual files. The projects are stored in the local repository within the modeling tool’s configuration files. The local repository serves as a central storage for all your projects, their dependencies, and standard libraries. This makes it easier to keep track of your models, review your work, and smoothly transition between local and server projects. It also provides a more robust format for exchanging models in air-gapped environments between servers.

When you open a project from the file system, the tool first imports it into the local repository. After the import completes, the tool opens the project copy from the local repository, not the file in your file system. The tool handles sample projects in the same way. As a result, every project you create, open, or import is stored in the local repository, allowing you to view and modify it.

The projects can be saved in an**.mdszip format**, where a single file contains both the project and its used projects. This file is intended only for importing projects into your local repository or server. Once imported, all ongoing work is saved directly in the repository. You only need to export to a file again if you want to share your project or create a backup.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### The Manage Projects dialog

The Manage Project dialog allows you to manage projects in the local repository. It displays all the projects in the local repository and allows you to , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing projects in the local repository' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing projects in the local repository' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing projects in the local repository' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing projects in the local repository' space=''], and[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing project categories in the local repository' space=''] your projects.

- The Name column in the Manage Projects dialog displays projects and project categories.
- The Branches column displays the names of [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing project imports in the local repository' space=''] .
- The Last modified column displays the last project modification date and time.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Opening the Manage Projects dialog 2026xOpening the Manage Projects dialog

To open the Manage Projects dialog

- In the main menu, click File > Local Repository . The Manage Projects dialog opens.

#### NOTE: 2026x HotFix1 version

2026x HotFix1 version

This documentation page is for the 2026x HotFix1 version of the SysML v2 Plugin. If you are working with the 2026x version, expand the section above. However, we highly advise upgrading to the 2026x HotFix1 version.

Local projects are managed via the local repository instead of working with individual files. The projects are stored in the local repository within the modeling tool’s configuration files. The local repository serves as a central storage for all your projects, their dependencies, and standard libraries. This makes it easier to keep track of your models, review your work, and smoothly transition between local and server projects. It also provides a more robust format for exchanging models in air-gapped environments between servers.

When you open a project from the file system, the tool first imports it into the local repository. After the import completes, the tool opens the project copy from the local repository, not the file in your file system. The tool handles sample projects in the same way. As a result, every project you create, open, or import is stored in the local repository, allowing you to view and modify it.

The projects can be saved in an**.mdszip format**, where a single file contains both the project and its used projects. This file is intended only for importing projects into your local repository or server. Once imported, all ongoing work is saved directly in the repository. You only need to export to a file again if you want to share your project or create a backup.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### The Manage Projects dialog

The Manage Project dialog allows you to manage projects in the local repository. It displays all the projects in the local repository and allows you to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing projects in the local repository' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing projects in the local repository' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing projects in the local repository' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing projects in the local repository' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing projects in the local repository' space=''], and[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing project categories in the local repository' space=''] your projects.

- The Name column in the Manage Projects dialog displays projects and project categories. Additionally, projects are displayed with the information about their [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing project imports in the local repository' space=''] .
- The Last modified column displays the last project modification date and time.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Opening the Manage Projects dialog

To open the Manage Projects dialog

- In the main menu, click File > Local Repository . The Manage Projects dialog opens.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=278725578 space=SYSML2P version=4 -->
## PAGE 00353: (2026x) Managing local used projects

- page_id: `278725578`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/278725578/2026x+Managing+local+used+projects
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2026-06-10T11:52:20.096+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Project management > (2026x) Working with projects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### EXPAND: Click here to expand the documentation for the 2026x version...

Click here to expand the documentation for the 2026x version...

It is advisable to split large projects with loosely dependent parts into used projects. Project partitioning provides additional benefits, such as reusing model parts (e.g., libraries) across multiple projects and improving performance when working with very large models. The used projects are always added from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='']; thus, the projects must first be added there to be used in other projects. Once added to the main project, you can view the used projects under the Containment tree's Used Projects node.

You can manage used projects via the Containment tree, the **Project Usages**dialog, and the **Advanced Project Usages Configuration**dialog.

There can be two kinds of used projects:

- *Directly used projects*: projects used directly by the main project.
- *Indirectly used projects*: projects used by other used projects. For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Indirectly used projects' space=''] subsection.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### The Project Usages dialog 2026xThe Project Usages dialog

The Project Usages dialog allows you to manage projects used from the local repository. You can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Using projects from the local repository' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Updating used projects to their latest versions' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Opening used projects' space=''], a used project, and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Switching between the original used project and its subsequent imports' space='']. Selecting a used project in the project usages tree displays the project's details (e.g., version, location) on the right side of the dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Opening the Project Usages dialog 2026xOpening the Project Usages dialog

To open the Project Usages dialog

1. Do one of the following:
  1. In the main menu, select Options > Project Usages .
  2. In the main menu, select Collaborate > Project Usages > Project Usages .
  3. In the Containment tree, expand the Used Projects node and right-click a used project. In the shortcut menu, click Options . The Project Usages dialog opens.

##### The Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog 2026xThe Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog

The Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog allows you to manage projects used from the local repository. You can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Using projects from the local repository' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Removing used projects' space=''] a used project, and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Switching between the original used project and its subsequent imports' space=''] of a used project. You can also review all the used projects' version information.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Some buttons in the **Advanced Project Usages Configuration**dialog are either disabled or not recommended for usage (e.g., **Project Usage Information**, **Notify on New Version**column functionality) as their functionality will be changed and/or removed in future releases.

###### Opening the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog 2026xOpening the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog

To open the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog

1. Open the **Project Usages**dialog .
2. In the Project Usages dialog's toolbar, click the Usages Configuration [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog opens.

#### NOTE: 2026x HotFix1 version

2026x HotFix1 version

This documentation page is for the 2026x HotFix1 version of the SysML v2 Plugin. If you are working with the 2026x version, expand the section above. However, we highly advise upgrading to the 2026x HotFix1 version.

It is advisable to split large projects with loosely dependent parts into used projects. Project partitioning provides additional benefits, such as reusing model parts (e.g., libraries) across multiple projects and improving performance when working with very large models. The used projects are always added from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='']; thus, the projects must first be added there to be used in other projects. Once added to the main project, you can view the used projects under the Containment tree's Used Projects node.

You can manage used projects via the Containment tree, the **Project Usages**dialog, and the **Advanced Project Usages Configuration** dialog.

There can be two kinds of used projects:

- *Directly used projects*: projects used directly by the main project.
- *Indirectly used projects*: projects used by other used projects. For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Indirectly used projects' space=''] page.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### The Project Usages dialog

The Project Usages dialog allows you to manage projects used from the local repository. You can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Using projects from the local repository' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Updating used projects to their latest versions' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Opening used projects' space=''], a used project, and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Switching between the original used project and its subsequent imports' space='']. Selecting a used project in the project usages tree displays the project's details (e.g., version, location) on the right side of the dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Opening the Project Usages dialog

To open the Project Usages dialog

1. Do one of the following:
  1. In the main menu, select Options > Project Usages .
  2. In the main menu, select Collaborate > Project Usages > Project Usages .
  3. In the Containment tree, expand the Used Projects node and right-click a used project. In the shortcut menu, click Options . The Project Usages dialog opens.

##### The Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog

The Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog allows you to manage projects used from the local repository. You can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Using projects from the local repository' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Updating used projects to their latest versions' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Removing used projects' space=''] a used project, and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Switching between the original used project and its subsequent imports' space=''] of a used project. You can also review the information about the version and the imports of all used projects.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Opening the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog

To open the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog

1. Open the **Project Usages** dialog .
2. In the Project Usages dialog's toolbar, click the Usages Configuration [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog opens.

Selecting a used project in the project usages tree displays the project's details (e.g., version, location) on the right side of the dialog.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="The Project Usages dialog 2026x.png" /></ac:image></p><h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4a9e2cb2-b192-4f48-b246-4c799a85d61f"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Opening the Project Usages dialog 2026x</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Opening the Project Usages dialog</h4><p>To open the Project Usages dialog</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="8916dfc7-3e6c-47f0-8548-759b1fa5761a">Do one of the following:<ol><li>In the main menu, select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Project Usages</strong>.</li><li>In the main menu, select <strong>Collaborate</strong> &gt; <strong>Project Usages </strong>&gt; <strong>Project Usages</strong>.</li><li>In the Containment tree, expand the <strong>Used Projects </strong>node and right-click a used project. In the shortcut menu, click <strong>Options</strong>.<br />The <strong>Project Usages</strong> dialog opens.</li></ol></li></ol><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="78511087-c35a-4ff9-9c9e-0265172c99b0"><ac:parameter ac:name="">The Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog 2026x</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>The Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog</h3><p>The Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog allows you to manage projects used from the local repository. You can <ac:link ac:anchor="Using a project from the local repository via the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog 2026x"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Using projects from the local repository" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[use (and undo this command)]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link ac:anchor="Removing a used project via the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog 2026x"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Removing used projects" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[remove (and undo this command)]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> a used project, and <ac:link ac:anchor="Switching between the original used project and its subsequent imports"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Switching between the original used project and its subsequent imports" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[switch between the original used project and its imports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of a used project. You can also review all the used projects' version information.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog 2026x.png" /></ac:image></p><p style="text-align: center;"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f9c8e29d-9d4b-4d97-91cd-8bdbf90d935c"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Some buttons in the <strong>Advanced Project Usages Configuration </strong>dialog are either disabled or not recommended for usage (e.g., <strong>Project Usage Information</strong>, <strong>Notify on New Version </strong>column functionality</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">) as their functionality will be changed and/or removed in future releases.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="edb5aa93-7feb-4261-9a1c-0c6054d86f3e"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Opening the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog 2026x</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Opening the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog</h4><p>To open the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="b23aaf09-c2c5-4faf-9dbd-3bac15ccbb05"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Project Usages dialog 2026x"><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Project Usages </strong>dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>. </li><li data-uuid="48e1cea3-4353-445b-bb3e-97772da72148">In the <strong>Project Usages </strong>dialog's toolbar, click the <strong>Usages Configuration <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="usages_configurations_button.png" /></ac:image> </strong>button. <br />The <strong>Advanced Project Usages Configuration</strong> dialog opens.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0862e273-07e4-4631-b2c0-755e9d812bc0"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">2026x HotFix1 version</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>This documentation page is for the 2026x HotFix1 version of the SysML v2 Plugin. If you are working with the 2026x version, expand the section above. However, we highly advise upgrading to the 2026x HotFix1 version.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>It is advisable to split large projects with loosely dependent parts into used projects. Project partitioning provides additional benefits, such as reusing model parts (e.g., libraries) across multiple projects and improving performance when working with very large models. The used projects are always added from the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[local repository]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>; thus, the projects must first be added there to be used in other projects. Once added to the main project, you can view the used projects under the Containment tree's Used Projects node. </p><p>You can manage used projects via the Containment tree, <ac:link ac:anchor="The Project Usages dialog"><ac:link-body>the <strong>Project Usages </strong>dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>, and <ac:link ac:anchor="The Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog"><ac:link-body>the <strong>Advanced Project Usages Configuration</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</p><p>There can be two kinds of used projects:</p><ul><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><em>Directly used projects</em>: projects used directly by the main project.<br /></span></li><li data-uuid="8731e327-e94a-4ce8-a9bf-11d0b15b2241"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><em>Indirectly used projects</em>: projects used by other used projects. For more information, see the </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Indirectly used projects" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Indirectly used projects]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> page.</span></li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Used Projects node in the Containment tree.png" /></ac:image></p><h3>The Project Usages dialog</h3><p>The Project Usages dialog allows you to manage projects used from the local repository. You can <ac:link ac:anchor="Using a project from the local repository via the Project Usages dialog"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Using projects from the local repository" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[use]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link ac:anchor="Updating a used project to its latest version via the Project Usages dialog"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Updating used projects to their latest versions" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[update]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link ac:anchor="Opening a used project via the Project Usages dialog"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Opening used projects" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[open]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link ac:anchor="Removing a used project via the Project Usages dialog"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[remove]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> a used project, and <ac:link ac:anchor="Switching between the original used project and its subsequent imports via the Project Usages dialog"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Switching between the original used project and its subsequent imports" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[switch between the original used project and its subsequent imports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. Selecting a used project in the project usages tree displays the project's details (e.g., version, location) on the right side of the dialog.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="The Project Usages dialog.png" /></ac:image></p><h4>Opening the Project Usages dialog</h4><p>To open the Project Usages dialog</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="7e600da9-6479-4977-85a9-aa4216b9f102">Do one of the following:<ol><li>In the main menu, select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Project Usages</strong>.</li><li>In the main menu, select <strong>Collaborate</strong> &gt; <strong>Project Usages </strong>&gt; <strong>Project Usages</strong>.</li><li>In the Containment tree, expand the <strong>Used Projects </strong>node and right-click a used project. In the shortcut menu, click <strong>Options</strong>.<br />The <strong>Project Usages</strong> dialog opens.</li></ol></li></ol><h3>The Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog</h3><p>The Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog allows you to manage projects used from the local repository. You can <ac:link ac:anchor="Using a project from the local repository via the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Using projects from the local repository" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[use (and undo this command)]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link ac:anchor="Updating a used project to its latest version via the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Updating used projects to their latest versions" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[update]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link ac:anchor="Removing a used project via the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Removing used projects" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[remove (and undo this command)]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> a used project, and <ac:link ac:anchor="Switching between the original used project and its subsequent imports via the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Switching between the original used project and its subsequent imports" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[switch between the original used project and its imports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of a used project. You can also review the information about the version and the imports of all used projects.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="The Advanced Usages Configuration dialog.png" /></ac:image></p><h4>Opening the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog</h4><p>To open the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="653adb75-bc64-445c-87c4-0acfe4338636"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Project Usages dialog"><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Project Usages</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>. </li><li data-uuid="ad169f26-2f7e-4642-a7e5-6da6e9653ca1">In the <strong>Project Usages </strong>dialog's toolbar, click the <strong>Usages Configuration <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="usages_configurations_button.png" /></ac:image> </strong>button. <br />The <strong>Advanced Project Usages Configuration</strong> dialog opens.</li></ol>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=312247003 space=SYSML2P version=6 -->
## PAGE 00354: (2026x) Managing project categories in the local repository

- page_id: `312247003`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/312247003/2026x+Managing+project+categories+in+the+local+repository
- version_number: 6
- version_date: `2026-06-10T11:32:41.662+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Project management > (2026x) Working with projects > (2026x) Managing local projects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### EXPAND: Click here to expand the documentation for the 2026x version...

Click here to expand the documentation for the 2026x version...

Project categories in the local repository help you organize and structure your local projects, providing a more efficient project management experience. You can create, delete, rename, and move projects between them. Category management commands are available in the toolbar of the **Manage Projects** dialog and the shortcut menus of categories and projects.

The*Standard Libraries*category is pre-populated with the SysML v2 standard libraries required for modeling. It is created automatically when the SysML v2 Plugin is first launched and cannot be edited or deleted.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The project categories are highlighted in the image.

###### Creating new project categories in the local repository

To create new project categories in the local repository

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='']Open the **Manage Projects**dialog and do one of the following:
  1. In the dialog's toolbar, click the Create Category [ATTACHMENT filename='create category button.png'] button. The Create Category dialog opens.
  2. Right-click a category and in the shortcut menu, click the Create Category command. The Create Category dialog opens.
2. In the dialog, specify the parent category and the name for the new category, then click OK .

###### Deleting project categories in the local repository

To delete project categories in the local repository

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='']Open the **Manage Projects**dialog and do one of the following:
  1. Select the needed category and in the dialog's toolbar, click the Delete Category [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The Delete Category question dialog opens.
  2. Right-click a category and in the shortcut menu, click the Delete Category command. The Delete Category question dialog opens.
2. In the Delete Category dialog, click Yes to confirm the deletion.

###### Renaming project categories in the local repository

To rename project categories in the local repository

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='']Open the **Manage Projects**dialog and do one of the following:
  1. Select the needed category and in the dialog's toolbar, click the Rename Category [ATTACHMENT filename='rename category button.png'] button. The Rename Category dialog opens.
  2. Right-click a category and in the shortcut menu, click the Rename Category command. The Rename Category dialog opens.
2. In the Rename Category dialog, specify the new name and click OK .

###### Moving projects to other project categories in the local repository 2026xMoving projects to other project categories in the local repository

Moving the original project to another project category in the local repository moves all of its subsequent imports, and vice versa. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing project imports in the local repository' space=''] page.

To move projects to other project categories in the local repository

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='']Open the **Manage Projects**dialog and do one of the following:
  1. Select the needed project and in the dialog's toolbar, click the Move To Category [ATTACHMENT filename='move to category button.png'] button. The Move To Category dialog opens.
  2. Right-click the project and in the shortcut menu, click the Move To Category command. The Move To Category dialog opens.
2. In the Move To Category dialog, select the needed category and click OK .

###### Managing empty project categories in the local repository

To manage empty project categories in the local repository

1. Open the **Manage Projects**dialog .
2. In the dialog's toolbar, click the Show empty categories [ATTACHMENT filename='Show empty categories button.png'] button to enable or disable the display of empty categories.

#### NOTE: 2026x HotFix1 version

2026x HotFix1 version

This documentation page is for the 2026x HotFix1 version of the SysML v2 Plugin. If you are working with the 2026x version, expand the section above. However, we highly advise upgrading to the 2026x HotFix1 version.

Project categories in the local repository help you organize and structure your local projects, providing a more efficient project management experience. You can create, delete, rename, and move projects between them. Category management commands are available in the toolbar of the **Manage Projects** dialog and the shortcut menus of categories and projects.

The*Standard Libraries*category is pre-populated with the SysML v2 standard libraries required for modeling. It is created automatically when the SysML v2 Plugin is first launched and cannot be edited or deleted.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The project categories are highlighted in the image.

###### Creating new project categories in the local repository

To create new project categories in the local repository

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='']Open the **Manage Projects**dialog and do one of the following:
  1. In the dialog's toolbar, click the Create Category [ATTACHMENT filename='create category button.png'] button. The Create Category dialog opens.
  2. Right-click a category and in the shortcut menu, click the Create Category command. The Create Category dialog opens.
2. In the dialog, specify the parent category and the name for the new category, then click OK .

###### Deleting project categories in the local repository

To delete project categories in the local repository

1. Open the **Manage Projects**dialog and do one of the following:
  1. Select the needed category and in the dialog's toolbar, click the Delete Category [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The Delete Category question dialog opens.
  2. Right-click a category and in the shortcut menu, click the Delete Category command. The Delete Category question dialog opens.
2. In the Delete Category dialog, click Yes to confirm the deletion.

###### Renaming project categories in the local repository

To rename project categories in the local repository

1. Open the **Manage Projects**dialog and do one of the following:
  1. Select the needed category and in the dialog's toolbar, click the Rename Category [ATTACHMENT filename='rename category button.png'] button. The Rename Category dialog opens.
  2. Right-click a category and in the shortcut menu, click the Rename Category command. The Rename Category dialog opens.
2. In the Rename Category dialog, specify the new name and click OK .

###### Moving projects to other project categories in the local repository

Moving the original project to another project category in the local repository moves all of its subsequent imports, and vice versa. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing project imports in the local repository' space=''] page.

To move projects to other project categories in the local repository

1. Open the **Manage Projects**dialog and do one of the following:
  1. Select the needed project and in the dialog's toolbar, click the Move To Category [ATTACHMENT filename='move to category button.png'] button. The Move To Category dialog opens.
  2. Right-click the project and in the shortcut menu, click the Move To Category command. The Move To Category dialog opens.
2. In the Move To Category dialog, select the needed category and click OK .

###### Managing empty project categories in the local repository

To manage empty project categories in the local repository

1. Open the **Manage Projects**dialog .
2. In the dialog's toolbar, click the Show empty categories [ATTACHMENT filename='Show empty categories button.png'] button to enable or disable the display of empty categories.

You can create, delete, rename, and move projects between them. Category management commands are available in the toolbar of the <strong>Manage Projects</strong> dialog and the shortcut menus of categories and projects.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ae7eef1c-6c85-4c82-b820-b9265d06c858"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The<span> </span><em>Standard Libraries</em><span> </span>category is pre-populated with the SysML v2 standard libraries required for modeling. It is created automatically when the SysML v2 Plugin is first launched and cannot be edited or deleted.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Managing project categories in the local repository_2026xHF1.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The project categories are highlighted in the image. </h6><h4>Creating new project categories in the local repository</h4><p>To create new project categories in the local repository</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="61d8e72b-9d0d-4b21-aed1-e870466adef5"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Manage Projects dialog"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Manage Projects </strong>dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> and do one of the following:<ol><li data-uuid="60838c58-13cb-4525-9761-d64e0cec68f9">In the dialog's toolbar, click the <strong>Create Category</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create category button.png" /></ac:image> button. The <strong>Create </strong><strong>Category</strong><strong> </strong>dialog opens.</li><li data-uuid="6dc30037-0c50-4839-9317-799d2e78f2e5">Right-click a category and in the shortcut menu, click the <strong>Create Category</strong> command. The <strong>Create </strong><strong>Category</strong><strong> </strong>dialog opens.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="7e35f7fd-be72-4cc7-b89f-c00608959faa">In the dialog, specify the parent category and the name for the new category, then click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><h4>Deleting project categories in the local repository</h4><p>To delete project categories in the local repository</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="5a0a383a-0e1e-41cd-b9a2-7c406566b263"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Manage Projects dialog"><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Manage Projects </strong>dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> and do one of the following:<ol><li data-uuid="d2ec254e-df7e-49b9-b52b-5b4adf47ce72">Select the needed category and in the dialog's toolbar, click the <strong>Delete Category <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="delete category button.png" /></ac:image></strong> button. The <strong>Delete Category </strong>question dialog opens. </li><li data-uuid="08d8f53f-f461-4cd5-ac1e-5db06f089abf">Right-click a category and in the shortcut menu, click the <strong>Delete Category </strong>command. The <strong>Delete Category </strong>question dialog opens. </li></ol></li><li data-uuid="946d2d08-2b5a-4b2d-b750-73b6c12b81e3">In the <strong>Delete Category </strong>dialog, click <strong>Yes </strong>to confirm the deletion.</li></ol><h4>Renaming project categories in the local repository</h4><p>To rename project categories in the local repository</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="fe96a45c-728b-4af1-9e92-9a37c7b3ca8c"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Manage Projects dialog"><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Manage Projects </strong>dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> and do one of the following: <ol><li data-uuid="89d0df79-e1d5-4e87-8b4d-e534217baac3">Select the needed category and in the dialog's toolbar, click the <strong>Rename Category</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="rename category button.png" /></ac:image> button. The <strong>Rename </strong><strong>Category</strong><strong> </strong>dialog opens.</li><li data-uuid="36e28752-59cd-46ac-83c5-0d7c452b10bb">Right-click a category and in the shortcut menu, click the <strong>Rename Category</strong> command. The <strong>Rename </strong><strong>Category</strong><strong> </strong>dialog opens.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="cea5f60c-bad1-4d94-a458-1cc60261d57e">In the <strong>Rename </strong><strong>Category</strong><strong> </strong>dialog, specify the new name and click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><h4>Moving projects to other project categories in the local repository</h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2ddbc4c8-3b0c-4931-804e-bbbae91126b4"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Moving the original project to another project category in the local repository moves all of its subsequent imports, and vice versa. See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing project imports in the local repository" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Managing project imports in the local repository]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To move projects to other project categories in the local repository</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="7a065692-e865-404c-a1df-d32a7629d177"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Manage Projects dialog"><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Manage Projects </strong>dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> and do one of the following:<ol><li data-uuid="eca0be86-c920-4901-993c-f460d9853825">Select the needed project and in the dialog's toolbar, click the <strong>Move To Category</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="move to category button.png" /></ac:image> button. The <strong>Move To Category</strong><strong> </strong>dialog opens.</li><li data-uuid="bfedbfd9-e537-4483-9164-eeb13ff9b922">Right-click the project and in the shortcut menu, click the <strong>Move To Category</strong> command. The <strong>Move To Category</strong><strong> </strong>dialog opens.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="bae0f61d-5f77-43cd-acfe-860c675844b2">In the <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Move To Category </strong>dialog, select the needed category and click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><h4>Managing empty project categories in the local repository</h4><p>To manage empty project categories in the local repository</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="ed4fc3f2-d89a-4655-93c4-a9ec83852219"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Manage Projects dialog"><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Manage Projects </strong>dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li><li data-uuid="dd856de9-a5e3-4c55-a5ef-8976a0bb1230">In the dialog's toolbar, click the <strong>Show empty categories</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Show empty categories button.png" /></ac:image> button to enable or disable the display of empty categories. </li></ol>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=312246975 space=SYSML2P version=6 -->
## PAGE 00355: (2026x) Managing project imports in the local repository

- page_id: `312246975`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/312246975/2026x+Managing+project+imports+in+the+local+repository
- version_number: 6
- version_date: `2026-06-10T11:32:32.092+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Project management > (2026x) Working with projects > (2026x) Managing local projects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### EXPAND: Click here to expand the documentation for the 2026x version...

Click here to expand the documentation for the 2026x version...

Each originally created or imported project in the local repository may have multiple subsequent imports of the same project. For example, you might [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space=''] a project from the local repository, share it with a colleague who makes changes while you continue working on the original, and then later [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space=''] your colleague’s modified copy back into your local repository. In this case, the local repository will display both projects: the original and the subsequent import of the same project. You can modify both projects independently, and although they are displayed as separate projects, they are treated as distinct but still interdependent instances of the same project.

- The original project is the first instance of the project that exists in the local repository, which can be created in or imported into the local repository.
  - If the original project was created in the local repository, it is displayed as trunk .
  - If the original project was imported into the local repository, it is displayed as <imported_file_name> .
- Subsequent imports of the same project are any later instances of the project that are imported into the local repository.
  - Subsequent imports of the same project originate only by importing a project into the local repository. They are displayed as: <imported_file_name> .
    - If there already is an import with the same name, a number is added: <imported_file_name<number>> .

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing projects in the local repository' space=''] the original project in the local repository renames all of its subsequent imports, and vice versa.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing projects in the local repository' space=''] the original project from the local repository removes all subsequent imports of the same project, and vice versa.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing project categories in the local repository' space=''] the original project to another project category in the local repository moves all of its subsequent imports, and vice versa.

You can view the project details in the **Manage Projects** dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### NOTE: 2026x HotFix1 version

2026x HotFix1 version

This documentation page is for the 2026x HotFix1 version of the SysML v2 Plugin. If you are working with the 2026x version, expand the section above. However, we highly advise upgrading to the 2026x HotFix1 version.

Each originally created or imported project in the local repository may have multiple subsequent imports of the same project. For example, you might [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space=''] a project from the local repository, share it with a colleague who makes changes while you continue working on the original, and then later [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space=''] your colleague’s modified copy back into your local repository. In this case, the local repository will display both projects: the original and the subsequent import of the same project. You can modify both projects independently, and although they are displayed as separate projects, they are treated as distinct but still interdependent instances of the same project.

- The original project**** is the first instance of the project that exists in the local repository, which can be created in or imported into the local repository.
  - If the original project was created in the local repository, it is displayed only by indicating the project name.
  - If the original project was imported into the local repository, it is displayed using the following structure: [<file_name>.mdszip imported @<date and time of the import>] .
- Subsequent imports of the same project are any later instances of the project that are imported into the local repository.
  - Subsequent imports of the same project originate only by importing a project into the local repository. They are displayed using the following structure: [<file_name>.mdszip imported @<date and time of the import>] .

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing projects in the local repository' space=''] the original project in the local repository renames all of its subsequent imports, and vice versa.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space=''] the original project from the local repository removes all subsequent imports of the same project. Removing any subsequent import of the same project does not affect the original project.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing project categories in the local repository' space=''] the original project to another project category in the local repository moves all of its subsequent imports, and vice versa.

You can view the project details in the **Manage Projects** dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=312246947 space=SYSML2P version=5 -->
## PAGE 00356: (2026x) Managing projects in the local repository

- page_id: `312246947`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/312246947/2026x+Managing+projects+in+the+local+repository
- version_number: 5
- version_date: `2026-06-10T11:32:21.976+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Project management > (2026x) Working with projects > (2026x) Managing local projects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### EXPAND: Click here to expand the documentation for the 2026x version...

Click here to expand the documentation for the 2026x version...

###### Creating projects in the local repository 2026xCreating projects in the local repository

Create projects in the local repository to create your models.

To create projects in the local repository

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='']Open the **Manage Projects**dialog .
2. In the dialog, click the New button. The New Project dialog opens.
3. In the dialog, specify the project name and category, then click OK .

###### Opening projects from the local repository 2026xOpening projects from the local repository

Open projects from the local repository to view and modify them in the tool.

To open projects from the local repository

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='']Open the **Manage Projects**dialog .
2. In the dialog, do one of the following:
  1. Select the needed project and click the Open button.
  2. Double-click the needed project.

###### Importing projects to the local repository 2026xImporting projects to the local repository

- The project from the file system is first imported into the local repository, and then opened from there. Therefore, the project file you open and work on in the tool is the project copy in the local repository, not the file in your file system.
- Imported projects are displayed in the local repository using the following structure: <project_name> or <project_name<number>> .

To import projects to the local repository

Do one of the following:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='']Open the **Manage Projects**dialog . In the dialog, click the Import button. The Open dialog opens. Select the needed file from the file system and click OK .
- In the main menu, click File > Open Project . In the Open dialog, select the project and click Open .
- In the main toolbar, click the Open Project button.
- On the keyboard, press Ctrl+O .
- Drag the project from the open window of your file system to the modeling tool. The project opens.

###### Removing projects from the local repository 2026xRemoving projects from the local repository

Removing the original project from the local repository removes all subsequent imports of the same project, and vice versa. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing project imports in the local repository' space=''] page.

To remove projects from the local repository

1. Open the **Manage Projects**dialog .
2. In the dialog, select the needed project and click the Remove button. In the dropdown menu, select Delete Draft or Delete Project .
3. The question dialog opens. Click the Delete button to confirm the removal of the project.

###### Renaming projects in the local repository 2026xRenaming projects in the local repository

Renaming the original project in the local repository renames all of its subsequent imports, and vice versa. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing project imports in the local repository' space=''] page.

To rename projects in the local repository

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='']Open the **Manage Projects**dialog .
2. In the dialog, select the needed project and click the Rename button. The Rename dialog opens.
3. In the dialog, update the project name and click OK .

##### Saving local projects

There are three ways to save a local project:

- ****. Recommended for ongoing work. Uses less disk space than saving as a new version in the local repository.
- ****. Saves the project's new version in the local repository. This option is required if you want to export the project to a file or to create a reusable baseline for other projects. Note that it consumes more disk space than saving as a draft.
- ****. Exports the project as an *.mdszip* file, which can be stored anywhere on your file system and shared with colleagues.

Use **Change Sets** to track project modifications. The latest changes are compared against the most recent project version saved in the local repository.

- The panel legend explains the modification markings.
- Click the Refresh icon to update the display.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Saving projects as drafts in the local repository 2026xSaving projects as drafts in the local repository

To save projects as drafts in the local repository

1. Do one of the following:
  1. In the main menu, click File > Save Project .
  2. On the keyboard, press Ctrl+S.
  3. In the main toolbar, click the Save Project button. Projects saved as drafts are displayed with the asterisk symbol on the project icon in the local repository. [ATTACHMENT filename='save_as_draft.png']

###### Saving projects as new versions in the local repository 2026xSaving projects as new versions in the local repository

To save projects as new versions in the local repository

1. Do one of the following:
  1. In the main menu, click File > Save As New Version .
  2. On the keyboard, press Ctrl+K. The question dialog opens.
2. In the dialog, click the Save As New Version button to confirm. The Save As New Version dialog opens.
3. In the dialog, specify a comment (optional) and click Save .

###### Exporting projects from the local repository as .mdszip files 2026xExporting projects from the local repository as .*mdszip* files

To export projects from the local repository as .*mdszip* files

1. In the main menu, click File > Save Project As . To export a project to a file, its new version must first be saved in the local repository. If it is not, clicking the **Save As Project** command opens the **Save As New Version** dialog. Click **Save** in the dialog to store the new version in the local repository and enable the project export to a file.
2. The Save dialog opens. Select the file location in the file system and click Save .

#### NOTE: 2026x HotFix1 version

2026x HotFix1 version

This documentation page is for the 2026x HotFix1 version of the SysML v2 Plugin. If you are working with the 2026x version, expand the section above. However, we highly advise upgrading to the 2026x HotFix1 version.

See the procedures on this page for creating, opening, importing, removing, renaming, saving, and exporting local projects.

###### Creating projects in the local repository

Create projects in the local repository to create your models.

To create projects in the local repository

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='']Open the **Manage Projects**dialog .
2. In the dialog, click the New button. The New Project dialog opens.
3. In the dialog, specify the project name and category, then click OK .

###### Opening projects from the local repository

Open projects from the local repository to view and modify them in the tool.

To open projects from the local repository

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing local projects' space='']Open the **Manage Projects**dialog .
2. In the dialog, do one of the following:
  1. Select the needed project and click the Open button.
  2. Double-click the needed projects.

###### Importing projects to the local repository

- The project from the file system is first imported into the local repository, and then opened from there. Therefore, the project file you open and work on in the tool is the project copy in the local repository, not the file in your file system.
- Imported projects are displayed in the local repository using the following structure: [<file_name>.mdszip imported @<date and time of the import>] .

To import projects to the local repository

Do one of the following:

- Open the **Manage Projects**dialog . In the dialog, click the Import button. The Open dialog opens. Select the needed file from the file system and click OK .
- In the main menu, click File > Open Project . In the Open dialog, select the project and click Open .
- In the main toolbar, click the Open Project button.
- On the keyboard, press Ctrl+O .
- Drag the project from the open window of your file system to the modeling tool. The project opens.

###### Removing projects from the local repository

Removing the original project from the local repository removes all subsequent imports of the same project. Removing any subsequent import of the same project does not affect the original project. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing project imports in the local repository' space=''] page.

To remove projects from the local repository

1. Open the **Manage Projects**dialog .
2. In the dialog, select the needed project and click the Remove button. The question dialog opens. Click the Delete button to confirm the removal of the project.

###### Renaming projects in the local repository

Renaming the original project in the local repository renames all of its subsequent imports, and vice versa. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing project imports in the local repository' space=''] page.

To rename projects in the local repository

1. Open the **Manage Projects**dialog .
2. Select the needed project and click the Rename button. The Rename dialog opens.
3. In the dialog, update the project name and click OK .

###### Saving projects in the local repository

To save a local project in the local repository

Do one of the following:

1. In the main menu, click File > Save Project .
2. On the keyboard, press Ctrl+S.
3. In the main toolbar, click the Save Project button.

Use the **Change Sets** tab to track project modifications. The latest changes are compared against the latest project version saved in the local repository.

- The panel legend explains the modification markings.
- Click the Refresh icon to update the display.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Exporting projects from the local repository as .*mdszip* files

Export projects from the local repository as *.mdszip* files, which can be stored anywhere on your file system as a backup or shared with colleagues.

To export projects from the local repository as .*mdszip* files

1. In the main menu, click File > Save Project As . To export a project to a file, its changes must first be saved in the local repository. If it is not, clicking the **Save As Project** command opens a question dialog. Click **Save** in the dialog to save changes in the local repository and enable the project export to a file.
2. The Save dialog opens. Select the file location in the file system and click Save .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Therefore, the project file you open and work on in the tool is the project copy in the local repository, not the file in your file system. </li><li data-uuid="f2fb5d5d-d35a-4b8e-9e97-43f6ac56ef0e">Imported projects are displayed in the local repository using the following structure: <em>&lt;project_name&gt;</em> or <em>&lt;project_name&lt;number&gt;&gt;</em>.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To import projects to the local repository</p><hr /><p>Do one of the following:</p><ul><li data-uuid="bfeb4a13-dd9f-40b0-9f0d-87597cfa8a74"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Manage Projects dialog 2026x"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Manage Projects </strong>dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>. In the dialog, click the <strong>Import</strong> button. The <strong>Open </strong>dialog opens. Select the needed file from the file system and click <strong>OK</strong>.</li><li class="atl-forced-newline" data-uuid="3b16a029-693a-4dad-8b49-5a60c0b46b37">In the main menu, click <strong>File</strong> &gt; <strong>Open Project</strong>. In the <strong>Open</strong> dialog, select the project and click<strong> Open</strong>.</li><li class="atl-forced-newline" data-uuid="bb5b7573-5883-4890-a48b-a4cf5b6f5ab0">In the main toolbar, click the <strong>Open Project</strong> button.</li><li data-uuid="f7d34695-e3ce-4e8f-bb58-b5c79aae5b73">On the keyboard, press <strong>Ctrl+O</strong>.</li><li class="atl-forced-newline" data-uuid="7607d3c8-b1f6-4e35-89b5-636fad86a09c">Drag the project from the open window of your file system to the modeling tool. See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing project imports in the local repository" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Managing project imports in the local repository]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To remove projects from the local repository</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="c2c49ae1-b7ba-448f-9aad-437d2bb76f84"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Manage Projects dialog 2026x"><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Manage Projects </strong>dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li><li data-uuid="04dc3dbd-be31-4352-abc5-577c23b62ee1">In the dialog, select the needed project and click the <strong>Remove</strong> button. Click the <strong>Delete</strong> button to confirm the removal of the project.</li></ol><h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="14332723-17e5-45fb-b843-d868569e83a2"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Renaming projects in the local repository 2026x</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Renaming projects in the local repository</h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0b73cec7-d808-487d-b907-a8dd4a7138e9"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Renaming the original project in the local repository renames all of its subsequent imports, and vice versa. See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing project imports in the local repository" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Managing project imports in the local repository]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To rename projects in the local repository</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="e0201773-2bc2-4346-8610-e5bd9909e548"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Manage Projects dialog 2026x"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Manage Projects </strong>dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li><li data-uuid="47f30f31-5b13-4f28-ad36-4cad51f91b65">In the dialog, select the needed project and click the <strong>Rename</strong> button. The <strong>Rename </strong>dialog opens.</li><li data-uuid="0b0aa0db-4542-419a-a146-ff34a0ab7f83">In the dialog, update the project name and click <strong>OK</strong>. </li></ol><h3>Saving local projects</h3><p>There are three ways to save a local project:</p><ul><li data-uuid="0e423b42-c603-4a73-8f4c-8053fff63767"><p><strong><ac:link ac:anchor="Saving projects as drafts in the local repository 2026x"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[As a draft]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong>. Recommended for ongoing work. Uses less disk space than saving as a new version in the local repository.</p></li><li data-uuid="76429502-7f5e-4ecb-9fca-626d5efd809b"><p><strong><ac:link ac:anchor="Saving projects as new versions in the local repository 2026x"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[As a new version]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong>. Saves the project's new version in the local repository. This option is required if you want to export the project to a file or to create a reusable baseline for other projects. Note that it consumes more disk space than saving as a draft.</p></li><li data-uuid="8eed1e1e-f2ac-4c60-9361-5161ab8994eb"><p><strong><ac:link ac:anchor="Exporting projects from the local repository as .mdszip files 2026x"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[As an exported file]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong>. Exports the project as an <em>.mdszip</em> file, which can be stored anywhere on your file system and shared with colleagues.</p></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b1074d31-938f-43d9-82a5-4710cb6ca8d9"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Use <strong>Change Sets</strong> to track project modifications. The latest changes are compared against the most recent project version saved in the local repository.</p><ul><li data-uuid="49138934-55a9-4314-8a8b-00fec36631f8">The panel legend explains the modification markings.</li><li data-uuid="a5184059-9efb-4850-af29-1097c444137c">Click the <strong>Refresh</strong> icon to update the display.</li></ul><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Change Sets.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="830145cc-d6f5-43b7-9af5-d9f1f7c81d33"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Saving projects as drafts in the local repository 2026x</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Saving projects as drafts in the local repository</h4><p>To save projects as drafts in the local repository</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="d4c1bf44-a9dc-4e38-be4c-4f9c5e397e3d">Do one of the following:<ol><li>In the main menu, click <strong>File </strong>&gt; <strong>Save Project</strong>.</li><li>On the keyboard, press Ctrl+S. </li><li>In the main toolbar, click the <strong>Save Project </strong>button.<br />Projects saved as drafts are displayed with the asterisk symbol on the project icon in the local repository.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="save_as_draft.png" /></ac:image></li></ol></li></ol><h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dc961119-c0e0-4bb3-8423-8b834b989695"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Saving projects as new versions in the local repository 2026x</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Saving projects as new versions in the local repository</h4><p>To save projects as new versions in the local repository</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="c33e0819-0ccb-4ca4-9165-f11bc07c3a97">Do one of the following:<ol><li>In the main menu, click <strong>File </strong>&gt; <strong>Save As New Version</strong>.</li><li>On the keyboard, press Ctrl+K.<br />The question dialog opens. </li></ol></li><li data-uuid="6e753e82-e85b-4110-9bbd-7cd9d631c2d3">In the dialog, click the <strong>Save As New Version </strong>button to confirm. The <strong>Save As New Version </strong>dialog opens.</li><li data-uuid="1df4a740-e22b-45e0-894d-27ea546d92f3">In the dialog, specify a comment (optional) and click <strong>Save</strong>.</li></ol><h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c5a94d82-fd21-4745-8bdc-185628597fa3"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Exporting projects from the local repository as .mdszip files 2026x</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Exporting projects from the local repository as .<em>mdszip</em> files</h4><p>To export projects from the local repository as .<em>mdszip</em> files</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="d969a07c-cd8e-4b39-b14f-5cfce34d6b92">In the main menu, click <strong>File </strong>&gt; <strong>Save Project As</strong>. <ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3afcd461-f40b-4799-bb11-1109833648f2"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To export a project to a file, its new version must first be saved in the local repository. If it is not, clicking the <strong>Save As Project</strong> command opens the <strong>Save As New Version</strong> dialog. Click <strong>Save</strong> in the dialog to store the new version in the local repository and enable the project export to a file.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="f8bbca43-247c-42ca-ae27-ff7a494e45fa">The <strong>Save </strong>dialog opens. Select the file location in the file system and click <strong>Save</strong>.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="175ece1f-43af-418f-92b0-425f7ae343e3"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">2026x HotFix1 version</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>This documentation page is for the 2026x HotFix1 version of the SysML v2 Plugin. If you are working with the 2026x version, expand the section above. However, we highly advise upgrading to the 2026x HotFix1 version.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p style="text-align: left;">See the procedures on this page for creating, opening, importing, removing, renaming, saving, and exporting local projects.</p><h4>Creating projects in the local repository</h4><p>Create projects in the local repository to create your models.</p><p>To create projects in the local repository</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="ee4dc1bd-426f-478b-a2ba-880dc831475d"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Manage Projects dialog"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Manage Projects </strong>dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li><li data-uuid="49fdd9ba-f50c-4182-95ad-04282f6175c1">In the dialog, click the <strong>New</strong> button. The <strong>New Project </strong>dialog opens.</li><li data-uuid="f1bab07a-4c2f-4bb7-861d-bc0dbe7a0881">In the dialog, specify the project name and category, then click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><h4>Opening projects from the local repository</h4><p>Open projects from the local repository to view and modify them in the tool.</p><p>To open projects from the local repository</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="371a2a42-1e22-4355-b439-c3e671ce13b3"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Manage Projects dialog"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing local projects" /><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Manage Projects </strong>dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li><li data-uuid="760f0de5-324f-4d64-bcdf-6be8e191cfe2">In the dialog, do one of the following:<ol><li>Select the needed project and click the <strong>Open</strong> button.</li><li>Double-click the needed projects.</li></ol></li></ol><h4>Importing projects to the local repository</h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2f3f80e0-1412-46b6-a3c6-8951f34232ad"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li style="text-align: left;">The project from the file system is first imported into the local repository, and then opened from there. Therefore, the project file you open and work on in the tool is the project copy in the local repository, not the file in your file system. </li><li style="text-align: left;" data-uuid="77541ec8-49af-4236-935e-6e052dc0f73a">Imported projects are displayed in the local repository using the following structure: <em>[&lt;file_name&gt;.mdszip imported @&lt;date and time of the import&gt;]</em>.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To import projects to the local repository</p><hr /><p>Do one of the following:</p><ul><li data-uuid="19e3a14e-bd62-4d21-a323-bc5e363548f1"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Manage Projects dialog"><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Manage Projects </strong>dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>. In the dialog, click the <strong>Import</strong> button. The <strong>Open </strong>dialog opens. Select the needed file from the file system and click <strong>OK</strong>.</li><li class="atl-forced-newline" data-uuid="fa68502d-35cd-4425-b6eb-58af1edb37de">In the main menu, click <strong>File</strong> &gt; <strong>Open Project</strong>. In the <strong>Open</strong> dialog, select the project and click<strong> Open</strong>.</li><li class="atl-forced-newline" data-uuid="c815147d-bdf5-4ffb-8442-65f72ea16198">In the main toolbar, click the <strong>Open Project</strong> button.</li><li class="atl-forced-newline" data-uuid="2c085453-ce82-47b6-8a1c-e605bb68abe5">On the keyboard, press <strong>Ctrl+O</strong>.</li><li class="atl-forced-newline" data-uuid="43ef0d7e-c5ca-4213-bb82-09c2fcea84d3">Drag the project from the open window of your file system to the modeling tool. The project opens.</li></ul><h4>Removing projects from the local repository</h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f17e9a71-ebd1-4500-baa1-73339606668e"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Removing the original project from the local repository removes all subsequent imports of the same project. Removing any subsequent import of the same project does not affect the original project. See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing project imports in the local repository" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Managing project imports in the local repository]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To remove projects from the local repository</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="00b29092-b408-4bee-854d-c9344143b8b4"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Manage Projects dialog"><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Manage Projects </strong>dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li><li data-uuid="57d7b69c-0081-4134-aefb-3ee85f1ea08b">In the dialog, select the needed project and click the <strong>Remove</strong> button. The question dialog opens. Click the <strong>Delete</strong> button to confirm the removal of the project.</li></ol><h4>Renaming projects in the local repository</h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="559532c8-f093-43f0-8ce2-44ee6f67431d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Renaming the original project in the local repository renames all of its subsequent imports, and vice versa. See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Managing project imports in the local repository" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Managing project imports in the local repository]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="1da4df71-dafa-4d67-816c-e5751622bae1">rename</ac:inline-comment-marker> projects in the local repository</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="91711a88-b145-4af2-a80d-06c7e431dc22"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Manage Projects dialog"><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Manage Projects </strong>dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li><li data-uuid="396c4e95-1bde-405f-9c9b-dc2cffd6e82e">Select the needed project and click the <strong>Rename</strong> button. If it is not, clicking the <strong>Save As Project</strong> command opens a question dialog. Click <strong>Save</strong> in the dialog to save changes in the local repository and enable the project export to a file.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="7b98258a-32b0-4d80-8c0e-568e2ec647e6">The <strong>Save </strong>dialog opens. Select the file location in the file system and click <strong>Save</strong>.</li></ol>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254422700 space=SYSML2P version=6 -->
## PAGE 00357: (2026x) Managing Textual Editor

- page_id: `254422700`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/254422700/2026x+Managing+Textual+Editor
- version_number: 6
- version_date: `2025-12-11T09:13:45.523+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > (2026x) Model management > (2026x) Working with Textual Editor
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This page outlines the basic procedures for working with the Textual Editor and the textual notation.

For SysML v2 Textual Editor-related environment options, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Environment options' space=''] page.

##### Managing the Textual Editor

###### Opening the Textual Editor

To open the Textual Editor

- In the Containment tree, right-click an element. In the shortcut menu, under Textual Editor , click Open .
- On a view , right-click an element symbol. In the shortcut menu, under Textual Editor , click Open .
- In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Tabular views' space=''] , click the **Open in Textual Editor** button.

###### Saving changes in the Textual Editor

To save changes in the Textual Editor

Do one of the following:

- In the Textual Editor's toolbar, click the Synchronize button [ATTACHMENT filename='sync_button.png'] .
- Use the Alt+S shortcut keys. You must synchronize the textual notation before saving your project. Otherwise, the project will be saved without the changes made via the Textual Editor. If the **Synchronize model with errors** [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Environment options' space=''] is set to:*true*(default), an informational message is displayed informing you the notation contains errors. The notation cannot be synchronized. Fix the errors and synchronize the changes again.*false*, the notation is synchronized as it is. This may lead to an incorrect model.

###### Updating changes in the Textual EditorUpdating changes in the Textual Editor

Making changes both in the textual notation and elsewhere in the model with the editor tab still open results in concurrent editing, which may lead to data loss. If you do make changes in the model while the Textual Editor is open, an informational message is displayed requesting you to refresh the textual notation displayed in the Textual Editor.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

In this case, there are two options:

- Click the **Refresh** button in the message. This updates the textual notation with the changes made elsewhere in the model, removing any changes made in the Textual Editor that were not previously saved.
- Click the **Synchronize** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button in the editor toolbar. This updates the model with changes made in the textual notation, removing any changes made elsewhere in the model after making modifications in the Textual Editor. You can detect previously unsaved changes made in the Textual Editor that will be lost after clicking the **Refresh**button by looking for elements that do not have display IDs in the textual notation.[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Commenting in the Textual Editor

- There can be two types of comments:
  - A line note.
    - This type of comment is for the Textual Editor only; it does not create a comment element in your model.
    - Two ways to write it:
      - Write it using double forward slashes followed by the text you want to leave in the textual notation.
      - Place the cursor on the line you want to comment out and press Ctrl+/ on the keyboard.
  - A comment element.
    - This type of comment creates a comment element in your model.
    - For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Annotations' space=''] page.
- By default, comments are indicated in #248000 color, differentiating them from other notational text. // May require revision - a comment that exists only in the Textual Editor 
// the following two options are equivalent; both create a *comment*element with the 'May require revision' text: 
/* May require revision */ 
comment/* May require revision */

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

